This document describes how to maintain the NetEco server.

Symbol Conventions
The symbols that may be found in this document are defined as follows.
Symbol Description
Indicates a hazard with a high level of risk which, if not
avoided, will result in death or serious injury.
Indicates a hazard with a medium level of risk which, if not
avoided, could result in death or serious injury.
Indicates a hazard with a low level of risk which, if not
avoided, could result in minor or moderate injury.

Symbol Description
Indicates a potentially hazardous situation which, if not
avoided, could result in equipment damage, data loss,
performance deterioration, or unanticipated results.
NOTICE is used to address practices not related to personal
injury.
Supplements the important information in the main text.
NOTE is used to address information not related to personal
injury, equipment damage, and environment deterioration.

1
Getting Started
After NetEco is installed, you can maintain it through the PowerEcho and
implement service functions through the NetEco.
1.1 Logging In to the NetEco
This section describes how to log in to NetEco using a browser.
Prerequisites
● The network connection between your PC and the client IP address of the
NetEco is normal and the services of the NetEco are running properly.
● You have obtained the username and password for login.
Context
● The OS and browser of your PC must meet the following requirements.
Table 1-1 Configuration requirements
Software Type Requirements
OS Windows 10 Professional 64bit is recommended
Browser Latest Chrome(Stable Channel) and Firefox (ESR
Release) are recommended
Resolution Optimal resolution: 1920 x 1080 (px)
● NetEco provides the default user admin as the system administrator that has
permissions on all resources. The initial password of the user is
Changeme_123. To ensure the security of NetEco, change the password
periodically and keep the new password secure. If the admin user enters
incorrect passwords for five consecutive times within 10 minutes, the login IP
address will be locked for 10 minutes.
● If three accounts using an IP address are locked within 10 minutes, this IP

on the Account Policy page, this IP address will be locked.
● If the login of a local account meets the account lockout conditions specified
on the Account Policy page, this account will be locked for 30 minutes by
default.
● All users can log in to the system again after their accounts are automatically
unlocked. Local users can also contact security administrators to unlock their
accounts for login again.
Procedure
Step 1 Open a browser, enter https://NetEco client IP address: 31943 in the address box,
and press Enter.
If the browser displays a message indicating that the connection is insecure, set
the browser parameters. For details, see 12.2 How Do I Handle the Problem of
Certificate Error or Security Alarm Displayed in the Web Browser.
NO TE
Client IP address: indicates the server IP address in the single-node system scenario and
floating IP address in the cluster scenario.
Step 2 On the login page, enter the username and password, and click Log In.
NO TE
● If you need to change the password after login, keep the new password secure. If you
forget the password of the admin user, you can restore the initial password only by
reinstalling the NetEco.
● For security purposes, do not set the browser to remember the password.
Step 3 Optional: If Two-factor authentication is enabled, you also need to obtain a
verification code. Enter the obtained verification code and click Log In.
NO TE
● Click Switch Account to return to the login page and use another account to log in.
● After two-factor authentication is enabled, if you do not use the verification code for
five consecutive times, the function of obtaining verification codes will be locked. In this
case, log in again as prompted or disable two-factor authentication by referring to 7.3.2
Disabling Two-Factor Authentication for a User. If the verification code fails to be
sent, contact the system administrator to go to the System > System Settings >
Notifications page to check whether the SMS server or email server is correctly
configured.
----End
1.2 Logging In to the PowerEcho
This section describes how to use a browser to log in to the PowerEcho.
Prerequisites
● The network connection between your PC and the client IP address of the

Context
● The operating system (OS) and browser of your PC must meet the
requirements listed in Table 1-2.
Table 1-2 Configuration requirements
Software Type Requirements
OS Recommended: Windows 10 Professional 64-bit
Browser Recommended: the latest version of Chrome (Stable
Channel) or Firefox (ESR Release)
Resolution Optimal resolution: 1920 x 1080 pixels
● The PowerEcho provides the default user admin. The initial password for the
user is Changeme_123. For security purposes of the PowerEcho, change the
password periodically and keep the new password secure.
● If you enter the password for the admin user incorrectly for five consecutive
times within 10 minutes, your IP address will be locked for 10 minutes.
● The management node in the document is the node where the PowerEcho is
installed, and the product node is the node where the NetEco is installed.
Procedure
Step 1 Open a browser, enter https://:31945 in the address box, and press Enter.
If the browser displays a message indicating that the connection is insecure, set
the browser parameters. For details, see 12.2 How Do I Handle the Problem of
Certificate Error or Security Alarm Displayed in the Web Browser.
Step 2 On the login page, enter the username and password, and click Log In.
NO TE
● When you log in to the system in local mode, enter the username admin and its
password. The initial password for the admin user is Changeme_123. For security
purposes, change the password periodically and keep the new password secure.
● When you log in to the PowerEcho for the first time, you are prompted to change the
initial password for the admin user. Perform operations as prompted. If the password
for the admin user is lost, you can restore the password only by reinstalling the NetEco.
● For security purposes, do not save your password in the browser.
● By default, if you do not perform any operation within 30 minutes after a successful
login, you will be automatically logged out.
----End

2
Power On and Power Off the System
This section describes the procedures for powering on and off the system.
2.1 Powering On the System
This section describes how to power on the hardware components when installing
the NetEco system. The NetEco system consists of multiple hardware components.
You need to power on them in sequence.
Procedure
Step 1 Prepare for powering on the server.
1. Ensure that the power switches of all the devices in the cabinet are set to OFF.
If a power switch is ON, set it to OFF.
2. Ensure that the hardware devices are correctly placed and cable connections
(especially the power cable connections) are correct and meet ESD
specifications.
Step 2 Turn on the power switch of the server to power on it.
NO TE
After the server is started properly, the button/indicator of its power switch is displayed green.
----End
2.2 Powering Off the System
This section describes how to power off the NetEco system safely. To ensure data
security, do not press and hold the power button on the front panel of the server
to forcibly power off the server. If the cluster system is used, perform the
operations in this section on each NetEco server.
Prerequisites
The PowerEcho and NetEco services have been stopped. For details, see 4.2

Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the NetEco server as the sopuser user in SSH mode. For
details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the root user:
$ su - root
Password: root password
Step 3 Run the following command to stop the NetEco server:
# sync;sync;sync;sync;sync;sync
# shutdown -h now
Step 4 When the power indicator turns steady yellow, remove the power cable from the
server.
----End

3.5 Starting the PowerEcho Databases
This section describes how to start the databases of the PowerEcho by running
commands.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossadm users of the
management node.
● You have obtained the IP addresses of all the management nodes from the
planning data. If the IP addresses of some management nodes have changed,
obtain the IP addresses after the change.
Procedure
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the management node as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
For details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.
NO TE
If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, open a new PuTTY window to start the
databases on Management1 within three minutes after you run the startup command on
Management0. After you have started databases on all the nodes, check the startup result
of each node. If the startup fails on a node, contact technical support.
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
Step 3 Run the following commands to start the PowerEcho databases:
> source /opt/oss/manager/bin/engr_profile.sh
> ipmc_adm -cmd startdc -tenant manager
If information similar to the following is displayed and success is displayed for all
processes, the databases of the PowerEcho are started successfully. Otherwise,
contact technical support.
============================ Starting data container processes...
Starting redis process woadapterrdb-1-14 ... success
...
Starting redis process serviceinspectionrdb-1-3 ... success
Starting redis process privilegerdb-1-28 ... success
============================ Starting data container processes is complete.
----End
3.6 Starting the PowerEcho Service

● The database instances of the PowerEcho are in the Running state. For
details, see 3.5 Starting the PowerEcho Databases.
● You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossadm users of the
management node.
● You have obtained the IP addresses of all the management nodes from the
planning data. If the IP addresses of some management nodes have changed,
obtain the IP addresses after the change.
Procedure
If the PowerEcho is deployed in single-server mode:
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the management node as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
For details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
Step 3 Run the following command to import environment variables:
> source /opt/oss/manager/bin/engr_profile.sh
Step 4 Run the following command to start HyperHAAgent:
> ipmc_adm -cmd startapp -tenant manager -app HyperHAAgent
If information similar to the following is displayed and success is displayed for all
processes, HyperHAAgent is started successfully. Otherwise, contact technical
support.
Starting process hyperhaagent-7-0 ... success
Step 5 Run the following command to start all services:
> ipmc_adm -cmd startapp -tenant manager
If information similar to the following is displayed and success is displayed for all
processes, all services on the node are started successfully. Otherwise, contact
technical support.
...
Starting process user-0-0 ... success
Starting process cron-0-0 ... success
...
----End
If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode:
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to Management0 as the sopuser user in SSH mode. For
details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm

> source /opt/oss/manager/bin/engr_profile.sh
> ipmc_adm -cmd startapp -tenant manager -app HyperHAAgent
If information similar to the following is displayed and success is displayed for all
processes, HyperHAAgent is started successfully. Otherwise, contact technical
support.
Starting process hyperhaagent-7-0 ... success
Step 4 Run the following command to start services other than HyperHAAgent on the
node:
> ipmc_adm -cmd startapp -tenant manager
If information similar to the following is displayed and success is displayed for all
processes, all services on the node are started successfully. Otherwise, contact
technical support.
...
Starting process user-0-0 ... success
Starting process cron-0-0 ... success
...
Step 5 Use PuTTY to log in to Management1 and Management2 in sequence, as the
sopuser user in SSH mode, and repeat Step 2 to Step 4.
----End

Status of the nodes are both Not Running in the Node List area.

4.5 Stopping the PowerEcho Service
This section describes how to stop the PowerEcho service by running commands.
Prerequisites
You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossadm users of the
management node.
Procedure
If the PowerEcho is deployed in single-server mode:
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the management node as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
For details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
Step 3 Run the following command to import environment variables:
> source /opt/oss/manager/bin/engr_profile.sh
Step 4 Run the following command to stop all services:
> ipmc_adm -cmd stopapp -tenant manager
If information similar to the following is displayed and success is displayed for all
processes, all services on the node are stopped successfully. Otherwise, contact
technical support.
...
Stopping process user-0-0 ... success
Stopping process cron-0-0 ... success
...
----End

Otherwise, contact technical support.

NO TE

process instance resides, see 12.21 How Do I Check the Active/Standy Status of a Node?.

5
Operation and Maintenance
Management
5.1 Panoramic Monitoring
After a product is installed on the PowerEcho, you can use this function to monitor
resources such as nodes, services, databases, and processes on the PowerEcho and
the NetEco. This helps you detect and resolve exceptions in a timely manner,
ensuring efficient server running and normal product running.
5.1.1 Monitoring Products
You can monitor the indicators of product nodes and services in a centralized
manner. By predicting and analyzing the indicators of different resources, you can
identify faults in a timely manner.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management > Panoramic
Monitoring from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Node Monitoring.
Step 3 In the upper left corner of the Node Monitoring page, select the product or
PowerEcho.
Step 4 In the upper right corner of the page, check whether any resource is abnormal.
NO TE
The number in red indicates the number of abnormal resources.
● If the number of abnormal resources is 0, all resources of the product are

the product. You can click the number to view details on the corresponding
tab page.
Some agent processes of the PowerEcho service are deployed on a product
node. After the product node is powered off, these agent processes are in the
Unknown state. As a result, the PowerEcho service is also in the Unknown
state, and the number of abnormal services is displayed in the Services area
of PowerEcho in the upper right corner of the page.
----End
5.1.2 Monitoring Nodes
You can monitor all nodes of the PowerEcho and the NetEco to identify and rectify
faults in a timely manner, which ensures that the node servers are running
efficiently.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Context
The PowerEcho periodically monitors resources of the system. Table 5-1 lists the
monitoring intervals. The status of monitored objects on the page is updated every
30 seconds.
Table 5-1 Node monitoring intervals
Monitoring Object Interval (Second)
Node status 30
Node resource (for example, CPU 30
usage)
Service status 30
Database status 30
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management > Panoramic
Monitoring from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Node Monitoring.
Step 3 In the upper left corner of the Node Monitoring page, select the product or
PowerEcho.
Step 4 In the Node List area, view the IP address (management IP address), CPU usage,
physical memory usage, and virtual memory usage of the node. Check the node

packet loss of each process on the node. For details about the file system status,
see Table 5-3.

5.1.3 Monitoring Services
You can monitor all services of the PowerEcho and the NetEco to identify and
rectify faults in a timely manner, which ensures that the services are running
properly.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Context
The PowerEcho monitors services of the system every 30 seconds. The status
displayed on the page is updated every 60 seconds.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance

Step 3 In the upper left corner of the Service Monitoring page, select the product or
PowerEcho.
Step 4 Click the Services tab and refer to Table 5-4 to check the service status.
Alternatively, click a desired instance name and check the status of resources such
as relational databases, Redis databases, and service processes on the service
details page.

5.1.4 Monitoring Databases
You can monitor the relational databases and Redis databases of the PowerEcho
and the NetEco to identify and rectify faults in a timely manner, which ensures
that the databases are running properly.

You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Context
● The PowerEcho monitors databases of the system every 60 seconds. The
status displayed on the page is refreshed every 60 seconds.
● Relational database: A GaussDB database created on the basis of a relational
model for storing persistent data.
● Redis database: A high-performance key-value database that stores cached
status-related data.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management > Panoramic
Monitoring from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Middleware Monitoring.
Step 3 In the upper left corner of the Middleware Monitoring page, select the product
or PowerEcho.
Step 4 Click the Relational Databases or Redis Databases tab. For details about each
tab page, see Table 5-5.

5.1.5 Monitoring Processes
You can monitor the processes of the PowerEcho and the NetEco to identify and
rectify faults in a timely manner, which ensures that the processes are running
properly.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Context
The PowerEcho monitors processes of the system every 60 seconds. The status
displayed on the page is refreshed every 30 seconds.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management > Panoramic
Monitoring from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Service Monitoring.
Step 3 In the upper left corner of the Service Monitoring page, select the product or
PowerEcho.
Step 4 Click the Processes tab and view the CPU usage, virtual memory usage, and
physical memory usage of the desired process in the process list. Table 5-9
describes the process status.
Table 5-9 Process status
Status Description Measure

----End
5.1.6 Monitoring Resources
NetEco resources, such as hardware, environments, and storage media, are
monitored in a centralized manner, helping you learn about the information about
different resource objects and identify risks during resource running.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management > Panoramic

Step 3 On the Resource Dashboard page, select the resource type and view the resource
data.
Click a resource name. On the Multi-Dimensional Data Association View panel,
view the resource information such as exceptions, key indicators, and associated
objects.
----End
5.1.7 Viewing Historical Indicator Information
You can add indicators to be concerned to the same view based on service
requirements. This helps you view these indicators and identify and handle service
risks in a timely manner. By default, the system provides indicator views for nodes,
services, and middleware.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management > Panoramic
Monitoring from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Indicator Dashboard. On the page that is
displayed, view indicator data according to Table 5-10.

View Type Description

1. Click Add View in the upper right corner.

indicators, and change the indicator graph names

1. In the upper left corner of the Indicator
Dashboard page, select the name of the
indicator view to be deleted.
2. Click Delete View in the upper right corner.
3. In the dialog box that is displayed, click Yes.
----End
5.1.8 Monitoring Services (APIs)
You can view the total number of API calls by third-party system and the total
number of failed API calls in a specified period. This helps you understand the API
call status, facilitating issue analysis and locating.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management > Panoramic

Step 3 In the upper left corner of the Function Monitoring page, select the desired
product.
On the API Gateway Monitoring tab page, select or customize a time range to
view the total number of API calls by third-party systems and the total number of
failed API calls within the time range in the charts. You can click a time point on
the Failed API Calls chart to view the number of failed API calls in each scenario
collected within 1 minute before and after the time point.
----End
5.2 Exceptions and Events
When the PowerEcho detects that indicators of some resources meet the threshold
conditions, the corresponding exceptions or events are generated. You can set
filter criteria to view exceptions or events that you need to pay attention to and
learn about the system running status.
5.2.1 Browsing Exceptions and Events
O&M personnel can view exceptions and events to learn about faults or potential
problems of middleware and IT infrastructure and handle them in a timely
manner. This ensures stable and reliable resource supply for services in the service
running environment.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Background
When the system detects an exception or event of hardware, the backup and
restore module, databases, or network, the system reports the exception or event
to the exception or event list of the PowerEcho based on the impact on services.
Table 5-11 describes the definitions of exceptions and events. Table 5-12 lists the
exceptions and events that need to be concerned by system O&M personnel.

5.2.2 Modifying Monitoring Thresholds
The PowerEcho can report alarms if a resource is abnormal. The system has
default monitoring thresholds for nodes, relational databases, and Redis
databases. When the usage of a monitored object reaches the thresholds, the
PowerEcho reports corresponding alarms, and the alarm notifications are received

monitoring thresholds based on site requirements.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Context
Table 5-13 lists the default thresholds and resource objects that can be
configured.
NO TE
X indicates the value of Alarm Generation Threshold, Y indicates the value of Alarm
Clearance Threshold, and N indicates the value of Threshold-crossing Times. Y must be
less than X.

5.2.3 Configuring Database Thresholds
Panoramic monitoring provides the database monitoring capability and provides
the default thresholds and monitoring intervals. When a database indicator meets
the threshold, the PowerEcho reports the corresponding event. If events are
frequently reported due to inappropriate thresholds, you can change the
thresholds or monitoring intervals based on site requirements.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance
Management > Rule Settings from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, click Database Thresholds.
Step 3 Set monitoring thresholds based on the site requirements.
----End
5.3 Handling Service Calling Faults
O&M personnel can view calling statistics to identify failed service operations or
operations that are frequently called. By viewing call chain details, O&M personnel
can quickly view demarcation results of the failed operations. In addition, O&M

You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Context
A service operation needs to call a series of resource objects to implement
functions. The resource objects are connected based on the calling sequence,
which forms a call chain.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management > Call
Chains from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Call Statistics or Calls, and perform operations as
prompted. For details, see Table 5-14.
Table 5-14 Related operations
Operation Description
Service call analysis On the Service Call tab page of the Call Statistics
page, view the total number of service calls and the
number of failed service calls. To analyze the cause of
the calling failures, click the number in the Failed Calls
column of the desired service. On the tab page that is
displayed, click the desired call SN to view the calling
details.
Fault demarcation 1. On the Calls page, search for call chains by call
name, additional information, SN, or API.
2. Click a call chain to be analyzed to view its call
details and related resource information.
NOTE
If the management node is also a product node, the node
name displayed in the calling relationship topology on the
details panel of the called object is the name of the
product node.
Fault locating On the Calls page, export logs to your local PC to
further analyze and locate the fault.
NOTE
The exported logs include call chain logs, run logs, and CPU
and memory information of the current node.
Fault diagnosis 1. On the Calls page, click a call SN to go to the call
details page.
2. Click Diagnose. On the diagnosis details page,
export the diagnosis result and view the diagnosis
process.

Configuring APIs to be called: In the navigation pane, choose Rule Settings > Call
Chains. On the page that is displayed, set the sampling rate of all service APIs, the
sampling rate of each service API, and whether to enable the full chain function
based on site requirements. If both sampling rates are configured, the
configuration for a service API is used. The settings take effect immediately. The
PowerEcho collects chain data based on the specified specification.
5.4 Managing Mediation Packages
If the default functions provided by the PowerEcho cannot meet service
requirements, you can import required mediation packages to extend the O&M
monitoring capability of the PowerEcho.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the mediation package and corresponding signature file to
be uploaded.
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Precautions
● The mediation package and signature file to be uploaded must meet the
following requirements:
– The mediation package must match the signature file, and they must be
named in the format of product type-product version-package type-
server architecture-dotted version.
– The size of the mediation package is less than or equal to 1 MB and is
in .zip format.
– The signature file is in the .p7s format.
● During the upload of the mediation package, the PowerEcho checks the
integrity of the mediation package. If the mediation package does not meet
the format requirements, the upload will fail.
● After a mediation package is deleted, service operations (such as rectification
of exceptions and events) that depend on the mediation package are
unavailable. Exercise caution when performing this operation.
Procedure
Step 1 Manually verify the integrity of the mediation package. For details, see 13.16
Verifying File Integrity Using a P7S Signature File.
Step 2 Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management > Mediation
Package Management from the main menu.
Step 3 Perform related operations as prompted.

5.5 Network Diagnostics
This section describes how to check the network connection status between the
NetEco server and device.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance
Management > Network Diagnostics from the main menu.
Step 2 Set Equipment IP Address, Port Number, and Packets.
Step 3 Click Check.
----End
5.6 Routine Inspection
This section describes how to check the health status of the NetEco server using
the PowerEcho client to learn related information about the OS, database and
NetEco software. You can export the health check report and download it to the
local PC for view.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance
Management > Routine Inspection from the main menu.
Step 2 Select OSS CPU usage and disk I/O status as required.
NO TE
If you select this option, the check takes at least 10 minutes. If this option is not selected,
the check result does not contain the check items OSS CPU Usage and Disk I/O Usage
status.
Step 3 Click Inspect Health.
When the inspection is complete, you can click Export Report to download the
file to your local PC. Then decompress the file to view inspection results.

5.7 History Report
This section describes how to download the health check reports using the
PowerEcho. You can download the health check reports for the latest 10 health
inspection report. These logs can be used for identifying the causes of NetEco
system faults.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● Routine inspection has been performed. For details, see 5.6 Routine
Inspection.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance
Management > History Report from the main menu.
Step 2 Click Download to download the health check reports to your local PC.
----End
5.8 Viewing Server Information
This section describes how to use the PowerEcho to view Software Information,
Resource Information, and Hardware Information of a NetEco server.

You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management > Server
Information from the main menu.
On the page, view the NetEco server information such as the version information,
CPU usage, and iBMC IP address.
NO TE
● Inthe cluster scenario, you can view the information about a NetEco server by selecting
the corresponding node tab.
● Server information is updated every 30 seconds, and CPU details are updated every 5
seconds.

6
Task Management
By viewing task details, you can learn about task execution status and locate
causes of failed tasks. In addition, you can terminate a pending or running task to
perform another task with higher priority.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Context
Table 6-1 lists the types and description of tasks that you can view on the
PowerEcho.
Table 6-1 Task management
Task Type Description Condition
System task Tasks that are System operations, for
automatically example:
triggered by the
● Default scheduled backup
system.
tasks
● Scheduled tasks for backing
up the PowerEcho
User task Tasks that are Manual operations, for
manually executed by example:
users.
● Adding products
● Scanning software
packages
● Manually backing up the
PowerEcho

The time required for backing up the product data increases with the data volume.
If you need to execute another task with higher priority, you can terminate the
task for backing up product data when the task is in the Initialization,
Preliminary checks, Pre operation, or Execution stage. After the task with higher
priority is complete, you can create the terminated backup task again.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Task List from the main menu.
Step 2 Perform operations described in Table 6-2 based on the application scenario.
Table 6-2 Task operations
Scenario Operation
Viewing the In the task list, view Task Status and Task Progress.
task progress
● Click the name of a desired task, and the task details page is
and status
displayed. You can view the task topology view and task
execution status.
● You can drag the task topology view to find a desired
subtask to check the status and logs of the subtask.
Locating task Perform the following operations to locate the failure causes of
failure causes a task:
1. Click the name of a desired task, and the task details page is
displayed. You can view the overall task execution status and
the status of each phase in the task.
2. In the task topology, click a task icon. The task details and
task logs are displayed on the panel on the right.
3. Copy the task logs to your local PC as follows to locate the
failure causes:
NOTE
– This subtask: Logs of only the selected subtask are exported.
– Flow of this subtask: All logs of the task flow that contains the
selected subtask are exported.
a. Select This subtask or Flow of this subtask as required.
b. Click Copy Log.
c. Paste the copied content to your local PC to view the task
logs.
4. After the fault is rectified, click Retry to retry the failed task.
Scheduling To execute an urgent task, click the Terminate icon in the
tasks Operation column of a task with lower priority that does not
affect the system status. After the urgent task is successfully
executed, execute the terminated task again.

Scenario Operation
Analyzing To view the duration, start time, end time, and sequence of a
task duration task:
● Click the name of a desired task, and the task details page is
displayed. On the tab page for task duration analysis, you
can view the task execution process and the duration of
each step in the task.
● Click a compound task to view the duration of each atomic
task.
● On the tab page for task duration analysis, move the cursor
to an atomic task to view the start time, end time, status,
and duration of the atomic task, and the duration
percentage of the atomic task out of the overall duration of
the compound task.
----End

7
User Security Management
User security management plays an important part in preventing unauthorized
user logins and ensuring data security. User security management includes user
password management, user permission management, user security policy
management, and other security policies.
7.1 Password Management
For security purposes, periodically change user passwords of the PowerEcho.
During routine maintenance, change the passwords at the specified interval.
7.1.1 OS Users
This section provides information about default OS users and describes how to
change the passwords for these default users.
7.1.1.1 Default OS Users
The system provides default users and initial passwords, and grants different
permissions to these users. Before performing operations, learn the default users
and their permissions to improve operation efficiency. You are advised to change
the passwords periodically (every three months) and set new passwords according
to the password requirements.
NO TICE
For security purposes, change the password on first login, update it periodically,
and keep it secure.

7.1.1.2 GRUB Password
This section describes the concept, usage, and initial password of GRUB.
For details, see Table 7-3.

7.1.1.3 Enabling OS Users and Changing Passwords
To log in to a node as a disabled OS user, you need to enable the OS user and
change its password.

● You have obtained the passwords for the OS users to be enabled.
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho as a user attached to the
Administrators role. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the PowerEcho.
Context
The user password must meet the password complexity requirements.
The password rules are as follows:
● Contain 8 to 64 characters.
● Must be a combination of the following:
– Uppercase letters
– Lowercase letters
– Digits
– Special characters ~@#^*-_+[{}]:./?%=! (Starting with ! is not allowed.)
● Not contain more than two consecutive identical characters.
● Not be the same as the reverse of it, regardless of the letter case.
● Not contain strings in the weak password dictionary.
NO TE
● The weak password dictionary is a collection of weak and common passwords that
are vulnerable to cracking. Using a password in the weak password dictionary is
not allowed, which is a commonly adopted measure in the industry to ensure
security.
● For details about how to check and change passwords in the weak password
dictionary, see 13.9 Managing Passwords in the Weak Password Dictionary.
● For EulerOS, in addition to the preceding requirements, the password must
meet the following requirements:
– Not be the same as any of the last five passwords.
– The password must contain at least five different characters from the
username or reverse of the username, and consecutive identical
characters are regarded as one character.
– The password cannot contain the username or the reverse of it,
regardless of the letter case.
For example, if the username is ossadm, the password cannot contain
Ossadm or mdAsso.
Precautions
● You can enable OS users and change their passwords only on the web client.
NO TICE
Do not enable OS users and change their passwords in CLI mode. Otherwise,
system exceptions may occur.

task on the System > Task List page on the PowerEcho is running. Change
the OS passwords after all tasks are complete.
● After the OS user password of the product node is changed, the historical
backup files of the application and data of the PowerEcho, OS of the product
node, and product data will become invalid. Therefore, the backup files
generated before the OS user password change cannot be used for
restoration. Back up data in a timely manner after the OS user password is
changed.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Password Modification > Enable OS
User and Change Password from the main menu.
Step 2 Select Enable OS User and perform operations as prompted.
NO TE
After enabling the OS user and changing the password, click Apply. A task is created.
Manually refresh the page after the Enable OS user task in the task list is complete.
Step 3 Manually back up data as required after the OS user password change.
● The application and data of the PowerEcho. For details, see 10.6.1 Manually
Backing Up the Application and Data of the PowerEcho.
● The product data. For details, see 10.5.2 Backing Up Product Data.
----End
7.1.1.4 Changing Passwords for OS Users
For security purposes, change the initial passwords for OS users. You are advised
to periodically change the password for the OS users (for example, every three
months). Set new passwords according to the password rules of the OS.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● Before changing the operating system user password, ensure that the
database is running properly. Otherwise, the operating system password fails
to be changed.
● The OS user whose password is to be changed has been enabled. For details,
see 7.1.1.3 Enabling OS Users and Changing Passwords.
Context
The user password must meet the password complexity requirements.
The password rules are as follows:
● The password must contain 8 to 64 characters.
● The password must be a combination of the following four types of

– At least one lowercase letter
– At least one digit
– At least one special character from ~@#^*-_+[{}]:./?%=! (Starting with ! is
not allowed.)
● The password cannot contain more than two consecutive identical characters.
● The password cannot be the same as the reverse of it, regardless of the case.
● In addition to the preceding requirements, the password must meet the
following requirements:
– The password cannot be the same as any of the last five passwords.
– The password cannot be a password in the weak password dictionary.
NO TE
● The weak password dictionary is a collection of weak and common passwords
that are vulnerable to cracking. Using a password in the weak password
dictionary is not allowed, which is a commonly adopted measure in the
industry to ensure security.
● For details about how to check and change passwords in the weak password
dictionary, see 13.9 Managing Passwords in the Weak Password
Dictionary.
– The password must contain at least five different characters from the
username or reverse of the username, and consecutive identical
characters are regarded as one character.
– The password cannot contain the username or the reverse of the
username, regardless of the case.
For example, if the username is ossadm, the password Ossadm or
mdAsso is not allowed.
Precautions
● To prevent password change failure, do not change the OS user password in
command and GUI mode at the same time.
● If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode and some management nodes
are faulty, restore the faulty nodes first. Otherwise, the OS password fails to
be modified.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Password Modification > Enable OS
User and Change Password from the main menu.
Step 2 Select Change OS User Password.
Step 3 On the Change OS User Password page, select the node for which the password
needs to be changed, click Add, change the OS user password as prompted.

NO TE
● If an OS user password has expired, perform operations in this section to change the
password.
● Keep the password for the root user secure. If the password is lost, it cannot be reset or
retrieved, and you need to reinstall the OS, which has great impact on the O&M.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
If the management node is used as the backup server and the password for the
backup server user is changed, update the password in the backup parameters.
Otherwise, the backup file cannot be saved to the backup server. For details, see
10.4 Configuring Backup Parameters.
7.1.1.5 Changing the GRUB Password
You need to enter the GRUB password when the OS startup fails or when you
need to select other boot options. For OS security purposes, you need to change
the GRUB password periodically.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho as a user attached to the
Administrators role. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the PowerEcho.
● You have obtained the password for the root user of the OS.
● If any node is faulty, restore the node first. Otherwise, the GRUB password
cannot be changed. For details, see 13.11 Product Node Faults.
Context
● GRUB is a boot management program for the OS to select different kernels
on the OS partitions and pass startup parameters to these kernels. To prevent
OS boot configurations from being maliciously modified or illegally accessed,
you need to set the GRUB password and change the password periodically.
● The GRUB password must meet the planned password complexity
requirements.
The password must meet the following requirements:
– Contain 8 to 64 characters.
– Be a combination of the following:
▪
Uppercase letters
▪
Lowercase letters
▪
Digits
▪
Special characters ~]#^*_+[{}:./?%=@-

● Do not change the password for the OS user root when changing the GRUB
password on the web client. Otherwise, the GRUB password fails to be
changed.
● To ensure the security of OS passwords, no OS password can be changed if a
task on the System > Task List page on the PowerEcho is running. Change
the OS passwords after all tasks are complete.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Password Modification > Enable OS
User and Change Password from the main menu.
Step 2 Click Change GRUB Password.
Step 3 Perform operations as prompted.
----End
7.1.2 Database Users
This section provides information about default database users and describes how
to change the passwords for these users.
7.1.2.1 Default Database Users
A database provides default usernames and initial passwords. Change the
password upon first login and you are advised to change a password periodically
(every three months) and set a new password based on the specified password
rules.
NO TICE
For security purposes, change the password on first login, update it periodically,
and keep it secure.
If you need to change the database password, ensure that the passwords of the
sys user for all instances are the same.
Precautions
Use the ossdbuser user when writing data. If you use another user, the
relationship between master and slave databases may be incorrect.

7.1.2.2 Changing Passwords for Database Users
For security purposes, change the initial passwords for the database users to
reduce security risks of violent password cracking. You are advised to periodically
change the passwords for the database users (every three months) and set new
passwords based on the password rules.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The PowerEcho service is running properly. For details about how to check the
status of PowerEcho service, see 5.1.3 Monitoring Services.
● The database of PowerEcho with the user password to be changed is running
properly. For details about how to check the database status, see 5.1.4
Monitoring Databases.
● Before the database user password of a product is changed, the services of
the product with the database user password to be changed must be in the
Not Running state, and the product database with the user password to be
changed must be in the Running state. For details, see 5.1.3 Monitoring
Services and 5.1.4 Monitoring Databases.
– If the services of the product with database user password to be changed
are in the Running state, PowerEcho will automatically stop the product
services before the password is changed.
– If the product database with user password to be changed is in the Not
Running state, PowerEcho will automatically start the product database
before the password is changed.
● If the database is deployed in master/slave mode, Status of the database with
user password to be changed must be Running, and Replication Status of

database, see 5.1.4 Monitoring Databases.
● If a management node is faulty, restore the node first. Otherwise, database
user passwords cannot be changed. For details, see Table 9-10.
Context
The user password must meet the password complexity requirements.
The password rules are as follows:
● Contain 8 to 64 characters.
● Must be a combination of the following:
– At least one uppercase letter
– At least one lowercase letter
– At least one digit
– At least one special character ~@#^*-_+[{}]:./?
● The password must have no more than two consecutive identical characters.
● The password must not contain more than three identical characters.
● The password must contain at least two characters different from those in the
initial password.
● The password must not contain the user name or the user name in reverse
order, regardless of the letter case.
NO TE
For the GaussDB, the new password must meet the complexity requirements and
contain at least two characters different from the old password. The new password
cannot be the same as any used in the past 60 days, and cannot be the same as any
of the last three passwords.
Procedure
Step 1 On the main menu of PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Password Modification
> Change Database User Password.
Step 2 On the Change Database User Password page, perform operations according to
Table 7-12.
Table 7-12 Operations for changing database user passwords
Task Operation
Changing the password for a database Select Change Database
administrator Administrator Password, and perform
operations as prompted.

Task Operation
Resetting the Resetting 1. Select Reset Common Database
password for a passwords for all User Password.

2. Click Add and perform operations

a random password for the user.
NO TE
● If you change or reset database user passwords in batches and passwords of some
database instances fail to be changed, record the failure details in the task list. Change
passwords of the failed database instances one by one by referring to 13.5 How Do I
Change the Database Instance Password?.
● When changing the passwords for the GaussDB T application database users, you can
set Username to ossdbuser to change the passwords for all application database users
at a time.
● If you deselect the option for automatically starting product services in the Warning
dialog box after changing or resetting database user passwords, manually start all the
product services after the operations are complete. For details, see 3.3 Starting Product
Services.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
Manually back up the product applications, product data, and database
applications of PowerEcho. For details, see 10.5.3 Backing Up Product
Applications, 10.5.4 Backing Up Database Applications, and 10.5.2 Backing Up
Product Data.
7.1.3 Web Users
This section describes how to change the password for the default system
administrator user of the web client.

The system provides default users and initial passwords. Change the password
upon first login and you are advised to change a password periodically (every
three months) and set a new password based on the specified password rules.
NO TICE
For NetEco web system security, change the password upon first login, update it
periodically, and keep it secure.

7.1.3.2 Changing the Password for the admin User (NetEco)
To prevent security risks, such as violent password cracking, change the initial
password for the admin user. You are also advised to change the password for the
admin user periodically (for example, every three months) based on password
requirements.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to NetEco as the admin user. For details, see 1.1 Logging In
to the NetEco.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > Personal Settings.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Change Password.
Step 3 Set Old password, New password, and Confirm password.
Step 4 Click Apply.
----End
7.1.3.3 Changing the Password for the System Administrator (the
PowerEcho)
To prevent security risks, such as brute-force password cracking, you need to
change the password for the system administrator on the PowerEcho based on the
password policy periodically.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Precautions
If the password for the system administrator is lost, reset the password. For
details, see 7.1.3.5 Resetting the Password for the admin User of PowerEcho or

Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Security Management > Change Password
from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Change Password page, change the password as prompted.
----End
7.1.3.4 Changing the User Name and Password of the Swift Deploy
Deployment Tool
To improve system security, you need to change the password of user admin for
Swift Deploy. To improve account security, you are advised to change the default
user name admin. You are advised to periodically change the password to ensure
system security.
Prerequisites
You have obtained the password of user admin for Swift Deploy. The default
password is Changeme_123.
Context
● To improve password security, set the password based on the following
complexity requirements:
– The password must contain 8 to 32 characters.
– The password must contain at least the following three character types:
▪
Uppercase letter
▪
Lowercase letter
▪
Digit
▪
Special character `~!@#$%^&*()-_=+\|[{}];:'",<.>/? and space
– The password cannot be any user name or user name in reverse order.
● The naming rules of the user are as follows:
– 1 to 32 characters.
– Contain only letters a-z and A-Z and digits 0-9 and underscores (_).
– The user name must begin with a letter or digit.
Procedure
1. Use PuTTY to log in to the NetEco as user sopuser. For details, see 13.1
Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.
2. Run the following commands to change the username and password:
$ su - ossadm
password: ossadm password
$ cd /opt/repo/deploytool/bin

password for Swift Deploy, set a new user name, new password, and confirm
password for Swift Deploy, and press Enter.
If the following information is displayed, the password has been successfully
changed:
Update user password success.
7.1.3.5 Resetting the Password for the admin User of PowerEcho or NetEco
If the password for the admin user of the PowerEcho or NetEco is lost, reset the
password.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the password for the ossadm user.
● You have obtained the DP_VXXXRXXXCXX_ResetTool.zip package.
NO TE
Contact technical support to obtain the package.
● For enterprise users:
1. Log in to https://support.huawei.com/enterprise.
2. Browse or search for iMaster NetEco SitePower.
3. On the Software tab page, download the DP_VXXXRXXXCXX_ResetTool.zip tool
package of the corresponding version and the digital signature.
● For carriers:
1. Log in to https://support.huawei.com.
2. Browse or search for iMaster NetEco SitePower.
3. On the Software tab page, download the DP_VXXXRXXXCXX_ResetTool.zip tool
package of the corresponding version and the digital signature.
Precautions
● You must obtain the customer's consent before resetting the password for the
admin user.
● Resetting the password for the admin user does not start or stop services or
databases. NetEco can still be used properly.
● After the password for the admin user is reset, use the new password to log
in to NetEco. If the original password is used, the admin user may be locked.
Do not reset the password for the admin user unless the password is lost.
Procedure
Step 1 Use FileZilla to upload the DP_VXXXRXXXCXX_ResetTool.zip tool package to
the /opt/patch_manager directory on the management node as the ossadm user.
For details, see 13.2 Transferring Files Using FileZilla.
NO TE
If PowerEcho is deployed in the cluster scenario, upload the package to the Management0
node. For details about how to obtain the IP address of a node, see 12.5 Querying the

13.1 Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.
NO TE
If PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, log in to the Management0 node and perform
the following operations. For details about how to obtain the IP address of a node, see 12.5
Querying the Management IP Address of a Node.
Step 3 Run the following commands to decompress the tool package to the specified
directory:
$ mkdir -p /opt/patch_manager/resetadmin
$ unzip /opt/patch_manager/DP_VXXXRXXXCXX_ResetTool.zip -d /opt/
patch_manager/resetadmin/
NO TE
Replace DP_VXXXRXXXCXX_ResetTool.zip in the preceding command with the actual
name of the tool package.
Step 4 Run the following commands to change the file permissions:
$ cd /opt/patch_manager/resetadmin
$ chmod 550 resetTool.*
Step 5 Run the following command to reset the password for the admin user:
$ bash resetTool.sh
Enter 1 or 2 as prompted to reset the password of the admin user of PowerEcho
or NetEco. Enter Q to exit the program. (The following describes how to reset the
password for the admin user of PowerEcho. Apply a similar procedure to that of
NetEco.)
=============================================================
Please Select The Option For Resetting The Password :
1--PowerEcho
2--NetEco
Q--Exit
=============================================================
Please make a choice : 1
When information similar to the following is displayed, enter y and press Enter to
confirm the change of the PowerEcho password:
Are you sure you want to reset PowerEcho password? (y/n)
Plan and set a new password based on the command output. The new password
must meet the password rules.

NO TE
The password must meet the following requirements:
● Contain 8 to 64 characters.
● Must be a combination of the following:
– Uppercase letters
– Lowercase letters
– Digits
– Special characters !"#$%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?@[\]^`{_|}~ (Starting with ! is not allowed.)
● The password must have no more than two consecutive identical characters.
● In addition to the preceding requirements, the password must meet the following
requirements:
– The password cannot be a password in the weak password dictionary.
▪
The weak password dictionary is a collection of weak and common passwords
that are vulnerable to cracking. Using a password in the weak password
dictionary is not allowed, which is a commonly adopted measure in the
industry to ensure security.
▪
For details about how to check and change passwords in the weak password
dictionary, see 13.9 Managing Passwords in the Weak Password
Dictionary.
– The password cannot contain the username or the reverse of the username,
regardless of the case.
For example, if the username is ossadm, the password Ossadm or mdAsso is not
allowed.
When information similar to the following is displayed, enter the new password
and press Enter:
Enter a new Password:
When information similar to the following is displayed, enter the new password
again and press Enter:
Confirm the new password. The two passwords must be the same: Enter the password for the second time.
The password is changed successfully if the following information is displayed:
[2024-07-29 19:46:51] [51550] reset request count is 1
[2024-07-29 19:46:51] [51550] status:200
[2024-07-29 19:46:51] [51550] response:succeed
The PowerEcho password has been resetted successfully.
After returning to the main interface of the program, enter Q to exit the program.
Step 6 Run the following commands to delete the /opt/patch_manager/resetadmin
directory and the DP_VXXXRXXXCXX_ResetTool.zip tool package:
$ cd /opt/patch_manager
$ rm -rf resetadmin
$ rm -rf DP_VXXXRXXXCXX_ResetTool.zip
Step 7 Use the new password set in Step 5 to log in to PowerEcho or NetEco. For details,
see 1.2 Logging In to the PowerEcho or 1.1 Logging In to the NetEco. If the
login fails, contact technical support.

You are advised to set the password of the BIOS for the NetEco server and change
the password periodically, thereby improving the server setting security.
7.1.4.1 Entering the Remote Management Window of the Server
This section describes how to navigate to the remote management window of the
server to manage the server.
Prerequisites
● The communication between the PC and the management network port on
the server is proper.
● The IP address of the remote terminal is on the same network segment as
that of the remote management port on the server.
Context
NO TICE
The remote management window provides the remote password change function.
However, this function does not limit the number of times of entering incorrect
old passwords, and passwords may be violently cracked. Therefore, you need to set
the password meeting the complexity requirements and change it periodically to
reduce such a risk. For details about the functions in the remote management
window, see the online help in this window.
Procedure
Step 1 Open browser on the PC. Type the IP address https://IP address of the remote
management port for logging in to the remote management system in the
address bar, and press Enter. The page for logging in to the remote management
system is displayed.
NO TE
During login, if the system displays There is a problem with this website's security
certificate, click Continue to this website.
Step 2 Type the user name root and the password, then click Log In.
NO TE
In the new installation scenario, the user name is Administrator, and the password is
Admin@9000.
In the preinstallation scenario, the user name is root, and the password is Changeme_123.
Step 3 Start the remote console.
NO TE

menu, then choose HTML5 Integrated Remote Console (Private) from the
displayed Remote Control page.
● If the iBMC version is 5.XX, on the home page, drag the page downwards. In
the Virtual Console area, click Start and select HTML5 Integrated Remote
Console (Private).
----End
7.1.4.2 Setting and Changing the Password of the Server BIOS (TaiShan
Server)
You are advised to set the password of the BIOS for the server and change the
password periodically, thereby improving the server setting security.
Prerequisites
You have remotely logged in to the server through the iBMC web interface on a
PC. For detailed operations, see 7.1.4.1 Entering the Remote Management
Window of the Server.
NO TE
If the remote management port is used, you are advised to hold down Shift when entering
uppercase letters. Caps Lock is not recommended.
Procedure
Step 1 Start or restart the server. When the window shown in Figure 7-1 is displayed,
press Del. The BIOS (Basic Input Output System) window is displayed.
NO TE
● Please wait until the system switches to BIOS. This process may use one minute.
● If the server is running, please restart the server by clicking the restart button on the
upper part of the page. Restarting the system will cause the system to be unavailable
temporarily. Exercise caution when performing this operation.

Step 2 Enter the password on the BIOS screen,and press Enter. Shown as Figure 7-2.
NO TE
If the default password is set before delivery, you need to enter the default password before
entering the BIOS. The default password is Admin@9000. If the default password is not used,
skip this step.

Step 3 Prompt the current password is the default password needs to be modified, press
Enter, as shown in Figure 7-3.
Figure 7-3 Configuration interface

Figure 7-4 Security screen
Step 5 Select Set Supervisor Password and press Enter. You can set the administrator
login password, as shown in Figure 7-5. Press Enter.

NO TE
Set administrator password:
● The default BIOS password is Admin@9000.
● The password must be between 8 and 16 digits long. It must contain special
characters (including spaces) and at least two of uppercase letters, lowercase letters,
and numbers.
● The new password cannot be the same as the previous five passwords.
● After the administrator password is set, the "Clear Supervisor Password" parameter
appears, which can be used to clear the administrator password.
● To change the administrator password, you need to enter the current administrator
password first. If there are three input errors, the machine will be locked, and the
server will be unlocked after restart.
Step 6 Press Enter to return to the security interface, press F10 and select Yes to save
and exit, as shown in Figure 7-6.
NO TE
You are advised to change the password of the BIOS every three months.

----End
