● Digital signature: Select this usage if the certificate
requires identity authentication and data integrity
authentication.
● Non-repudiation: Select this usage if the certificate
requires authentication on the digital signature of the
public key to prevent the signing entity from denying its
requests.
● Key encipherment: Select this usage if the certificate
requires an encryption protocol.
● Data encipherment: Select this usage if the certificate
encrypts its application data.
● Key agreement: Select this usage if the public key is used
by the sender and receiver to encrypt data during
communication through plaintext agreement.
● Certificate signing: Select this usage if the public key
authenticates the certificate signature. It can only be used
as a proxy certificate.
● CRL signing: Select this usage if the public key of the
certificate is used to verify the signature on the revocation
information.
● Encipher only: Select this usage if Key agreement is
selected. The public key is used only for encrypting data
during the key agreement.
● Decipher only: Select this usage if Key agreement is
selected. The public key is used only for deciphering data
during the key agreement.

selected.
● Client authentication: TLS WWW client authentication.
Select this usage when Digital signature or Key
agreement in Key usage is selected.
● Email protection: Email protection. Select this usage when
the Digital signature, Non-repudiation, Key
encipherment, or Key agreement in Key usage is
selected.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.

Related Tasks
● Modify the certificate template.
Click in the Operation column of the row that contains the desired
certificate template to modify the template information.
● Delete the certificate template.
Click in the Operation column of the row that contains the desired
certificate template to delete the template.
8.4.9 Updating Certificates for Internal NetEco
Communication
Certificates for internal NetEco communication ensure the reliability of system
communication. All certificates need to be updated after the initial system
installation, and need to be periodically updated during system maintenance. If a
certificate is about to expire or the private key of a certificate is leaked, update the
certificate immediately to prevent system function unavailability and ensure
system security.
8.4.9.1 Updating the CA Certificate by Uploading Certificate Files
During routine maintenance, you can use the certificate management function to
upload certificate files to update the CA certificate, and view details about the
existing certificates, such as the issuer and validity period. During routine
maintenance, periodically update the certificates to prevent security risks caused
by private key leakage, and periodically check for alarms for expiring certificates
and update the certificates in time to prevent system function unavailability due
to certificate expiration. If there is an alarm for an expired certificate, update the
certificate immediately.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to
the PowerEcho.
If you fail to log in to the PowerEcho or the NetEco using a browser because
the certificate has expired, you can update the certificate in CLI mode. For
details, see 8.4.9.3.1 Updating the CA Certificate in CLI Mode.
● You have obtained the password for the ossadm user of the management

files and the password for the private key of the identity certificate. For
details, see Table 8-44.
If the obtained certificate is in .pem, .crt, or .der format, convert it to .cer
format by referring to 13.13 Converting the Certificate Format.
Table 8-44 Certificate requirements
Certificate Type Format Purpose

Precautions
● During the CA certificate update, all services on the nodes of the PowerEcho
and the NetEco are automatically restarted for the update to take effect, and
you cannot log in to the PowerEcho and the NetEco during the restart. You
are advised to perform this operation during off-peak hours.
● IR certificates are authenticated by the CA certificate of the management
node. After the CA certificate is updated, IR certificates are re-authenticated.
In this case, updating the CA certificates will automatically update IR

during the update. You do not need to manually change the password for the
new certificate.
● After the CA certificate is updated, historical backup files of the application
and data of the the PowerEcho, OS of the management node, product
database applications, OS of the product nodes, product applications, and
product data will become invalid. Therefore, the backup files generated before
the certificate update cannot be used for restoration. Back up data in a timely
manner after the certificate is updated.
● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to 8.4.13.1 Checking
Validity of the PowerEcho Certificates.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Certificate and Key > Certificate
Management from the main menu.
Step 2 Click the tile of the CA certificate.
Step 3 Click Update. Set the parameters according to Table 8-45 and perform operations
as prompted.
Table 8-45 Parameter description
Parameter Description
Scenario Select Local.
OS username Retain the default value ossadm.
Password Enter the password for the OS user ossadm.
CA certificate Select the certificate file ca.cer you have
obtained.
CA certificate private key Select the private key file ca_key.pem you
have obtained.
Private key password Enter the password for the private key file
ca_key.pem you have obtained.
Step 4 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Task List from the main menu. Click in the
row that contains the task to view the task execution result, and perform
operations according to Table 8-46.
Table 8-46 Operation guide
Execution Result Operation
The CA certificate is updated Go to Step 5.
successfully.

The CA certificate fails to be updated. Restore the CA certificate that fails to
be updated by referring to 8.4.9.3.4
Restoring the CA Certificates That
Failed to Be Updated, and then
contact technical support.

nodes at the corresponding site. For

Certificates on the Product Nodes

Step 5 After the CA certificate is updated, back up the following data:
● The application and data of the PowerEcho. For details, see 10.6.1 Manually
Backing Up the Application and Data of the PowerEcho.
● The product data. For details, see 10.5.2 Backing Up Product Data.
● The product applications. For details, see 10.5.3 Backing Up Product
Applications.
● The database applications. For details, see 10.5.4 Backing Up Database
Applications.
----End
Related Tasks
On the Identity Certificate tab page, click Export Trust Chain to export the
server certificate trust chain that contains the root certificate and intermediate
certificate. The server certificate trust chain can be imported to the peer site for
authentication.
8.4.9.2 Updating the User Management Certificates of the PowerEcho
For token security purposes, the user management function uses a certificate to
sign tokens. During routine maintenance, periodically update the certificates to
prevent security risks caused by private key disclosure, and periodically check for
alarms for expiring certificates and update the certificates in time to prevent
system function unavailability due to certificate expiration. If there is an alarm for
an expired certificate, update the certificate immediately.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossadm users of the
management node.
● You have applied for and obtained, from the CA, the new certificate files and
the password for the private key of the identity certificate. Table 8-47
describes the certificate requirements. For details about the certificate details,

Item Requirement
Certificate signature Use industry-leading security algorithms,
algorithm such as sha256WithRSAEncryption.
Key length ● For keys encrypted using RSA, the
recommended length is 3072 bits or
longer.
● For keys encrypted using ECDSA, the
recommended length is 256 bits or
longer.
NOTE
RSA keys shorter than 2048 bits or ECDSA keys
shorter than 224 bits are insecure. For security
purposes, ensure that the private key of the
certificate is an RSA key of 3072 bits or longer or
an ECDSA key of 256 bits or longer.
Certificate validity period Set this parameter based on the customer's
IT security management requirements.
Format The format and name of the certificate and
private key files must be consistent with the
following, and only lowercase letters are
allowed in the file name:
● signing_cert.pem: identity certificate of
user management
● ca.pem: trust certificate of user
management
● signing_key.pem: private key of the
identity certificate of user management.
The private key must be encrypted using
the AES-128-CBC algorithm. If the private
key is not encrypted, encrypt it by
referring to 13.4 Encrypting the Private
Key of the Signature Certificate (the
PowerEcho).

Item Requirement
Password complexity of the ● Contain 10 to 32 characters.
private key file
● Be a combination of the following four
types of characters:
– Uppercase letters
– Lowercase letters
– Digits
– Special characters !"#$%&'()*
+,-./:;<=>?@[]^`{_|}~
● Not contain double quotation marks (")
and single quotation marks (') at the
same time.
● Contain less than three consecutive
identical characters.
● Contain less than four identical
characters.
Precautions
● During the update of the user management certificate, MCCommonService is
automatically restarted for the update to take effect, and you cannot log in to
the PowerEcho during the restart. You are advised to perform this operation
during off-peak hours.
● The token obtained using the original certificate becomes invalid. After the
certificate is updated, you need to use the new certificate to obtain a token.
● You can check for alarms for expiring and expired certificates by referring to
8.4.13.1 Checking Validity of the PowerEcho Certificates.
Procedure
Step 1 Perform the following operations to update the user management certificates:
1. Use PuTTY to log in to the management node as the sopuser user in SSH
mode. For details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.
2. Run the following commands to create a temporary directory for storing the
certificates and set permission for the directory:
> mkdir /certificatebak
> chmod -R 750 /certificatebak
3. Use FileZilla to upload the user management certificate files to the /
certificatebak directory on the node described in Step 1.1, as the sopuser
user in SFTP mode. For details, see 13.2 Transferring Files Using FileZilla.
4. Use PuTTY to run the following command as the sopuser user to set the
permission for the certificate:
> chmod -R 640 /certificatebak/*
5. Run the following commands to switch to the ossadm user, create a directory

Password: password for the ossadm user
> mkdir /certificate
> chmod 750 /certificate
6. Run the following command to copy the certificate files to the new directory:
> cp -R /certificatebak/* /certificate
7. Run the following commands to update the certificate:
> cd /opt/oss/manager/apps/MCCommonService/bin
> bash deployPKICert.sh -d /certificate
8. If the following information is displayed, enter the password for the
signing_key.pem file:
Enter private key password:password for the signing_key.pem file
Reenter Password: password for the signing_key.pem file
If information similar to the following is displayed, the certificate is updated
successfully:
The original certificate has been backed up.
The files ca.pem, signing_cert.pem and signing_key.pem in folder /certificate have been deleted.
Certificate updated successfully.
After the user management certificate is updated, the user management
certificate files in the certificate storage directory are automatically deleted. If
information similar to the following is displayed, the certificate files fail to be
automatically deleted. Go to Step 3 to manually delete them.
Failed to delete the file ca.pem, signing_cert.pem or signing_key.pem in folder /certificate. Manually
delete it.
Step 2 Log in to the PowerEcho. If the login is successful, the update has taken effect.
Otherwise, contact technical support.
Step 3 Delete the temporary files.
1. Run the following commands to manually delete the certificate files in the
certificate storage directory only when they fail to be automatically deleted:
> cd
> rm -rf certificate
2. Switch back to the sopuser user and run the following commands to delete
the temporary directory for storing the certificate files:
> exit
> cd
> rm -rf certificatebak
Step 4 After the update, back up the application and data of the PowerEcho. For details,
see 10.6.1 Manually Backing Up the Application and Data of the PowerEcho.
----End
8.4.9.3 Updating CA and IR Certificates in CLI Mode
CA certificates have the issuing function and are used for issuing IR certificates of
the PowerEcho and the NetEco. IR certificates are certificates in the trusted
domain and are used for two-way authentication during communication between

certificate is about to expire or the private key file of the certificate is disclosed,
update the certificate immediately to prevent system function unavailability and
ensure system security.
8.4.9.3.1 Updating the CA Certificate in CLI Mode
CA certificates have the issuing function and are used for issuing IR certificates of
the PowerEcho and the NetEco. IR certificates are certificates in the trusted
domain and are used for two-way authentication during communications between
NetEco services. During routine maintenance, periodically update the certificates
to prevent security risks caused by private key disclosure, and periodically check
for alarms for expiring certificates and update the certificates in time to prevent
system function unavailability due to certificate expiration. If there is an alarm for
an expired certificate, update the certificate immediately.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the IP address and the password for the sopuser and
ossadm users of the management node, and the IP address of a product
node.
● You have obtained the new certificate files and the password for the private
key file. Table 8-48 describes the certificate requirements. For details about
the certificate details, see 13.12 Viewing Certificate Details.
If the obtained certificate is in .pem, .crt, or .der format, convert it to .cer
format by referring to 13.13 Converting the Certificate Format.

Certificate Type Format Purpose
Identity Identity certificate of NetEco: Used to issue IR
certificate certificates of the
CER (the file name must be
PowerEcho and the
ca.cer), used with the private
NetEco.
key (the file name must be
ca_key.pem). IR certificates are
authenticated by the
NOTE
If the private key file ca_key.pem CA certificate of the

certificate is updated,

PowerEcho). Ensure that its
authenticated. In this
password meets the following
requirements and keep it secure: case, updating the CA
certificates will
● Contain 10 to 32 characters.
automatically update
● Be a combination of the
IR certificates of all
following four types of
characters: nodes.
● Uppercase letters
● Lowercase letters
● Digits
● Special characters !"#$
%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?@[]^`{_|}~
● Not contain double quotation
marks (") and single quotation
marks (') at the same time.
● Contain less than three
consecutive identical
characters.
● Contain less than four identical
characters.
Context
● CA certificates are stored in the /opt/oss/manager/var/ca directory on the
management node.
● The CA certificate contains the following files:
– ca.cer: identity certificate file of the root certificate
– ca_key.pem: private key of the identity certificate of the root certificate
– manifest.json: certificate configuration file
– server.conf: certificate configuration file
When the CA certificate is updated, the manifest.json file is automatically
generated.
Precautions
● IR certificates are authenticated by the CA certificate of the management

certificates of all nodes.
● When the CA certificate is updated, the system automatically backs up the CA
certificate and IR certificates to the /opt/oss/tmp/cert/CA and /opt/oss/tmp/
cert/internal directories respectively on the management node. After the
update is successful, the backup certificates are automatically deleted.
● During the CA certificate update, all services on the nodes of the PowerEcho
and the NetEco need to be restarted for the update to take effect, and you
cannot log in to the PowerEcho and the NetEco during the restart. You are
advised to perform this operation during off-peak hours.
● After the certificate is updated, historical backup files of the application and
data of the PowerEcho, OS of the management node, product database
applications, OS of the product nodes, product applications, and product data
will become invalid. Therefore, the backup files generated before the
certificate update cannot be used for restoration. Back up data in a timely
manner after the certificate is updated.
● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to 8.4.13.1 Checking
Validity of the PowerEcho Certificates.
Procedure
Step 1 If the management node and product node are the same node, check the product
service status.
1. Use PuTTY to log in to a product node, as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
2. Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
3. Run the following commands to check the product service status:
> source /opt/oss/manager/bin/engr_profile.sh
> ipmc_adm -cmd statusnodes -tenant product name
Information similar to the following is displayed. If APP Status of all nodes
whose Node Type contains APP is RUNNING, the services are running.
Otherwise, contact technical support.
Node Name Node Type Node IP APP Status DB Status
node01 APP,DB 10.10.154.42 RUNNING RUNNING
node02 APP,DB 10.10.154.43 RUNNING RUNNING
node03 APP 10.10.154.44 RUNNING NA
Step 2 Use PuTTY to log in to the management node as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
NO TE
● Use the fixed IP address instead of the floating IP address of the management node for
login. Otherwise, PuTTY will be disconnected, causing the certificate update to fail.
● Services need to be restarted during the certificate update. If no operation is performed
on PuTTY for a long time, PuTTY may be disconnected upon timeout, which causes the
certificate update to fail. Set the PuTTY timeout period by referring to 12.20 How Do I
Prevent PuTTY from Being Disconnected upon Timeout?
Step 3 Run the following commands to create a temporary directory for uploading the
new certificate and change the permission for the directory:

Step 4 Use FileZilla to upload the certificate files ca.cer and ca_key.pem to the /CAbak
directory on the node described in 2, as the sopuser user in SFTP mode. For
details, see 13.2 Transferring Files Using FileZilla.
Step 5 Use PuTTY to run the following command as the sopuser user to set the
permission for the new certificate in the /CAbak directory:
> chmod 640 /CAbak/*
Step 6 Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
Step 7 Run the following commands to create a directory for storing the new certificate
and change the permission for the directory:
> mkdir -p /CA
> chmod 750 /CA
Step 8 Run the following command to copy the certificate files to the new directory:
> cp /CAbak/* /CA
Step 9 Run the following commands to set permission for the certificate files:
> chmod 700 /CA
> find /CA -type f | xargs chmod 600
Step 10 Run the following commands to update the CA certificate:
The old CA certificate is automatically backed up to the /opt/oss/tmp/cert
directory on the management node.
> cd /opt/oss/manager/apps/UniEPService/tools/common
> bash replaceCaCert.sh -type local -capath /CA
The following information is displayed:
Replacing the certificates will interrupt services. Are you sure you want to continue? (y/n)
Step 11 Enter y and press Enter.
The following information is displayed:
Password:
Step 12 Enter the password for the obtained CA certificate and press Enter.
After the password passes the verification, the system restarts all services and
updates the CA certificate.
● If the following information is displayed, the CA certificate is updated
successfully. Go to 14.
Success to replace ca certificate.
● If other information is displayed, the CA certificate fails to be updated. Go to

Step 13 If the certificate fails to be updated, perform the following operations to update
the certificate again:
1. Run the following command to back up the old certificate:
> cp -a /opt/oss/tmp/cert /opt/oss/tmp/cert_old
2. Run the following commands to update the CA certificate:
> cd /opt/oss/manager/apps/UniEPService/tools/common
> bash replaceCaCert.sh -type local -capath /CA
The following information is displayed:
Replacing the certificates will interrupt services. Are you sure you want to continue? (y/n)
3. Enter y and press Enter.
The following information is displayed:
Password:
4. Enter the password for the obtained CA certificate and press Enter.
After the password passes the verification, the system restarts all services and
updates the CA certificate. Perform the corresponding operation according to
the command output.

8.4.9.3.2 Updating IR Certificates on the Product Nodes Failed When CA
Certificates Are Being Updated
If the new certificate is used to update the CA certificate of the management node
and the IR certificates of the product nodes fail to be updated, manually update
the IR certificates on the nodes that fail to be updated.
Prerequisites
You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossadm users of the node
where the certificate update failed.
Context
When the CA certificate is updated, the system copies the CA certificate of
Management1 as the certificate of the NetEco, that is, trust.cer.
Precautions
● If the CA certificate fails to be updated, the system automatically backs up
the CA certificate and IR certificates to the /opt/oss/tmp/cert/CA
and /opt/oss/tmp/cert/internal directories on the management node,
respectively.
● Services need to be restarted so that the certificates can take effect after the
restoration. You are advised to perform this operation during off-peak hours.
Procedure
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the product node where the certificate update fails, as the

> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
Step 3 Run the following commands to stop services on the node:
> source /opt/oss/manager/bin/engr_profile.sh
> ipmc_adm -cmd stopnode
Step 4 After the services are stopped, run the following commands to update the IR
certificates of the failed node:
> cd /opt/oss/manager/agent/bin
> bash osskey -cmd replace_ircerts
The following information is displayed:
Are you sure to replace IR certs(Y/N):
Step 5 Enter y, and press Enter.
If the following information is displayed, the task is successfully executed:
Execute osskey cmd:replace_ircerts Successful
Step 6 Run the following command to update the IR certificates of all services on the
current node:
> bash osskey -cmd replace_ircerts -service all
The following information is displayed:
Are you sure to replace IR certs for all service instances(Y/N):
Step 7 Enter y, and press Enter.
If the following information is displayed, the task is successfully executed:
Execute osskey cmd:replace_ircerts Successful
Step 8 Run the following commands to start services on the node:
> ipmc_adm -cmd startnode
If success is displayed for all services, the services are started successfully.
Step 9 Run the following command to delete the backup certificates:
> cd /opt/oss/tmp
> rm -rf cert/*
> exit
----End
8.4.9.3.3 Updating IR Certificates
IR certificates are certificates in the trusted domain and are used for two-way
authentication during communications between NetEco services. IR certificates are

certificates to prevent security risks caused by private key leakage, and periodically
check for alarms for expiring certificates and update the certificates in time to
prevent system function unavailability due to certificate expiration. If there is an
alarm for an expired certificate, update the certificate immediately.
Prerequisites
You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossadm users of the
management node.
Context
● On the management node, public IR certificates are stored in /opt/oss/
manager/etc/ssl/internal, and service IR certificates are stored in /opt/oss/
manager/apps/service name/etc/ssl/internal.
● On the product nodes, public IR certificates are stored in /opt/oss/
NetEco/etc/ssl/internal, and service IR certificates are stored in /opt/oss/
NetEco/apps/service name/etc/ssl/internal.
● The IR certificates of the node where the GaussDB database is deployed are
stored in the /opt/zenith/ssl directory on the node.
● The files related to the IR certificate are as follows:
– cert_pwd: file that stores the encrypted password for the identity
certificate
– manifest.json: certificate configuration file
– server.cer: identity certificate
– server_key_crypto.pem: private key of the identity certificate file
– server.p12: identity certificate in .p12 format
– server_key.pem: private key of the identity certificate
– trust.cer: trust certificate
– trust.jks: trust certificate in .jks format
Precautions
● When IR certificates are updated, the IR certificates on all nodes where the
PowerEcho and the NetEco reside are also updated.
● When IR certificates are updated, the IR certificates on the node where the
GaussDB database is deployed are also updated.
● Services on all nodes where the PowerEcho and the NetEco reside are
automatically restarted so that the certificates can take effect after the
update. You are advised to perform this operation during off-peak hours.
● After the certificate is updated, historical backup files of the application and
data of the PowerEcho, OS of the management node, product database
applications, OS of the product nodes, product applications, and product data
will become invalid. Therefore, the backup files generated before the
certificate update cannot be used for restoration. Back up data in a timely
manner after the certificate is updated.
● You can check for alarms for expiring and expired certificates by referring to

Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the management node as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
For details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.
NO TE
Services need to be restarted during the certificate update. If no operation is performed on
PuTTY for a long time, PuTTY may be disconnected upon timeout. Set the PuTTY timeout
period by referring to 12.20 How Do I Prevent PuTTY from Being Disconnected upon
Timeout?
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
Step 3 Run the following commands to update the IR certificate:
> cd /opt/oss/manager/apps/UniEPService/tools/common
> bash updatecertificate.sh -certtype internal
The following information is displayed:
Replacing the certificate will interrupt services. Are you sure you want to continue? (y/n)
Step 4 Enter y and press Enter.
The system automatically stops the services, updates the IR certificates on all the
nodes, and then starts the services.
● If the following information is displayed, the IR certificates are updated
successfully. Go to Step 5.
Certificates replaced successfully.
● If other information is displayed, the IR certificates fail to be updated. In this
case, retry the update. If the update still fails, contact technical support.
Step 5 After the update, back up the following data:
● The application and data of the PowerEcho. For details, see 10.6.1 Manually
Backing Up the Application and Data of the PowerEcho.
● The product data. For details, see 10.5.2 Backing Up Product Data.
● The product applications. For details, see 10.5.3 Backing Up Product
Applications.
● The database applications. For details, see 10.5.4 Backing Up Database
Applications.
----End
8.4.9.3.4 Restoring the CA Certificates That Failed to Be Updated
If a message is displayed, indicating that services on the management node fail to
be started when you update the CA certificate of the management node, restore
the CA certificate from the backup files automatically generated after the update
failure in a timely manner. This prevents the PowerEcho or the NetEco from being

You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossadm users of the
management node.
Precautions
● If the CA certificate fails to be updated, the system automatically backs up
the CA certificate and IR certificates to the /opt/oss/tmp/cert/CA
and /opt/oss/tmp/cert/internal directories on the management node,
respectively.
● If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, the CA certificate and IR
certificates are backed up only to Management0 or Management1.
● Services need to be restarted so that the certificates can take effect after the
restoration. You are advised to perform this operation during off-peak hours.
Procedure
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the management node as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
For details about how to obtain the IP address of a node, see 12.5 Querying the
Management IP Address of a Node.
NO TE
● If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, log in to Management0 and
Management1 and perform Step 2 to Step 3 on the two nodes respectively.
● Use the fixed IP address instead of the floating IP address of the management node for
login. Otherwise, PuTTY will be disconnected, causing the certificate update to fail.
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
Step 3 If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, log in to Management0 and
Management1 and run the following command on both nodes to check whether
the CA and internal directories exist. Perform Step 4 on the management nodes
where the directories exist.
> ls /opt/oss/tmp/cert
Step 4 Run the following commands to restore the CA certificate by using the backup CA
certificate:
> cp -aP /opt/oss/tmp/cert/CA/* /opt/oss/manager/var/ca/
> cp -aP /opt/oss/tmp/cert/internal/* /opt/oss/manager/etc/ssl/internal/
NO TE
To roll back after you have retried the update, run the following commands:
> cp -aP /opt/oss/tmp/cert_old/CA/* /opt/oss/manager/var/ca/
> cp -aP /opt/oss/tmp/cert_old/internal/* /opt/oss/manager/etc/ssl/internal/

> cd /opt/oss/manager/agent/bin
> bash osskey -cmd replace_ircerts
The following information is displayed:
Are you sure to replace IR certs(Y/N):
2. Enter y and press Enter.
If the following information is displayed, the task is successfully executed:
Execute osskey cmd:replace_ircerts Successful
Step 6 Restart the services on the node.
1. Run the following commands to stop services on the node:
> source /opt/oss/manager/bin/engr_profile.sh
> ipmc_adm -cmd stopnode
If success is displayed for all services, the services are stopped successfully.
2. Run the following commands to start services on the node:
> ipmc_adm -cmd startnode
If success is displayed for all services, the services are started successfully.
Step 7 If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, restore the certificates of other
management nodes.
1. Run the following command as the ossadm user on the management node
described in Step 1 to check whether the cert_bak directory exists:
> ll /tmp
– If yes, run the following command to delete the directory:
> rm -rf cert_bak
– If no, go to Step 7.2.
2. Run the following command to copy the directory that stores the
automatically backed up CA certificate and IR certificates to the temporary
directory:
> cp -P -r /opt/oss/tmp/cert /cert_bak
3. Run the following commands to configure permissions for the certificate files
in the temporary directory:
> chmod -R 750 /cert_bak
> chgrp -R sopgroup /cert_bak
4. Use FileZilla to download the cert_bak directory in on the management node
to your local PC, as the sopuser user in SFTP mode. For details, see 13.2
Transferring Files Using FileZilla.
The cert_bak directory contains the CA and internal folders that store the CA
and IR certificate files, respectively.
5. Use FileZilla to upload the downloaded cert_bak directory to the directory on
the other management node, as the sopuser user in SFTP mode.
If the cert_bak directory is downloaded from Management0, upload the
cert_bak directory to Management1. If the cert_bak directory is downloaded
from Management1, upload it to Management0. Upload the cert_bak

ossadm user and run the following commands to delete the temporary
directory and switch to the sopuser user:
> cd
> rm -rf cert_bak
> exit
7. Use PuTTY to log in to the management node in Step 7.5 to which the
cert_bak directory has been uploaded, as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
8. Run the following commands to copy the files from the directory to the
temporary directory:
> cp -P -r /cert_bak /cert_new
> chmod -R 750 /cert_new
> chgrp -R sopgroup /cert_new
9. Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
10. Run the following commands to copy the certificate files from the temporary
directories to certificate storage directories:
> cp -P -r /cert_new/CA/* /opt/oss/manager/var/ca
> cp -P -r /cert_new/internal/* /opt/oss/manager/etc/ssl/internal
11. Run the following commands to set file permission and delete temporary files:
> exit
> cd
> rm -rf cert_new
> rm -rf cert_bak
12. Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
13. Perform Step 5 to Step 6 to restore the CA certificate and IR certificates of
the management node that you have logged in to.
14. Perform Step 5 to restore the IR certificates on management nodes other
than Management0 and Management1, as the ossadm user.
----End
8.4.9.4 Updating the Signature Certificate Revocation List
If the private key of a certificate is disclosed, you need to update the certificate
revocation list (CRL) of the build signature or release signature immediately to
prevent unauthorized operations.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossadm users of the

from https://support.huawei.com/pki/pkidetail!
loadCatagoryPkiData.action?type=PKI_TYPE-CRL.
Precautions
The system automatically backs up the signature CRL before the signature CRL of
management node or a specified node is updated. If a backup file already exists,
the system will not back up the signature CRL. If you need to back up the CRL
again, run the huaweicrltool.sh -cmd rmbackupCrl -node IP address of the node
where the backup file resides -type signature type command to delete the backup
file of the node before the update.
Procedure
Step 1 Upload the latest build signature or release signature CRL.
1. Rename the signature CRL.
– For a build signature CRL, rename it HuaweiRootCAsipCRLs.crl.
– For a release signature CRL, rename it
HuaweiRootCAsipCRLs_Release.crl.
NO TE
The following describes how to update the build signature CRL. Replace
HuaweiRootCAsipCRLs.crl with HuaweiRootCAsipCRLs_Release.crl when
updating the release signature CRL.
2. Use FileZilla to upload the HuaweiRootCAsipCRLs.crl file to the /home/
sopuser directory on the management node, as the sopuser user in SFTP
mode.
NO TE
If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, that is, there are multiple management
nodes, perform operations on Management0 or Management1. For details about how
to obtain the IP address of a node, see 12.5 Querying the Management IP Address
of a Node.
3. Use PuTTY to log in to the node described in Step 1.2, as the sopuser user in
SSH mode.
4. Run the following command to change the file permission:
> chmod 640 /home/sopuser/HuaweiRootCAsipCRLs.crl
5. Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
6. Run the following command to check whether the /home/ossadm/oss
directory exists:
> ll /home/ossadm/oss
– If information similar to the following is displayed, the /home/
ossadm/oss directory exists. Go to Step 1.7.
total 0
...
– Otherwise, the /home/ossadm/oss directory does not exist. Run the

7. Run the following command to copy the file to the /home/ossadm/oss
directory:
> cp -P /home/sopuser/HuaweiRootCAsipCRLs.crl /home/ossadm/oss
8. Run the following commands to switch back to the sopuser user and delete
the temporary file:
> exit
> cd /home/sopuser
> rm -f HuaweiRootCAsipCRLs.crl
9. Run the following commands to switch to the ossadm user and change the
file permission:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
> chmod 400 /home/ossadm/oss/HuaweiRootCAsipCRLs.crl
Step 2 Run the following commands to update the build signature CRLs of all
management nodes:
> cd /opt/oss/manager/apps/UniEPService/tools/crlTools/
NO TE
If the PowerEcho is deployed in single-server mode, and the management node and
product node are the same node, replace UniEPService with UniEPLiteService.
> bash huaweicrltool.sh -cmd replaceCrl -all
Replace the values of the preceding parameters based on site requirements.
Parameter Description
-node IP address of the node whose signature CRL
needs to be updated. If there are multiple
nodes, separate their IP addresses with
commas (,).

Parameter Description
-type Type of the signature with CRL to be updated.
This parameter is optional. If this parameter is
not set, the default value BUILD_SIGN is used.
The options are as follows:
● RELEASE_SIGN: release signature, which is
used for digital signature and verification on
released software packages. This type of
signature is completed in the release phase
to provide a unified and basic integrity
protection mechanism.
NOTE
Run the following command to update the
release signature CRL on the node:
> bash huaweicrltool.sh -cmd replaceCrl -type
RELEASE_SIGN
● BUILD_SIGN: build signature, which is used
for digital signature and verification on non-
variable files in a software package. This
type of signature is completed in the build
phase and is integrated in the final software
package to provide integrity verification in
the installation and upgrade scenarios.
-all Updates the build signature CRL of all
management nodes.
When information similar to the following is displayed, enter y:
Are you sure to continue? [y/n] y
● If information similar to the following is displayed, the signature CRL is
updated successfully:
success replaceCrl on node:10.10.10.101,10.10.10.102,10.10.10.103
● If information similar to the following is displayed, the signature CRL of the
node fails to be updated. Rectify the fault on the node and go to Step 2.
failed replaceCrl on node : 10.10.10.101 please try again
● If information similar to the following is displayed, the signature CRL file is
incorrect. Contact technical support to obtain the correct one, and update the
signature CRL by following instructions provided in this section.
verify crl failed
Step 3 Delete the temporary file.
1. Run the following commands to delete the temporary file:
> cd /home/ossadm
> rm -rf oss
2. Run the following command to exit from the ossadm user:
> exit

● To restore the signature CRLs of all management nodes, run the
huaweicrltool.sh -cmd restoreCrl -all -type signature type command.
● To restore the signature CRLs of specified nodes, run the huaweicrltool.sh -
cmd restoreCrl -node IP address of the node with signature CRL to be
restored -type signature type command.
NO TE
Use commas (,) to separate multiple IP addresses.
8.4.9.5 Updating Certificates for Internal NetEco Communication by
Uploading Certificate Files (the NetEco)
The system provides the certificate management function on the web client. You
can register the certificates of the NetEco services to update these certificates in
GUI mode. Currently, certificate management supports the update of certificates
and CRLs of some services in GUI mode. During routine maintenance, periodically
update the certificates to prevent security risks caused by private key leakage. To
prevent system function unavailability due to certificate expiration, periodically
check the certificate validity in certificate management or check for alarms for
expiring and expired certificates. If a certificate is about to expire, update the
certificate in time. If a certificate has expired or the private key of a certificate is
leaked, update the certificate immediately. If the trusted CA has updated the CRL,
update the CRL in a timely manner.
For details about the supported services, see the list of service certificates
supported by certificate management.
Prerequisites
● You have the Query Certificates, Import Certificates, and Delete
Certificates permissions.
● SnbCertMgmt has been deployed, and the certificate management function is
available.
● You have obtained the new certificates and related information. For details,
see the list of service certificates supported by certificate management. You
can obtain the certificates by referring to 8.4.4 Process of Uploading and
Updating Certificates.
Table 8-49 List of service certificates supported by certificate management
Service Cert Certificate and Relevant Purpose
ifica Files
te
Type
Precautions
● When you update a certificate (identity certificate, trust certificate, or CRL),

Therefore, you are advised to perform this operation in off-peak hours.
● You can check the validity period of a certificate by referring to 8.4.13.2
Checking Validity of the NetEco Certificates.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Service Certificate Management.
Step 3 On the Services page, click the service with certificates to be imported.
Step 4 Configure certificate information based on the certificate type.
1. On the Identity Certificates tab page, click Import.
2. On the Import Identity Certificate page, set related parameters, and click
Submit.
NO TE
Identity certificates include the Certificate library type and Independent public and
private keys type. The certificate type varies depending on the service requirements.
– Certificate library: The public key, private key, and certificate chain are in the
same keystore file. When importing the certificate, you only need to import one
file.
– Independent public and private keys: The public key and private key are separate
files.

3. On the Trust Certificates tab page, click Import. On the Import Trust
Certificate page, set related parameters, and click Submit.
4. On the Certificate Revocation Lists tab page, click Import. On the Import
Certificate Revocation List page, set related parameters, and click Submit.
NO TE
– By default, Enable CRL check is enabled on the Certificate Management >
Settings > General Configurations page. If Enable CRL check is disabled, you do
not need to import the CRL.
– The total number of identity certificates, trust certificates, and CRLs imported for a
service cannot exceed 200.

Step 6.
● If not, click Cancel.

When an identity certificate is imported, the message displayed contains the
parameter comparison between the old and new certificates.
Step 6 In the dialog box that is displayed, click OK.

8.4.9.6 Updating Certificates for Internal NetEco Communication Online (the
NetEco)
You can quickly apply for a certificate using an existing certificate template, or
apply for certificate by manually configuring certificate information. You can then
apply the obtained certificate to each service. For a service that has an identity
certificate, you can quickly update its identity certificate using wizard-based
update. During routine maintenance, periodically update the certificates to
prevent security risks caused by private key leakage. To prevent system function
unavailability due to certificate expiration, periodically check the certificate validity
in certificate management or check for alarms for expiring and expired certificates.
If the private key is leaked or a certificate has expired, update the certificate

● The interconnection with the CA server has been configured.
● You have the Query Certificate Application Task and Manage Certificate
Application Task permissions.
● You have obtained the information about the certificate, such as the
certificate format, common name (CN), country or region (C), province or
state (ST), city (L), organization (O), department (OU), email (E), validity
period, key algorithm, key length, subject alternative name (SAN), certificate
type, key usage, and extended key usage.
Context
● The time difference between NetEco and the CA server must be less than 5
minutes. Otherwise, the certificate application fails.
● The validity period of a certificate applied from the CA is jointly determined
by the custom validity period, validity period of the CA root certificate,
maximum validity period supported by the CA, and validity period of the
application template supported by the CA. Generally, the validity period does
not exceed the maximum validity period supported by the CA and the validity
period of the application template supported by the CA. The actual validity
period depends on the interconnected CA.
● Table 8-52 describes the certificate formats, key algorithms, key lengths,
certificate types, key usages, and extended key usages supported by certificate
management.
● When you update a certificate (identity certificate or trust certificate), the
corresponding services will load the certificate. During the loading, the
corresponding functions may be unavailable for a short period of time.
Therefore, you are advised to perform this operation in off-peak hours.
● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to "Checking Validity of
the NetEco Certificates".
Table 8-52 Certificate information
Parameter Description
Certificate Format of the certificate.
format
● PEM
● PKCS12
Key Key algorithm of the certificate.
algorithm
● RSA
● ECDSA

Parameter Description
Key length ● Length of the RSA certificate key:
– 8192
– 6144
– 4096
– 3072
– 2048
NOTE
RSA whose length is shorter than 2,048 bits is insecure. You are
advised to use the certificate encrypted by RSA whose length is
3,072 bits or longer.
● Length of the ECDSA certificate key:
– 521
– 384
– 256
Certificate Type of the certificate.
type
● End Entity: Select this type if you do not need to use the
key corresponding to the certificate to issue certificates.
● CA: Select this type when you need to use the
corresponding key to issue certificates.

Parameter Description
Key usage Usage of the certificate key.
● Digital signature: Select this usage if the certificate
requires identity authentication and data integrity
authentication.
● Non-repudiation: Select this usage if the certificate
requires authentication on the digital signature of the
public key to prevent the signing entity from denying its
requests.
● Key encipherment: Select this usage if the certificate
requires an encryption protocol.
● Data encipherment: Select this usage if the certificate
encrypts its application data.
● Key agreement: Select this usage if the public key is used
by the sender and receiver to encrypt data during
communication through plaintext agreement.
● Certificate signing: Select this usage if the public key
authenticates the certificate signature. It can only be used
as a proxy certificate.
● CRL signing: Select this usage if the public key of the
certificate is used to verify the signature on the revocation
information.
● Encipher only: Select this usage if Key agreement is
selected. The public key is used only for encrypting data
during the key agreement.
● Decipher only: Select this usage if Key agreement is
selected. The public key is used only for deciphering data
during the key agreement.
Extended Extended usage of the certificate key.
key usage
● Server authentication: TLS WWW server authentication.
Select this usage when Digital signature, Key
encipherment, or Key agreement in Key usage is
selected.
● Client authentication: TLS WWW client authentication.
Select this usage when Digital signature or Key
agreement in Key usage is selected.
● Email protection: Email protection. Select this usage when
the Digital signature, Non-repudiation, Key
encipherment, or Key agreement in Key usage is
selected.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.

requirements.
NO TE
● Online Application: You can quickly apply for a certificate using an existing certificate
template, or apply for a certificate (identity certificate or trust certificate) by manually
configuring certificate information.
● Wizard-based Update: You can quickly update the identity certificates for the services
that already have an identity certificate.

NOTE
After a certificate application template is selected, you can change
the values of some parameters as required. For example, if the
validity period set in the certificate application template is 5 years,
but the required validity period of an online certificate application
task is 10 years, you can set the validity period to 10 years for the

as prompted based on site requirements.
3. Click OK.
4. In the list of online certificate update tasks, check Task Status
of the created task.
– Pending: The online certificate application task is applying
for a new certificate. Refresh the page and check the task
status.
– Successful: The online certificate application task is created
successfully.
– Failed: The online certificate application task fails to be
created. Rectify the fault based on the failure causes. Click
in the Operation column. In the dialog box that is displayed,
click Details and rectify the fault based on the details. If the
problem persists, contact technical support.

to Step 3.5.c.
a. Click in the Operation column of the row that contains
the certificate.

and confirm whether to apply certificate to services.

click OK, and go to Step 3.5.e.

corresponding services.

is displayed, the certificate is successfully applied to the
service. Click OK. Otherwise, handle the problem according to
the information in the dialog box.

When a certificate is applied to services, the identity certificate and
certificate chain of the certificate are applied to the services.

default value is 50 years.

Server. In the dialog box that is displayed, configure the CA
server interconnected with the certificates of the selected
services.

that contains the corresponding service. On the page that is

confirm whether to update the certificates of the services.
– If so, select I understand the risk and want to continue,
and click OK.
– If not, click Cancel.

of the created task.
– Pending: The wizard-based certificate update task is applying
for a new certificate. Refresh the page and check the task
status.
– Successful: The certificates of all services are successfully
updated.
– Partially successful: The certificates of some services are
successfully updated.
– Failed: The certificates of all services fail to be updated.

If the task status is Partially successful or Failed, click in the
Operation column of row that contains the corresponding task. In the
dialog box that is displayed, click Details and rectify the fault based on
the information. If the fault persists, contact technical support.

certificate:

c. Click the Identity Certificates tab.

the row that contains the desired certificate.
8.4.9.7 Updating Certificates for Internal NetEco Communication Using CSR
Management (the NetEco)
You can use the CSR management function to create a CSR, export the CSR file,
and submit the CSR to the CA to apply for a public key and certificate chain. After
applying for the public key and certificate chain, you can use the CSR
management function to bind the public key and certificate chain to the CSR and
private key in the database to form a complete identity certificate. After the
binding, the certificate can be quickly applied to services. During routine
maintenance, periodically update the certificates to prevent security risks caused
by certificate expiration and private key leakage. To prevent system function
unavailability due to certificate expiration, periodically check the certificate validity
in certificate management or check for alarms for expiring and expired certificates.
If the private key is leaked or a certificate has expired, update the certificate
immediately. If a certificate is about to expire, update the certificate in time.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the information about the CSR to be created, such as the
common name (CN), country or region (C), province or state (ST), city (L),
organization (O), department (OU), email (E), key algorithm, key length,
subject alternative name, certificate type, key usage, and extended key usage.
● Before updating a certificate, you have applied for and obtained the public
key and certificate chain from the CA.
Context
● Table 8-54 describes the key algorithms, key lengths, certificate types, key
usages, and extended key usages supported by certificate management.
● When you update a certificate (identity certificate), the corresponding services
will load the certificate. During the loading, the corresponding functions may
be unavailable for a short period of time. Therefore, you are advised to
perform this operation in off-peak hours.
● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to "Checking Validity of

Parameter Description
Key Key algorithm of the certificate.
algorithm
● RSA
● ECDSA
Key length ● Length of the RSA certificate key:
– 8192
– 6144
– 4096
– 3072
– 2048
NOTE
RSA whose length is shorter than 2,048 bits is insecure. You are
advised to use the certificate encrypted by RSA whose length is
3,072 bits or longer.
● Length of the ECDSA certificate key:
– 521
– 384
– 256
Certificate Type of the certificate.
type
● End Entity: Select this type if you do not need to use the
key corresponding to the certificate to issue certificates.
● CA: Select this type when you need to use the
corresponding key to issue certificates.

Parameter Description
Key usage Usage of the certificate key.
● Digital signature: Select this usage if the certificate
requires identity authentication and data integrity
authentication.
● Non-repudiation: Select this usage if the certificate
requires authentication on the digital signature of the
public key to prevent the signing entity from denying its
requests.
● Key encipherment: Select this usage if the certificate
requires an encryption protocol.
● Data encipherment: Select this usage if the certificate
encrypts its application data.
● Key agreement: Select this usage if the public key is used
by the sender and receiver to encrypt data during
communication through plaintext agreement.
● Certificate signing: Select this usage if the public key
authenticates the certificate signature. It can only be used
as a proxy certificate.
● CRL signing: Select this usage if the public key of the

information.

during the key agreement.
● Decipher only: Select this usage if Key agreement is

during the key agreement.

key usage

encipherment, or Key agreement in Key usage is
selected.

selected.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.

1. Click Create CSR.
2. Select a CSR creation mode based on site requirements.
– Creating a CSR by selecting an existing template
i. (Optional) Create a certificate application template. For details, see
8.4.8.4 (Optional) Managing Certificate Templates (the NetEco).
ii. Select a template from the Template drop-down list.
iii. Set parameters as prompted based on site requirements.
NO TE
After using an existing certificate application template, you can also modify
some parameters as required.
– Creating a CSR directly
Set parameters as prompted based on site requirements.
3. Click OK.
Step 4 Export the CSR file and apply for a public key and certificate chain from the CA.
1. Click in the Operation column of the row that contains the CSR to export
the CSR as a file. The CSR file is in .csr format.
NO TE
In the CSR management list, you can view the status of the created CSRs.
– Not exported: CSRs in the Not exported state can be modified, exported, and
deleted.
– To be bound: CSRs in the To be bound state can be modified, exported, bound,
and deleted.
– Bound: CSRs in the Bound state can be deleted and the corresponding certificate
can be applied to services.
2. Submit the CSR file to the CA to apply for a public key and certificate chain.
Step 5 Bind the obtained public key and certificate chain to the CSR and private key in
the database.
1. Click in the Operation column of the row that contains the desired CSR.
2. Enter certificate information and import the public key and certificate chain.
3. Click Submit.
4. In the dialog box, read the information carefully and confirm whether to bind
the certificate.
– If so, click OK. Go to Step 6.
– If not, click Cancel.
Step 6 Apply the certificate bound with the CSR to services.
1. Click in the Operation column of the row that contains the desired CSR.
2. Select the services to which you want to apply the certificate.
3. Click OK.
4. In the High Risk dialog box, read the information carefully and confirm

go to Step 6.5.
– If not, click Cancel.
5. Check whether the certificate is successfully applied to the corresponding
services.
If the message "Certificates applied to services successfully." is displayed, the
certificate is successfully applied to the service. Click OK. Otherwise, handle
the problem according to the information in the dialog box.
NO TE
When a certificate is applied to services, the identity certificate and certificate chain of
the certificate are applied to the services.
----End
Related Tasks
● Modifying a CSR
To modify the information about an existing CSR, click in the Operation
column of the CSR on the CSR Management page.
NO TE
A CSR that has been bound to a certificate cannot be modified.
● Deleting a CSR
On the CSR Management page, click in the Operation column of the
desired CSR to delete it.
NO TE
CSRs corresponding to certificates that have been applied to services cannot be
deleted. To delete such a CSR, delete the certificate of the corresponding services first.
8.4.9.8 Updating the CRL (Network AI Engine Infer Service)
If the private key of a certificate is leaked, you need to update the certificate
revocation list (CRL) of the build signature or release signature immediately to
prevent unauthorized operations.
Prerequisite
● You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossadm users of the
nodes where AppClientService resides.
● You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossuser users of the
nodes where NAIEBasicCombService resides.
● You have obtained the signature CRL file HuaweiRootCAsipCRLs.crl from
https://support.huawei.com/pki/pkidetail.
Precautions
● Update the build signature or release the signature CRL.
● Perform this operation on all nodes where AppClientService and

Step 1 Use a test tool to upload the HuaweiRootCAsipCRLs.crl file to the /home/
sopuser directory on the node as the sopuser user in SFTP mode.
Step 2 Run the following command to change the file permission:
> chmod 640 /home/sopuser/HuaweiRootCAsipCRLs.crl
Step 3 Switch to the target user.
● For AppClientService, switch to the mcossuser user.
> su - ossadm
> sudo -u mcossuser bash
● For NAIEBasicCombService, switch to the ossuser user.
> su - ossuser
Step 4 Copy the file in Step 2 to the directories where the services reside.
● AppClientService
> cp -P /home/sopuser/HuaweiRootCAsipCRLs.crl /opt/oss/manager/apps/
AppClientService/etc/ssl/internal
● NAIEBasicCombService
> cp -P /home/sopuser/HuaweiRootCAsipCRLs.crl /opt/oss/SOP/apps/
NAIEBasicCombService/etc/ssl/internal
Step 5 Run the following commands to switch back to the sopuser user and delete the
temporary file:
> exit
> cd /home/sopuser
> rm -f HuaweiRootCAsipCRLs.crl
----End
8.4.9.9 Updating the Signature Certificate of a Model Package in the Asset
Library
The SamGalleryServiceOP service can use a script to generate and automatically
import a self-signed certificate. Replace the certificate to prevent system security
risks caused by brute force cracking or certificate expiration.
NO TICE
The certificate and password are sensitive information. Keep them secure.
The security verification rules for self-signed certificates are as follows:
● Signature algorithm: RSASSA-PSS
● Signature hash algorithm: SHA-384 or SHA-512
● Key length: The value must be greater than or equal to 4096.

● You have obtained the passwords of the sopuser and ossuser users for
logging in to the The management node.
● After the self-signed certificate is replaced, the original one will be
overwritten.
● The service caches the certificate. The certificate update takes effect after one
hour.
Procedure
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the management node as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
For details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.
NO TE
If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, that is, there are multiple management
nodes, perform operations on Management0 and then on Management1. For details about
how to obtain the IP address of a node, see 12.5 Querying the Management IP Address
of a Node.
Step 2 Switch to the ossuser user.
su - ossuser
Step 3 Run the following commands to generate and automatically import the certificate:
export OSS_ROOT=/opt/oss/NetEco
export APP_ROOT=/opt/oss/NetEco/apps/NAIEBasicCombService
sh /opt/oss/NetEco/apps/NAIEBasicCombService/tools/init_sign_cert.sh
----End
8.4.10 Updating Certificates for Communication Between
NetEco and Browsers
The certificates for communication between NetEco and browsers ensure the
reliability when users use browsers to access the web client of NetEco. You need to
update all certificates after initial system installation, and update the certificates
during routine maintenance. If a certificate is about to expire or the private key of
a certificate is leaked, update the certificate immediately to prevent system
function unavailability and ensure system security.
Process of Updating ER Certificates
If NetEco is not interconnected with a CA or new certificates are on your local PC,
you can update certificates by uploading the certificate files. If NetEco is
interconnected with a CA, you can update certificates online. Figure 8-6 describes
the update process.

Table 8-56 describes the process of updating ER certificates by uploading files,
and Table 8-55 describes the process of updating ER certificates online.
