8.4.10.1 Updating ER Certificates by Uploading Certificate Files
ER certificates are required for authentication during the communication with an
external environment. For example, the browser uses the ER certificate to verify

view details about the existing certificates, such as the issuer and validity period.
Periodically check for alarms for expiring certificates and update certificates in
time to prevent system function unavailability and ensure system security. If there
is an alarm for an expired certificate, update the certificate immediately. If the
private key of a certificate is disclosed or the trusted CA has updated the CRL,
update the certificate and CRL immediately.
Prerequisites
● Before updating a certificate, you need to understand the certificate update
process. For details, see 8.4.10 Updating Certificates for Communication
Between NetEco and Browsers.
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
If you fail to log in to the PowerEcho or the NetEco using a browser because
the ER certificate has expired, you can update the ER certificate of the
PowerEcho or the NetEco based on site requirements. Alternatively, you can
update all the ER certificates of both in random sequence. For details, see
8.4.10.3 Updating ER Certificates of the PowerEcho in CLI Mode and
8.4.10.4 Updating ER Certificates of the NetEco in CLI Mode.
● The CSR has been bound with the certificate if you need to use CSR
management to obtain the new certificate. For details, see 8.4.7.1.3 Binding a
CSR with a Certificate.
● A CSR file has been generated before you apply for the certificate from the
CA, if you need to update the certificate by uploading certificate files. For
details, see 8.4.7.1.2 Generating a CSR File in CLI Mode. Table 8-57 lists the
certificate requirements.
If the obtained certificate is in .pem, .crt, or .der format, convert it to .cer
format by referring to 13.13 Converting the Certificate Format.
Table 8-57 Certificate requirements
Item Requirement
Certificate Use industry-leading security algorithms, such as
signature sha256WithRSAEncryption.
algorithm
Key length ● For keys encrypted using RSA, the recommended length is
3072 bits or longer.
● For keys encrypted using ECDSA, the length can be 256 or
384 bits.
NOTE
RSA keys shorter than 2048 bits or ECDSA keys shorter than 224 bits
are insecure. For security purposes, ensure that the private key of the
certificate is an RSA key of 3072 bits or longer or an ECDSA key of
256 bits or longer.
Certificate Set this parameter based on the customer's IT security
validity management requirements.

Item Requirement
Extended The X509v3 Subject Alternative Name attribute is
certificate mandatory. The value is an IP address or a domain name. If
attributes an IP address is used to log in to the PowerEcho or the
NetEco client, set the value to the IP address. If a floating IP
address is used, set the value to the floating IP address. If a
domain name is used, set the value to the domain name.
Password If the private key file server_key.pem is not encrypted, you
complexity need to encrypt it by referring to 13.4 Encrypting the Private
of the Key of the Signature Certificate (the PowerEcho). Ensure
private key that its password meets the following requirements and keep
file it secure:
● Contain 10 to 32 characters.
● Be a combination of the following four types of characters:
– Uppercase letters
– Lowercase letters
– Digits
– Special characters !"#$%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?@[]^`{_|}~
● Not contain double quotation marks (") and single
quotation marks (') at the same time.
● Contain less than three consecutive identical characters.
● Contain less than four identical characters.

Item Requirement
Certificate The PowerEcho supports .cer and .p12 formats. The format
format and name of the certificate and private key files must be
consistent with the following, and only lowercase letters are
allowed in the file name:
● If the identity certificate is in .cer format, select Base-64
encoded X.509(.CER) when exporting the certificate.
– server.cer: identity certificate of NetEco, used to
authenticate the identity of NetEco by browsers. One
server.cer certificate file can contain only one identity
certificate.
– server_key.pem: private key of the identity certificate,
which is used with its password.
– trust.cer: certificate of the CA that issues the identity
certificate. Ensure that the certificate trust chain is
complete. For details about how to merge the root CA
certificate and the intermediate certificate, see 13.14
Merging Trust Certificate Files.
– crl.pem: certificate revocation list of the trust certificate.
This file is used to manage untrusted certificates, which
prevents risks of access to NetEco using an invalid
identity.
● If the identity certificate is in .p12 format:
– server.p12: identity certificate of NetEco, used to
authenticate the identity of NetEco by the peer system.
– trust.cer: certificate of the CA that issues the identity
certificate. Ensure that the certificate trust chain is
complete.
– crl.pem: certificate revocation list of the trust certificate.
This file is used to manage untrusted certificates, which
prevents risks of access to NetEco using an invalid
identity.
NOTE
● If the certificate is obtained from a local CA, download the CA
certificate chain by referring to Downloading the CA certificate
chain.
● If the obtained certificate is in .pem, .crt, or .der format, convert it
to .cer format by referring to 13.13 Converting the Certificate
Format.
Precautions
● If a task for updating the CA certificate has been created, the ER certificate
can be updated only after the CA certificate update task is complete.
Otherwise, the ER certificate may fail to be updated.
● The system automatically randomizes the password for the new certificate
during the update. You do not need to manually change the password for the

directories /opt/oss/manager/etc/ssl/er and /opt/oss/NetEco/etc/ssl/er.
Otherwise, the PowerEcho or product rollback will fail.
● During the ER certificate update of the PowerEcho, MCHIROERService is
automatically restarted for the update to take effect, and you cannot log in to
the PowerEcho during the restart. You are advised to perform this operation
during off-peak hours.
● During the ER certificate update of the NetEco, HIROERService is
automatically restarted for the update to take effect, and you cannot log in to
the NetEco during the restart. You are advised to perform this operation
during off-peak hours.
● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to 8.4.13.1 Checking
Validity of the PowerEcho Certificates.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Certificate and Key > Certificate
Management from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Certificate Management page, click the tile corresponding to the ER
certificate of the PowerEcho or your desired product.
Step 3 Set Certificate alarms based on site requirements.
● If the alarms "51020 The certificate is about to expire" and "51021 The
certificate has expired" need to be reported, toggle on Certificate alarms
(this parameter is toggled on by default). If a certificate-related alarm is
generated, you can view the alarm on the Service Monitor > Alarms >
Current Alarms page of the NetEco.
● If these alarms do not need to be reported, toggle off Certificate alarms. In
this case, you may fail to learn about certificate expiration, causing security
risks in internal communications. Exercise caution when performing this
operation.
Step 4 Perform operations according to Table 8-58.

Scenario Operation
Updating the identity 1. On the Identity Certificate tab page, click
certificate, trust certificate, Update.
and CRL
2. Select Upload. Upload certificate files based
on site requirements.
– If you need to upload certificate files from
your local PC, set Apply for certificates
using CSR management to , and
perform operations as prompted.
– If you need to apply for a new certificate
using CSR management, set Apply for
certificates using CSR management to
, select the CSR bound to the
certificate, and perform operations as
prompted.
NOTE
● After the CA trusted by NetEco updates the CRL,
you need to update the CRL to NetEco in a timely
manner to prevent unauthorized operations by
identity fraud. For security purposes, you need to
upload a new CRL for update when you update
certificates.
● The total size of the certificate files cannot exceed
2 MB.
Importing only the trust 1. On the Trust Certificate tab page, click
certificate Import.
2. Perform operations as prompted.
– You can import multiple trust certificates
one by one. The total size of all trust
certificate files cannot exceed 2 MB.
– If authentication of NetEco on browsers'
trust certificates is required, enable
forcible authentication of NetEco on
browsers' digital certificates first, and then
import the browsers' trust certificates for
authentication. For details, see 8.4.10.6
(Optional) Enabling and Disabling
Forcible Authentication of the
PowerEcho Server on Digital
Certificates of Browser Clients.
Importing only the CRL 1. Click the Certificate Revocation List tab and
click Import.
2. Perform operations as prompted.
You can import multiple CRLs one by one.
The total size of all CRL files cannot exceed 2
MB, and the number of all CRL files cannot
exceed 1000.

Step 5 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Task List from the main menu to check the
execution status of the task for updating the ER certificate.
● If Task Status is Execution Succeeded, the ER certificate is updated
successfully.
● If Task Status is Execution Failed, rectify the fault based on the handling
suggestions in the task details.
Step 6 After the certificate update is complete, back up the following data:
● If the ER certificate of the PowerEcho is updated, back up the application and
data of the PowerEcho. For details, see 10.6.1 Manually Backing Up the
Application and Data of the PowerEcho.
● If the ER certificate of the NetEco is updated, back up the following data of
the product:
– The product application. For details, see 10.5.3 Backing Up Product
Applications.
– The database applications. For details, see10.5.4 Backing Up Database
Applications.
– The product data. For details, see 10.5.2 Backing Up Product Data.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
If you log in to the management plane using a browser and a message similar to
"This Connection is Untrusted" is displayed, perform the following operations:
1. Click Export Trust Chain to export the server certificate trust chain that
contains the root certificate and intermediate certificate, split the exported file
into a root certificate and an intermediate certificate in .cer format
respectively.
2. Add the trust certificate of the ER certificate to the browser's certificates. For
details, see 8.4.10.8 Configuring Trust Certificates of Browsers.
Related Tasks
● Restoring the certificate to the last update
Step 1 On the Certificate Management page, click the tile corresponding to the ER
certificate to be restored.
Step 2 On the Identity Certificate tab page, click Restore, and perform operations as
prompted.
----End
● Downloading the CA certificate chain
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Authority Service from the main menu.

Certificate tab, select all certificates, and click Download in the upper right
corner to download the CA certificate chain in .pem format to your local PC.
----End
8.4.10.2 Updating ER Certificates Online
ER certificates are required for authentication during the communication with an
external environment. For example, the browser uses the ER certificate to verify
the identity of the PowerEcho or the NetEco. After the system is interconnected
with a CA, you can periodically update ER certificates online using certificate
management during routine maintenance, and view certificate details, such as the
issuer and validity period. Periodically check for alarms for expiring certificates and
update certificates in time to prevent system function unavailability and ensure
system security. If there is an alarm for an expired certificate, update the
certificate immediately. If the private key of a certificate is disclosed or the trusted
CA has updated the CRL, update the certificate and CRL immediately.
Prerequisites
● Before updating a certificate, you need to understand the certificate update
process. For details, see 8.4.10 Updating Certificates for Communication
Between NetEco and Browsers.
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The PowerEcho has been interconnected with a CA server, and the CA server
configuration (such as the IP address, port number, and address information)
is consistent with the CA server from which the certificate is to be obtained.
For details, see 8.4.8.2.1 Setting CA Server Connection Parameters (the
PowerEcho).
● To update CRLs online, the PowerEcho must be interconnected with a CRL
server. For details, see 8.4.8.2.3 Setting CRL Server Connection Parameters
(the PowerEcho).
● You have obtained the information about the certificate to be updated, such
as the name of CA server interconnecting with the PowerEcho, certificate
subject information, certificate validity period, key algorithm, key length, key
usage, and extended key usage.
● Table 8-59 describes the key algorithms, key lengths, certificate types, key
usages, and extended key usages supported by certificate management.
Table 8-59 Certificate information
Parameter Description
Key algorithm Key algorithm of the certificate.
● RSA
● ECDSA

Parameter Description
Key length ● Length of the RSA certificate key:
– 2048
– 3072
– 4096
– 8192
NOTE
RSA keys shorter than 2048 bits are insecure. For
security purposes, ensure that the private key of the
certificate is an RSA key of 3072 bits or longer.
● Length of the ECDSA certificate key:
– 256
– 384
NOTE
ECDSA keys shorter than 224 bits are insecure. For
security purposes, ensure that the private key of the
certificate is an ECDSA key of 256 bits or longer.
Certificate type Type of the certificate.
End Entity: Select this type if you do not need to
use the key corresponding to the certificate to issue
certificates.

Parameter Description
Key usage Usage of the certificate key. This parameter is
optional. If this parameter is set, the value must
contain Digital signature. Otherwise, you cannot
log in to the PowerEcho and the NetEco after the ER
certificates are updated.
● Digital signature: Select this usage if the
certificate requires identity authentication and
data integrity authentication.
● Non-repudiation: Select this usage if the
certificate requires authentication on the digital
signature of the public key to prevent the signing
entity from denying its requests.
● Key encipherment: Select this usage if the
certificate requires an encryption protocol.
● Data encipherment: Select this usage if the
certificate encrypts its application data.
● Key agreement: Select this usage if the public
key is used by the sender and receiver to encrypt
data during communication through plaintext
agreement.
● Certificate signing: Select this usage if the
public key is used to verify the certificate
signature. It can only be used as a proxy
certificate.
● CRL signing: Select this usage if the public key of
the certificate is used to verify the signature on
the revocation information.
● Encipher only: Select this usage if Key
agreement is selected. The public key is used
only for encrypting data during the key
agreement.
● Decipher only: Select this usage if Key
agreement is also selected. The public key is
used only for decrypting data during the key
agreement.

Parameter Description
Extended key usage Extended usage of the certificate key.
● Server authentication: TLS WWW server
authentication. Select this usage when Digital
signature, Key encipherment, or Key
agreement in Key usage is selected.
● Client authentication: TLS WWW client
authentication. Select this usage when Digital
signature or Key agreement in Key usage is
selected.
● Email protection: Email protection. Select this
usage when the Digital signature, Non-
repudiation, Key encipherment, or Key
agreement in Key usage is selected.
Precautions
● If a task for updating the CA certificate has been created, the ER certificate
can be updated only after the CA certificate update task is complete.
Otherwise, the ER certificate may fail to be updated.
● The system automatically randomizes the password for the new certificate
during the update. You do not need to manually change the password for the
new certificate.
● Do not modify the content or permission of files in the certificate storage
directories /opt/oss/manager/etc/ssl/er and /opt/oss/NetEco/etc/ssl/er.
Otherwise, the PowerEcho or product rollback will fail.
● During the ER certificate update of the PowerEcho, MCHIROERService is
automatically restarted for the update to take effect, and you cannot log in to
the PowerEcho during the restart. You are advised to perform this operation
during off-peak hours.
● During the ER certificate update of the NetEco, HIROERService is
automatically restarted for the update to take effect, and you cannot log in to
the NetEco during the restart. You are advised to perform this operation
during off-peak hours.
● After the ER certificate of the PowerEcho is updated, historical backup files of
the application and data of the PowerEcho and OS of the management node
will become invalid. Therefore, the backup files generated before the
certificate update cannot be used for restoration. Back up data in a timely
manner after the certificate is updated.
● After the ER certificate of the NetEco is updated, historical backup files of the
product database applications, OS of the product nodes, product application,
and product data will become invalid. Therefore, the backup files generated
before the certificate update cannot be used for restoration. Back up data in a
timely manner after the certificate is updated.
● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to 8.4.13.1 Checking
Validity of the PowerEcho Certificates.

Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Certificate and Key > Certificate
Management from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Certificate Management page, click the tile corresponding to the ER
certificate of the PowerEcho or your desired product.
Step 3 Set Certificate alarms based on site requirements.
● If the alarms "51020 The certificate is about to expire" and "51021 The
certificate has expired" need to be reported, toggle on Certificate alarms
(this parameter is toggled on by default). If a certificate-related alarm is
generated, you can view the alarm on the Service Monitor > Alarms >
Current Alarms page of the NetEco.
● If these alarms do not need to be reported, toggle off Certificate alarms. In
this case, you may fail to learn about certificate expiration, causing security
risks in internal communications. Exercise caution when performing this
operation.
Step 4 Perform the following operations to update the identity certificate, trust
certificate, and CRL:
1. On the Identity Certificate tab page, click Update.
2. Select Online update.
3. Optional: Select the desired certificate template from the Template drop-
down list. For details about how to create a certificate template, see 8.4.8.3
(Optional) Managing Certificate Templates (the PowerEcho).
4. Set related parameters, click Update, and perform operations as prompted.
NO TE
After the CA trusted by NetEco updates the CRL, you need to update the CRL to
NetEco in a timely manner to prevent unauthorized operations by identity fraud. For
security purposes, you need to update the CRL when you update certificates.
Step 5 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Task List from the main menu to check the
execution status of the task for updating the ER certificate.
● If Task Status is Execution Succeeded, the ER certificate is updated
successfully.
● If Task Status is Execution Failed, rectify the fault based on the handling
suggestions in the task details.
Step 6 After the certificate update is complete, back up the following data:
● If the ER certificate of the PowerEcho is updated, back up the application and
data of the PowerEcho. For details, see 10.6.1 Manually Backing Up the
Application and Data of the PowerEcho.
● If the ER certificate of the NetEco is updated, back up the following data of
the product:
– The product application. For details, see 10.5.3 Backing Up Product
Applications.
– The database applications. For details, see 10.5.4 Backing Up Database

----End
Follow-up Procedure
If you log in to the PowerEcho using a browser and a message similar to "This
Connection is Untrusted" is displayed, perform the following operations:
1. Click Export Trust Chain to export the server certificate trust chain that
contains the root certificate and intermediate certificate, split the exported file
into a root certificate and an intermediate certificate in .cer format
respectively.
2. Add the trust certificate of the ER certificate to the browser's certificates. For
details, see 8.4.10.8 Configuring Trust Certificates of Browsers.
Related Tasks
Restoring the certificate to the last update
Step 1 On the Certificate Management page, click the tile corresponding to the ER
certificate to be restored.
Step 2 On the Identity Certificate tab page, click Restore, and perform operations as
prompted.
----End
8.4.10.3 Updating ER Certificates of the PowerEcho in CLI Mode
ER certificates are required for authentication during the communication with an
external environment. For example, the browser uses the ER certificate to verify
the identity of the PowerEcho. If you cannot log in to the PowerEcho, you can
update the ER certificate in CLI mode. Periodically check for alarms for expiring
certificates and update certificates in time to prevent system function
unavailability and ensure system security. If there is an alarm for an expired
certificate, update the certificate immediately. If the private key of a certificate is
disclosed or the trusted CA has updated the CRL, update the certificate and CRL
immediately.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossadm users of the
management node.
● The certificate request file has been generated before you apply for
certificates from the CA. For details, see 8.4.7.1.2 Generating a CSR File in
CLI Mode.
● You have applied for a new certificate from the CA and have obtained related
files and the password for the private key of the identity certificate. Table
8-60 lists the certificate requirements. For details about how to view
certificate details, see 13.12 Viewing Certificate Details.

Item Requirement
Certificate Use industry-leading security algorithms, such as
signature sha256WithRSAEncryption.
algorithm
Key length ● For keys encrypted using RSA, the recommended
length is 3072 bits or longer.
● For keys encrypted using ECDSA, the length can be
256 or 384 bits.
NOTE
RSA keys shorter than 2048 bits or ECDSA keys shorter than 224
bits are insecure. For security purposes, ensure that the private
key of the certificate is an RSA key of 3072 bits or longer or an
ECDSA key of 256 bits or longer.
Certificate Set this parameter based on the customer's IT security
validity period management requirements.
Extended The X509v3 Subject Alternative Name attribute is
certificate mandatory. The value is an IP address or a domain
attributes name. If an IP address is used to log in to the PowerEcho
or the NetEco client, set the value to the IP address. If a
floating IP address is used, set the value to the floating
IP address. If a domain name is used, set the value to the
domain name.
Password If the private key file server_key.pem is not encrypted,
complexity of you need to encrypt it by referring to 13.4 Encrypting
the private key the Private Key of the Signature Certificate (the
file PowerEcho). Ensure that its password meets the
following requirements and keep it secure:
● Contain 10 to 32 characters.
● Be a combination of the following four types of
characters:
– Uppercase letters
– Lowercase letters
– Digits
– Special characters !"#$%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?@[]^`{_|}~
● Not contain double quotation marks (") and single
quotation marks (') at the same time.
● Contain less than three consecutive identical
characters.
● Contain less than four identical characters.

Item Requirement
Certificate Base64-encoded .cer format is supported. The format
format and name of the certificate and private key files must be
consistent with the following, and only lowercase letters
are allowed in the file name:
● server.cer: identity certificate of NetEco, used to
authenticate the identity of NetEco by the peer
system.
● server_key.pem: private key of the identity certificate.
● trust.cer: certificate of the CA that issues the identity
certificate.
NOTE
● If the certificate is obtained from a local CA, download the
CA certificate chain by referring to Related Tasks.
● If the obtained certificate is in .pem, .crt, or .der format,
convert it to .cer format by referring to 13.13 Converting
the Certificate Format.
Precautions
● During the ER certificate update, the old ER certificate is automatically backed
up to the /opt/oss/manager/var/tmp/er_random code directory on the
management node, for example, /opt/oss/manager/var/tmp/er_202022222.
After the certificates are updated, the directory is automatically deleted.
● Do not modify the content or permission of files in the certificate storage
directories /opt/oss/manager/etc/ssl/er and /opt/oss/NetEco/etc/ssl/er.
Otherwise, the PowerEcho or product rollback will fail.
● During the ER certificate update of the PowerEcho, MCHIROERService is
automatically restarted for the update to take effect, and you cannot log in to
the PowerEcho during the restart. You are advised to perform this operation
during off-peak hours.
● The system automatically randomizes the password for the new certificate.
You do not need to manually change the password for the new certificate.
● After the ER certificate of the PowerEcho is updated, historical backup files of
the application and data of the PowerEcho and OS of the management node
will become invalid. Therefore, the backup files generated before the
certificate update cannot be used for restoration. Back up data in a timely
manner after the certificate is updated.
● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to 8.4.13.1 Checking
Validity of the PowerEcho Certificates.
Procedure
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the management node as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
For details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.

NO TE
● Use the fixed IP address instead of the floating IP address of the management node for
login. Otherwise, PuTTY will be disconnected, causing the certificate update to fail.
● Services need to be restarted during the certificate update. If no operation is performed
on PuTTY for a long time, PuTTY may be disconnected upon timeout, which causes the
certificate update to fail. Set the PuTTY timeout period by referring to 12.20 How Do I
Prevent PuTTY from Being Disconnected upon Timeout?
Step 2 Run the following commands to create a temporary directory for uploading the
new certificate, for example, /management1, and change the permission for the
directory:
> mkdir /management1
> chmod 750 /management1
Step 3 Use FileZilla to upload the ER certificate files to the /management1 directory on
the node described in Step 1, as the sopuser user in SFTP mode. For details, see
13.2 Transferring Files Using FileZilla.
Step 4 Use PuTTY to run the following command as the sopuser user to change the
permission for the certificate files:
> chmod 640 /management1/*
Step 5 Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
Step 6 Run the following commands to create a directory for storing the new certificate
files and change the permission for the directory:
> mkdir -p /cer/management
> chmod -R 750 /cer/management
Step 7 Run the following command to copy the certificate files to the /cer/management
directory:
> cp /management1/* /cer/management
Step 8 Run the following commands to set permission for the certificate files:
> chmod 700 /cer/management
> find /cer/management -type f | xargs chmod 600
Step 9 Run the following commands to update the ER certificate:
> cd /opt/oss/manager/apps/UniEPService/tools/common
> bash updatecertificate.sh -certtype er -certpath /tmp/cer/management
NO TE
If the ER certificate is encrypted with the ECDSA encryption algorithm, run the following
command: bash updatecertificate.sh -certtype er -certpath /tmp/cer/management -
type ecdsa

Related Tasks
Downloading the CA certificate chain
Step 1 On the NetEco, choose System > System Settings > Certificate Authority
Service from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose PKI Management > CA.
Step 3 On the CA page, click the name of the subordinate CA. Click the Associate
Certificate tab, select all certificates, and click Download in the upper right
corner to download the CA certificate chain in .pem format to your local PC.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
If you log in to the PowerEcho using a browser and a message similar to "This
Connection is Untrusted" is displayed, perform the following operations:
1. Click Export Trust Chain to export the server certificate trust chain that
contains the root certificate and intermediate certificate, split the exported file
into a root certificate and an intermediate certificate in .cer format
respectively.
2. Add the trust certificate of the ER certificate to the browser's certificates. For
details, see 8.4.10.8 Configuring Trust Certificates of Browsers.
8.4.10.4 Updating ER Certificates of the NetEco in CLI Mode
ER certificates are required for authentication during the communication with an
external environment. For example, the browser uses the ER certificate to verify
the identity of the NetEco. If you cannot log in to the PowerEcho, you can update
the ER certificate in CLI mode. Periodically check for alarms for expiring
certificates and update certificates in time to prevent system function
unavailability and ensure system security. If there is an alarm for an expired
certificate, update the certificate immediately. If the private key of a certificate is
disclosed or the trusted CA has updated the CRL, update the certificate and CRL
immediately.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossadm users of the
management node.
● The certificate request file has been generated before you apply for
certificates from the CA. For details, see 8.4.7.1.2 Generating a CSR File in
CLI Mode.
● You have applied for a new certificate from the CA and have obtained related

certificate details, see 13.12 Viewing Certificate Details.
Table 8-61 Certificate requirements
Item Requirement
Certificate signature Use industry-leading security algorithms,
algorithm such as sha256WithRSAEncryption.
Key length ● For keys encrypted using RSA, the
recommended length is 3072 bits or
longer.
● For keys encrypted using ECDSA, the
length can be 256 or 384 bits.
NOTE
RSA keys shorter than 2048 bits or ECDSA keys
shorter than 224 bits are insecure. For security
purposes, ensure that the private key of the
certificate is an RSA key of 3072 bits or longer or
an ECDSA key of 256 bits or longer.
Certificate validity period Set this parameter based on the customer's
IT security management requirements.
Extended certificate The X509v3 Subject Alternative Name
attributes attribute is mandatory. The value is an IP
address or a domain name. If an IP address
is used to log in to the PowerEcho or the
NetEco client, set the value to the IP
address. If a floating IP address is used, set
the value to the floating IP address. If a
domain name is used, set the value to the
domain name.

Item Requirement
Password complexity of the If the private key file server_key.pem is not
private key file encrypted, you need to encrypt it by
referring to 13.4 Encrypting the Private
Key of the Signature Certificate (the
PowerEcho). Ensure that its password meets
the following requirements and keep it
secure:
● Contain 10 to 32 characters.
● Be a combination of the following four
types of characters:
– Uppercase letters
– Lowercase letters
– Digits
– Special characters !"#$%&'()*
+,-./:;<=>?@[]^`{_|}~
● Not contain double quotation marks (")
and single quotation marks (') at the
same time.
● Contain less than three consecutive
identical characters.
● Contain less than four identical
characters.
Certificate storage format Base64-encoded .cer format is supported.
The format and name of the certificate and
private key files must be consistent with the
following, and only lowercase letters are
allowed in the file name:
● server.cer: identity certificate file
● server_key.pem: private key of the
identity certificate.
● trust.cer: trust certificate file
NOTE
● If the certificate is obtained from a local CA,
download the CA certificate chain by referring
to Related Tasks.
● If the obtained certificate is in .pem, .crt,
or .der format, convert it to .cer format by
referring to 13.13 Converting the Certificate
Format.

8.4.10.5 Updating Domain Name-Specific ER Certificate of the PowerEcho or
the NetEco
ER certificates are required for authentication during the communication with an
external environment. For example, the browser uses the ER certificate to
authenticate the identity of the PowerEcho or the NetEco. The system allows you
to import multiple ER certificates with different domain names. To prevent risk
messages of untrusted certificates displayed on the browser during
communication authentication, you need to import and update domain name-
specific ER certificates of the PowerEcho or the NetEco, and the domain name-
specific ER certificates are preferentially used for authentication. Periodically check
for alarms for expiring certificates and update certificates in time to prevent
system function unavailability and ensure system security. If there is an alarm for
an expired certificate, update the certificate immediately. If the private key of a
certificate is disclosed or the trusted CA has updated the CRL, update the
certificate and CRL immediately.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossadm users of the
management node.
● The certificate request file has been generated before you apply for
certificates from the CA. For details, see 8.4.7.1.2 Generating a CSR File in
CLI Mode.
● You have applied for a new certificate from the CA and have obtained related
files and the password for the private key of the identity certificate. Table
8-62 lists the certificate requirements.
Table 8-62 Certificate requirements
Item Requirement
Certificate Use industry-leading security algorithms, such as
signature sha256WithRSAEncryption.
algorithm
Key length ● For keys encrypted using RSA, the recommended
length is 3072 bits or longer.
● For keys encrypted using ECDSA, the length can be
256 or 384 bits.
NOTE
RSA keys shorter than 2048 bits or ECDSA keys shorter than 256
bits are insecure. For security purposes, ensure that the private
key of the ER certificate is an RSA key of 3072 bits or longer or

Item Requirement
Certificate Set this parameter based on the customer's IT security
validity period management requirements.
Format Base64-encoded .cer format is supported. The format and
name of the certificate and private key files must be
consistent with the following, and only lowercase letters
are allowed in the file name:
● server.cer: identity certificate of NetEco, used to
authenticate the identity of NetEco by the peer
system.
● server_key.pem: private key of the identity certificate,
which is used with its password.
● trust.cer: certificate of the CA that issues the identity
certificate.
NOTE
● If the certificate is obtained from a local CA, download the
CA certificate chain by referring to Downloading the CA
certificate chain.
● If the obtained certificate is in .pem, .crt, or .der format,
convert it to .cer format by referring to 13.13 Converting the
Certificate Format.
Common name Common name of the certificate subject. It is
(CN) recommended that the value contain a unique code that
identifies the product or non-volatile product information.
This parameter needs to be set to the domain name for
domain name-specific ER certificates.
The common name must meet the following complexity
requirements:
1. Contain 1 to 128 characters.
2. Contain only digits, uppercase letters, lowercase
letters, dots, and hyphens (-) and not start or end with
a dot (.).
3. Not contain consecutive dots.

● During the ER certificate update of the PowerEcho or the NetEco,

off-peak hours.
● Do not modify the content or permission of files in the certificate storage
directories /opt/oss/manager/etc/ssl/er and /opt/oss/NetEco/etc/ssl/er.
Otherwise, the PowerEcho or product rollback will fail.
● The system automatically randomizes the password for the new certificate.

historical backup files of the product database applications, OS of the product

● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to 8.4.13.1 Checking
Validity of the PowerEcho Certificates.

8.4.10.6 (Optional) Enabling and Disabling Forcible Authentication of the
PowerEcho Server on Digital Certificates of Browser Clients
This chapter describes how to enable the forcible authentication of the PowerEcho
server on digital certificates of browser clients to ensure the communication
security between the management system and external systems. By default, the
forcible authentication of the PowerEcho server on digital certificates of browser
clients is disabled. If you do not need to enable the forcible authentication of the
PowerEcho server on digital certificates of browser clients, skip this chapter.
Prerequisites
You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossadm users of the node
where the mchiroer process instance resides. For details about how to obtain the
IP address of a node, see 12.3 Querying the Management IP Address of the
Node Where a Process or Service Resides.
NO TE
The specifications set in the operations described in this section take effect only in the
current version. To retain the specifications after an upgrade, perform the operations again.
Procedure
Table 8-63 describes the related operations.

Operation Procedure
Enabling the 1. Use PuTTY to log in to the node where the mchiroer
forcible process instance resides, as the sopuser user in SSH
authentication of mode.
the PowerEcho
NOTE
server on digital If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, log in to the
certificates of node where the active mchiroer process instance resides and
browser clients perform the subsequent operations. Then, log in to the node
where the standby mchiroer process instance resides and
perform the subsequent operations.
2. Run the following command to switch to the ossadm
user:
su - ossadm
Password: Password for the ossadm user
3. Run the following command to switch to the
mcwebuser user:
sudo -u mcwebuser bash
4. Run the following command to switch to the directory
that stores the switch_tool script:
cd /opt/oss/manager/apps/MCHIROERService/init
5. Run the following command to import environment
variables:
source switch_tool.sh
6. Run the following command to enable the two-way
authentication between the browser and NetEco server:
switch_tool verifyClientCertificateOn
If information similar to the following is displayed, the
function is enabled successfully:
Modify verifyClientCertificateOn successful!
7. Run the following command to switch back to the
ossadm user:
exit
8. Run the following command to import environment
variables:
source /opt/oss/manager/bin/engr_profile.sh
9. Run the following command to restart the ER service of
the PowerEcho:
ipmc_adm -cmd restartapp -app MCHIROERService -
tenant manager
If information similar to the following is displayed, the
ER service of the PowerEcho is restarted successfully.
Otherwise, contact technical support.
Stopping process mchiroer-0-0 ... success
Starting process mchiroer-0-0 ... success

Follow-up Procedure
● After the forcible authentication of the PowerEcho server on digital

certificates is successful:
– On a PC with an identity certificate installed, the PowerEcho can be
accessed using a browser.
– On a PC without an identity certificate installed, the PowerEcho cannot
be accessed using a browser. The message
"ERR_BAD_SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_CERT" is displayed.
● After the forcible authentication of the PowerEcho server on digital
certificates of browser clients is disabled, restart the browser. If both of the
following conditions are met, disabling of forcible client digital certificate
authentication is successful:
On a PC without an identity certificate installed, the PowerEcho can be
accessed using a browser.
8.4.10.7 (Optional) Enabling and Disabling Forcible Authentication of the

This chapter describes how to enable the forcible authentication of the NetEco
server on digital certificates of browser clients to ensure the communication
security between the NetEco and external systems. By default, the forcible
authentication of the NetEco server on digital certificates of browser clients is
disabled. If you do not need to enable the forcible authentication, skip this
chapter.
Prerequisites

where the hiroer process instance resides. For details about how to obtain the IP
address of a node, see 12.3 Querying the Management IP Address of the Node
Where a Process or Service Resides.

8.4.10.8 Configuring Trust Certificates of Browsers
If a message is displayed on the browser, indicating that security risks exist, you
need to add the certificate to the browser trust list by following the instructions
provided in this section.
8.4.10.8.1 Adding Trust Certificates to the Trust List of Firefox
If the ER certificate of the PowerEcho or the NetEco is not in the trust list of a
browser, a message similar to "This Connection is Untrusted" will be displayed
when you log in to the PowerEcho or the NetEco by using the browser. This
section describes how to import the certificate to the browser if this problem
occurs. The GUI strings may vary depending on the browser version.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained new ER certificates or have applied for certificates from
the CA to update the certificates in the system. After the system is initially
installed, new ER certificates are dynamically generated and initial ER
certificates cannot be used.
● You have saved the trust certificate (generally in .cer format) to your local PC.
The trust certificate contains the root certificate and the intermediate
certificate. Obtain the trust certificate as follows:
a. On the PowerEcho, choose System > Certificate and Key > Certificate
Management from the main menu.
b. On the Certificate Management page, click the tile corresponding to the
ER certificate of the PowerEcho or your desired product.
c. Click Export Trust Chain to export the server certificate trust chain that
contains the root certificate and intermediate certificate.
Procedure
Step 1 In the upper right corner of the Firefox window, click .

Certificates in the Certificates area.
Step 4 In the Certificate Manager dialog box, click the Authorities tab.
Step 5 Click Import and select the root certificate.
Step 6 In the Downloading Certificate dialog box, select Trust this CA to identify
websites. Click OK.
Step 7 Repeat Step 5 to Step 6 to import the intermediate certificate.
Step 8 In the Certificate Manager dialog box, click OK.
Step 9 After the certificates are imported, if you log in to the PowerEcho or the NetEco
using the domain name, you need to configure the mapping between the domain
name and IP address by referring to 12.26 Adding Domain Name Mappings. In
other cases, skip this step.
Step 10 Restart Firefox and log in to the PowerEcho or the NetEco.
If Firefox does not display the message similar to "This Connection is Untrusted",
the trust certificate is successfully imported. Otherwise, contact technical support.
----End
8.4.10.8.2 Adding Trust Certificates to the Trust List of Chrome
If the ER certificate of the PowerEcho or the NetEco is not in the trust list of a
browser, a message indicating that the security certificate is incorrect or untrusted
will be displayed when you log in to the PowerEcho or the NetEco by using the
browser. This section describes how to import the certificate to the browser if this
problem occurs. The GUI strings may vary depending on the browser version.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained new ER certificates or have applied for certificates from
the CA to update the certificates in the system. After the system is initially
installed, new ER certificates are dynamically generated and initial ER
certificates cannot be used.
● You have saved the trust certificate (generally in .cer format) to your local PC.
The trust certificate contains the root certificate and the intermediate
certificate. Obtain the trust certificate as follows:
a. On the PowerEcho, choose System > Certificate and Key > Certificate
Management from the main menu.
b. On the Certificate Management page, click the tile corresponding to the
ER certificate of the PowerEcho or your desired product.
c. Click Export Trust Chain to export the server certificate trust chain that
contains the root certificate and intermediate certificate.
Procedure
Step 1 Double-click the obtained trust certificate on your PC.

Step 4 In the Certificate Import Wizard dialog box, select Place all certificates in the
following store and click Browse.
Step 5 In the Select Certificate Store dialog box, select Trusted Root Certification
Authorities and click OK.
Step 6 In the Certificate Import Wizard dialog box, click Next and Finish.
Step 7 When importing the root certificate, read the security warning carefully, and click
Yes.
Step 8 The Certificate Import Wizard dialog box is displayed, indicating that the file is
imported successfully. Click OK.
Step 9 In the Certificate dialog box, click OK.
Step 10 Repeat the preceding operations to import all the trust certificates.
Step 11 After the certificates are imported, if you log in to the PowerEcho or the NetEco
using the domain name, you need to configure the mapping between the domain
name and IP address by referring to 12.26 Adding Domain Name Mappings. In
other cases, skip this step.
Step 12 Restart the browser and log in to the PowerEcho or the NetEco.
If the browser does not display the message indicating that the security certificate
is incorrect or untrusted, the trust certificate is successfully imported. Otherwise,
contact technical support.
----End
8.4.10.9 Rolling Back ER Certificates That Fail to Be Updated in CLI Mode
If the ER certificate fails to be updated using the new certificate in CLI mode,
restore it from the backup certificate. Otherwise, the PowerEcho or the NetEco
may become unavailable.
Prerequisites
You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossadm users of the
management node.
Precautions
● During the ER certificate update, the system automatically backs up the old
ER certificate to the /opt/oss/manager/var/tmp/er_random code directory
on the management node, for example, /opt/oss/manager/var/tmp/
er_101011111. After the certificate is updated, the directory is automatically
deleted.
● The latest backup certificate is used for certificate restoration.
● Services are automatically restarted so that the certificate can take effect
after the restoration. You are advised to perform this operation in off-peak

Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the management node as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
NO TE
● If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, that is, there are multiple management
nodes, perform operations on Management0 or Management1. For details about how to
obtain the IP address of a node, see 12.5 Querying the Management IP Address of a
Node.
● Use the fixed IP address instead of the floating IP address of the management node for
login. Otherwise, PuTTY will be disconnected, causing the certificate update to fail.
● Services need to be restarted during the certificate update. If no operation is performed
on PuTTY for a long time, PuTTY may be disconnected upon timeout, which causes the
certificate update to fail. Set the PuTTY timeout period by referring to 12.20 How Do I
Prevent PuTTY from Being Disconnected upon Timeout?
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
Step 3 Run the following commands to restore the ER certificate:
> cd /opt/oss/manager/apps/UniEPService/tools/common
> bash restorecertificate.sh -certtype er
NO TE
If the PowerEcho is deployed in single-server mode, and the management node and
product node are the same node, replace UniEPService with UniEPLiteService. For details
about how to check that the management node and product node are the same node, see
12.11 Checking the Deployment Mode of Nodes.
Information similar to the following is displayed:
Restoring the er certificates...please waiting.
1.management
2.product name
Please select the number of the product information:
1. Enter the corresponding number based on site requirements and press Enter.
– 1: Update the ER certificate of the PowerEcho.
– 2: Update the ER certificate of the corresponding product.
The following information is displayed:
Restore the certificate will interrupt services. Are you sure you want to continue? (y/n):
2. Enter y and press Enter.
The system automatically stops the services, restores the certificate, and then
starts the services.
– If the following information is displayed, the certificate is restored
successfully:
Certificates restored successfully.
– If other information is displayed, the certificate fails to be restored.
Contact technical support.

Step 5 Log in to the PowerEcho. If the login is successful, the certificate is rolled back
successfully. Otherwise, contact technical support.
----End
8.4.11 Updating Certificates for Communications Between
NetEco and Third-Party Systems
When NetEco needs to authenticate the third-party system that communicates
with it, you need to import the trust certificate of the third-party system to
NetEco. When NetEco authentication is enabled on the third-party system, if both
systems trust the same CA, you only need to update the trust certificate and
identity certificate of each system. If the two systems trust different CAs, you need
to update the identity certificate of each system, and import the trust certificate of
the peer system for the two systems. During routine maintenance, periodically
update the certificates to prevent security risks caused by private key disclosure. To
prevent system function unavailability due to certificate expiration, periodically
check the certificate validity in certificate management or check for alarms for
expiring and expired certificates. If a certificate is about to expire, update the
certificate in time. If a certificate has expired or the private key of a certificate is
disclosed, update the certificate immediately. If the trusted CA has updated the
CRL, update the CRL in a timely manner.
8.4.11.1 Updating the PowerEcho Certificates for Communication Between
NetEco and Third-Party Systems by Uploading Certificate Files
During routine maintenance, you can use the certificate management function to
update SSO client trust certificates of the PowerEcho and syslog server trust
certificates (including their CRLs), and view details about the existing certificates,
such as the issuer and validity period. Periodically check for alarms for expiring
certificates and update certificates in time to prevent system function
unavailability and ensure system security. If there is an alarm for an expired
certificate, update the certificate immediately. If the private key of a certificate is
disclosed or the trusted CA has updated the CRL, update the certificate and CRL
immediately.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
If you fail to log in to the PowerEcho or the NetEco using a browser because
the certificate has expired, you can update the certificate in CLI mode. For
details, see 8.4.9.3.1 Updating the CA Certificate in CLI Mode, 8.4.10.3
Updating ER Certificates of the PowerEcho in CLI Mode, or 8.4.10.4
Updating ER Certificates of the NetEco in CLI Mode.
● When updating the CA certificate, you have obtained the password for the
ossadm user of the management node.
● Only the system administrator can update the CAS SSO client trust certificate

management to obtain the new certificate. For details, see 8.4.7.1.3 Binding a
CSR with a Certificate.
● You have obtained the new certificate and related files if you need to upload
certificate files to update the certificate. For details, see Table 8-65.
If the obtained certificate is in .pem, .crt, or .der format, convert it to .cer
format by referring to 13.13 Converting the Certificate Format.

Precautions
● During the update of the syslog server trust certificate or CAS SSO client trust
certificate, MCCommonService is automatically restarted for the update to
take effect, and you cannot log in to the PowerEcho during the restart. You
are advised to perform this operation during off-peak hours.
● During the update of the syslog server trust certificate, log forwarding may
fail. Since the system forwards logs every 30 seconds, the logs that failed to
be forwarded are forwarded first after the update is complete. In this case, the
update has no impact.
● If a task for updating the CA certificate has been created, the syslog server
trust certificate can be updated only after the CA certificate update task is
complete. Otherwise, the syslog server trust certificate may fail to be updated.
● The system automatically randomizes the password for the new certificate.
You do not need to manually change the password for the new certificate.
● After the update, historical backup files of the application and data of the
PowerEcho and OS of the management node will become invalid. Therefore,
the backup files generated before the update cannot be used for restoration.
Back up data in a timely manner after the certificate is updated.
● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to 8.4.13.1 Checking
Validity of the PowerEcho Certificates.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Certificate and Key > Certificate

Step 3 Set Certificate alarms based on site requirements.
● If the alarms "51020 The certificate is about to expire" and "51021 The
certificate has expired" need to be reported, toggle on Certificate alarms
(this parameter is toggled on by default). If a certificate-related alarm is
generated, you can view the alarm on the Service Monitor > Alarms >
Current Alarms page of the NetEco.
● If these alarms do not need to be reported, toggle off Certificate alarms. In
this case, you may fail to learn about certificate expiration, causing security
risks during the communication with the third-party system. Exercise caution
when performing this operation.
Step 4 Click the tab corresponding to the certificate type and configure the certificate
information according to Table 8-66.

● Click the card corresponding to the syslog server trust certificate, and Export

certificate. The server certificate trust chain can be imported to the peer
system for authentication.
8.4.11.2 Updating the PowerEcho Certificates for Communication Between
NetEco and Third-Party Systems Online
During routine maintenance, you can use the certificate management function to
update syslog server trust certificates (including their CRLs) online, and view
details about the existing certificates, such as the issuer and validity period.
Periodically check for alarms for expiring certificates and update certificates in
time to prevent system function unavailability and ensure system security. If there
is an alarm for an expired certificate, update the certificate immediately. If the
private key of a certificate is disclosed or the trusted CA has updated the CRL,
update the certificate and CRL immediately.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The PowerEcho has been interconnected with a CA server, and the CA server
configuration (such as the IP address, port number, and address information)
is consistent with the CA server from which the certificate is to be obtained.
For details, see 8.4.8.2.1 Setting CA Server Connection Parameters (the

server. For details, see 8.4.8.2.3 Setting CRL Server Connection Parameters
(the PowerEcho).
● You have obtained the information about the certificate to be updated, such
as the name of CA server interconnecting with the PowerEcho, certificate
subject information, certificate validity period, key algorithm, key length, key
usage, and extended key usage.
● Table 8-67 describes the key algorithms, key lengths, certificate types, key
usages, and extended key usages supported by certificate management.
Table 8-67 Certificate information
Parameter Description
Key algorithm Key algorithm of the certificate.
● RSA
● ECDSA
Key length ● Length of the RSA certificate key:
– 2048
– 3072
– 4096
– 8192
NOTE
RSA keys shorter than 2048 bits are insecure. For
security purposes, ensure that the private key of the
certificate is an RSA key of 3072 bits or longer.
● Length of the ECDSA certificate key:
– 256
– 384
NOTE
ECDSA keys shorter than 224 bits are insecure. For
security purposes, ensure that the private key of the
certificate is an ECDSA key of 256 bits or longer.
Certificate type Type of the certificate.
End Entity: Select this type if you do not need to
use the key corresponding to the certificate to issue
certificates.

Parameter Description
Key usage Usage of the certificate key.
● Digital signature: Select this usage if the
certificate requires identity authentication and
data integrity authentication.
● Non-repudiation: Select this usage if the
certificate requires authentication on the digital
signature of the public key to prevent the signing
entity from denying its requests.
● Key encipherment: Select this usage if the
certificate requires an encryption protocol.
● Data encipherment: Select this usage if the
certificate encrypts its application data.
● Key agreement: Select this usage if the public
key is used by the sender and receiver to encrypt
data during communication through plaintext
agreement.
● Certificate signing: Select this usage if the
public key authenticates the certificate signature.
It can only be used as a proxy certificate.
● CRL signing: Select this usage if the public key of
the certificate is used to verify the signature on
the revocation information.
● Encipher only: Select this usage if Key
agreement is selected. The public key is used
only for encrypting data during the key
agreement.
● Decipher only: Select this usage if Key
agreement is also selected. The public key is
used only for deciphering data during the key
agreement.
Extended key usage Extended usage of the certificate key.
● Server authentication: TLS WWW server
authentication. Select this usage when Digital
signature, Key encipherment, or Key
agreement in Key usage is selected.
● Client authentication: TLS WWW client
authentication. Select this usage when Digital
signature or Key agreement in Key usage is
selected.
● Email protection: Email protection. Select this
usage when the Digital signature, Non-
repudiation, Key encipherment, or Key
agreement in Key usage is selected.

● During the update of the syslog server trust certificate, MCCommonService is
automatically restarted for the update to take effect, and you cannot log in to
the PowerEcho during the restart. You are advised to perform this operation
during off-peak hours. If the PowerEcho is deployed in single-server mode,
and the management node and product node are the same node, the service
to be restarted is UniEPLiteService.
● During the update of the syslog server trust certificate, log forwarding may
fail. Since the system forwards logs every 30 seconds, the logs that failed to
be forwarded are forwarded first after the update is complete. In this case, the
update has no impact.
● If a task for updating the CA certificate has been created, the syslog server
trust certificate can be updated only after the CA certificate update task is
complete. Otherwise, the syslog server trust certificate may fail to be updated.
● The system automatically randomizes the password for the new certificate.
You do not need to manually change the password for the new certificate.
● After the update, historical backup files of the application and data of the
PowerEcho and OS of the management node will become invalid. Therefore,
the backup files generated before the update cannot be used for restoration.
Back up data in a timely manner after the certificate is updated.
● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to 8.4.13.1 Checking
Validity of the PowerEcho Certificates.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Certificate and Key > Certificate
Management from the main menu.
Step 2 Click the certificate to be updated.
Step 3 Set Certificate alarms based on site requirements.
● If the alarms "51020 The certificate is about to expire" and "51021 The
certificate has expired" need to be reported, toggle on Certificate alarms
(this parameter is toggled on by default). If a certificate-related alarm is
generated, you can view the alarm on the Service Monitor > Alarms >
Current Alarms page of the NetEco.
● If these alarms do not need to be reported, toggle off Certificate alarms. In
this case, you may fail to learn about certificate expiration, causing security
risks during the communication with the third-party system. Exercise caution
when performing this operation.
Step 4 Perform the following operations to update the identity certificate, trust
certificate, and CRL at the same time:
1. On the Identity Certificate tab page, click Update.
2. Select Online update.
3. Optional: Select the desired certificate template from the Template drop-
down list. For details about how to create a certificate template, see 8.4.8.3
(Optional) Managing Certificate Templates (the PowerEcho).

NO TE
After the CA trusted by NetEco updates the CRL, you need to update the CRL to
NetEco in a timely manner to prevent unauthorized operations by identity fraud. For
security purposes, you need to update the CRL when you update certificates.
Step 5 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Task List from the main menu to check the
execution status of the task for updating certificates.
Step 6 After the update, back up the application and data of the PowerEcho. For details,
see 10.6.1 Manually Backing Up the Application and Data of the PowerEcho.
----End
Related Tasks
● To restore the syslog server trust certificate to the last update, perform the
following steps:
Step 1 On the Certificate Management page, click the card corresponding to the syslog
server trust certificate to be restored.
Step 2 On the Identity Certificate tab page, click Restore, and perform operations as
prompted.
----End
● Click the card corresponding to the syslog server trust certificate, and Export
Trust Chain on the Identity Certificate tab page to export the server
certificate trust chain that contains the root certificate and intermediate
certificate. The server certificate trust chain can be imported to the peer
system for authentication.
8.4.11.3 Updating the LDAP Certificate on the PowerEcho
The LDAP certificate protects data security during the communication. During
routine maintenance, periodically update the certificates to prevent security risks
caused by private key disclosure. To prevent user login failures due to system
function unavailability, periodically check for alarms for expiring certificates and
update the certificates in time. If there is an alarm for an expired certificate,
update the certificate immediately. If the private key of a certificate is leaked or
the trusted CA has updated the CRL, update the certificate and CRL immediately.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The PowerEcho has been interconnected with a CA server and the CA server
configuration (such as the IP address, port number, and address information)
is consistent with the CA server from which the certificate is to be obtained, if
the certificate needs to be updated online. For details, see 8.4.8.2.1 Setting
CA Server Connection Parameters (the PowerEcho).
● The CSR has been bound with the certificate if you need to use CSR
management to obtain the new certificate. For details, see 8.4.7.1.3 Binding a

the identity certificate if you need to update the certificate by uploading
certificate files. Table 8-68 describes the certificate requirements. For details
about the certificate details, see 13.12 Viewing Certificate Details.
Table 8-68 Certificate requirements
Item Requirement
Certificate format ● If Certificate type is set to JKS,P12:
– Root certificate (trust certificate of the third-
party LDAP server): JKS (in .jks format), used
with its password
– Identity certificate of NetEco (when NetEco
authentication is enabled on the LDAP server):
PKCS12 (in .p12 format), used with its password
– CRL issued by all trusted CAs: CRL (in .crl
format). This file is used to manage untrusted
certificates, which prevents risks of access to
NetEco using an invalid identity.
● If Certificate type is set to CER,DER,PEM,PVK:
– Root certificate (trust certificate of the third-
party LDAP server): PEM (in .pem, .der, or .cer
format)
– Identity certificate of NetEco (when NetEco
authentication is enabled on the LDAP server):
PEM (in .pem, .der, or .cer format)
– Private key: PEM (in .pem format), used with its
password
– CRL issued by all trusted CAs: CRL (in .crl
format). This file is used to manage untrusted
certificates, which prevents risks of access to
NetEco using an invalid identity.
NOTE
The certificate in CER format must be encoded using Base64.
The file name extension can be .cer, .pem, or .crt.
Password ● The password must contain at least 16 characters.
complexity of the
● The password must contain at least two of the
private key file
following:
– Uppercase letters
– Lowercase letters
– Digits
– Special characters !"#$%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?
@[]^`{_|}~ and spaces

● When configuring LDAP authentication, you are advised to use a secure
connection such as TLS to ensure data security during the communication. If
Enable TLS is toggled on, you need to configure the LDAP certificates.
● In the LDAP interconnection scenario, updating the certificate affects the
interconnection and remote users cannot log in to the system during the
update. You are advised to update the certificate during off-peak hours.
● If TLS of an earlier version needs to be enabled for the TLS connection, see
7.2.7.5 Setting Service Parameters for User Management. For data security
during communication, you are advised to use TLS v1.2 or later.
● If the encryption algorithm configured in the system is not supported by the
LDAP server, you can change the encryption algorithm in the service item
Remote Authentication-LDAP Authentication in application security to
ensure successful LDAP interconnection. For details, see 8.1.2 Checking
Service Configurations.
● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to 8.4.13.1 Checking
Validity of the PowerEcho Certificates.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > Security Management > Authentication from the menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Remote Authentication.
Step 3 On the Remote Authentication page, click LDAP authentication.
Step 4 Select Enable TLS, and select the required protocol version and certificate type.
Step 5 Upload certificate files in either of the following ways to update the LDAP
certificate:
● Uploading certificate files from the local PC: Disable Apply for certificates
using CSR management.
– If Certificate type is JKS,P12, click Upload and perform operations as
prompted.
– If Certificate type is CER,DER,PEM,PVK, click Upload or Update Online
as required, and perform operations as prompted.
● If you need to apply for a new certificate using CSR management, enable
Apply for certificates using CSR management, select the CSR bound to the
certificate, and perform operations as prompted.
NO TE
If the CA has issued a CRL, you are advised to update the CRL in a timely manner to ensure
secure interconnection with the LDAP server.
Step 6 Click Test. In the Test Connection dialog box, enter the username and password
of the remote user on the LDAP server.
● If the message "Test successful." is displayed, interconnection between the
system and the LDAP server is successful.
● If the connection test fails, a failure message is displayed. Check whether the
LDAP server is correctly configured and try again until the interconnection is

Step 8 After the certificate is updated, log in to the PowerEcho as a remote user.
● If the login is successful, the certificate is updated successfully and the LDAP
interconnection is normal.
● If the login fails, the certificate fails to be updated. Rectify the fault as
prompted or contact technical support to locate the fault based on the logs.
----End
Related Tasks
To export the certificate trust chain for peer system authentication, perform the
following operations:
1. On the PowerEcho, choose System > Certificate and Key > Certificate
Management from the main menu.
2. On the Certificate Management page, click the card corresponding to the
LDAP certificate.
3. Click Export Trust Chain to export the server certificate trust chain that
contains the root certificate and intermediate certificate.
8.4.11.4 Updating the RADIUS Certificate on the PowerEcho
The RADIUS certificate protects data security during the communication. During
routine maintenance, periodically update the certificates to prevent security risks
caused by private key disclosure. To prevent user login failures due to system
function unavailability, periodically check for alarms for expiring certificates and
update the certificates in time. If there is an alarm for an expired certificate,
update the certificate immediately. If the private key of a certificate is leaked or
the trusted CA has updated the CRL, update the certificate and CRL immediately.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The PowerEcho has been interconnected with a CA server and the CA server
configuration (such as the IP address, port number, and address information)
is consistent with the CA server from which the certificate is to be obtained, if
the certificate needs to be updated online. For details, see 8.4.8.2.1 Setting
CA Server Connection Parameters (the PowerEcho).
● The CSR has been bound with the certificate if you need to use CSR
management to obtain the new certificate. For details, see 8.4.7.1.3 Binding a
CSR with a Certificate.
● You have obtained the new certificate files and the private key password for
the identity certificate if you need to update the certificate by uploading
certificate files. Table 8-69 describes the certificate application requirements.
For details about the certificate details, see 8.4.11.4 Updating the RADIUS
Certificate on the PowerEcho.

Item Requirement
Certificate format ● If Certificate type is set to JKS,P12:
– Root certificate (trust certificate of the third-
party RADIUS server): JKS (in .jks format), used
with its password
– Identity certificate of NetEco (when NetEco
authentication is enabled on the RADIUS
server): PKCS12 (in .p12 format), used with its
password
– CRL issued by all trusted CAs: CRL (in .crl
format). This file is used to manage untrusted
certificates, which prevents risks of access to
NetEco using an invalid identity.
● If Certificate type is set to CER,DER,PEM,PVK:
– Root certificate (trust certificate of the third-
party RADIUS server): PEM (in .pem, .der, or .cer
format)
– Identity certificate of NetEco (when NetEco
authentication is enabled on the RADIUS
server): PEM (in .pem, .der, or .cer format)
– Private key: PEM (in .pem format), used with its
password
– CRL issued by all trusted CAs: CRL (in .crl
format). This file is used to manage untrusted
certificates, which prevents risks of access to
NetEco using an invalid identity.
NOTE
The certificate in CER format must be encoded using Base64.
The file name extension can be .cer, .pem, or .crt.
Password ● The password must contain at least 16 characters.
complexity of the
● The password must contain at least two of the
private key file
following:
– Uppercase letters
– Lowercase letters
– Digits
– Special characters !"#$%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?
@[]^`{_|}~ and spaces
Precautions
● When configuring RADIUS authentication, you are advised to use a secure
connection such as TLS to ensure data security during the communication. If
Enable TLS is turned on, you need to configure the RADIUS certificate.

interconnection and remote users cannot log in to the system during the
update. You are advised to update the certificate during off-peak hours.
● If the encryption algorithm configured in the system is not supported by the
RADIUS server, you can change the encryption algorithm in the service item
Remote Authentication-RADIUS Authentication in application security to
ensure successful RADIUS interconnection. For details, see 8.1.2 Checking
Service Configurations.
● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to 8.4.13.2 Checking
Validity of the NetEco Certificates.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > Security Management > Authentication from the menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Remote Authentication.
Step 3 On the Remote Authentication page, click RADIUS Authentication.
Step 4 Select Enable TLS, and select the required protocol version and certificate type.
Step 5 Upload certificate files in either of the following ways to update the RADIUS
certificate:
● Uploading certificate files from the local PC: Disable Apply for certificates
using CSR management.
– If Certificate type is JKS,P12, click Upload and perform operations as
prompted.
– If Certificate type is CER,DER,PEM,PVK, click Upload or Update Online
as required, and perform operations as prompted.
● If you need to apply for a new certificate using CSR management, enable
Apply for certificates using CSR management, select the CSR bound to the
certificate, and perform operations as prompted.
NO TE
If the CA has issued a CRL, you are advised to update the CRL in a timely manner to ensure
secure interconnection with the RADIUS server.
Step 6 Click Test. In the Test Connection dialog box, enter the username and password
of the remote user on the RADIUS server.
● If "Test successful." is displayed, the interconnection between the system and
the RADIUS server is successful.
● If the test fails, a failure message is displayed. Check whether the RADIUS
server is correctly configured and try again until the interconnection is
successful.
Step 7 Click Apply for the RADIUS authentication settings to take effect.
Step 8 After the certificate is updated, log in to the PowerEcho as a remote user.
● If the login is successful, the certificate is updated successfully and the

prompted or contact technical support to locate the fault based on the logs.
----End
Related Tasks
To export the certificate trust chain for peer system authentication, perform the
following operations:
1. On the PowerEcho, choose System > Certificate and Key > Certificate
Management from the main menu.
2. On the Certificate Management page, click the card corresponding to the
RADIUS certificate.
3. Click Export Trust Chain to export the server certificate trust chain that
contains the root certificate and intermediate certificate.
8.4.11.5 Updating Certificates for Communication Between NetEco and
Third-Party Systems by Uploading Certificate Files (the NetEco)
The system provides the certificate management function on the web client. You
can register the certificates of the NetEco services to update these certificates in
GUI mode. Currently, certificate management supports the update of certificates
and CRLs of some services in GUI mode. During routine maintenance, periodically
update the certificates to prevent security risks caused by private key leakage. To
prevent system function unavailability due to certificate expiration, periodically
check the certificate validity in certificate management or check for alarms for
expiring and expired certificates. If a certificate is about to expire, update the
certificate in time. If a certificate has expired or the private key of a certificate is
leaked, update the certificate immediately. If the trusted CA has updated the CRL,
update the CRL in a timely manner.
For details about the supported services, see the list of service certificates
supported by certificate management.
Prerequisites
● You have the Query Certificates, Import Certificates, and Delete
Certificates permissions.
● SnbCertMgmt has been deployed, and the certificate management function is
available.
● You have obtained the new certificates and related information. For details,
see the list of service certificates supported by certificate management. You
can obtain the certificates by referring to 8.4.4 Process of Uploading and
Updating Certificates.
