Precautions
● When you update a certificate (identity certificate, trust certificate, or CRL),
the corresponding services will load the certificate. During the loading, the
corresponding functions may be unavailable for a short period of time.
Therefore, you are advised to perform this operation in off-peak hours.
● You can check the validity period of a certificate by referring to 8.4.13.2
Checking Validity of the NetEco Certificates.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Service Certificate Management.
Step 3 On the Services page, click the service with certificates to be imported.
Step 4 Configure certificate information based on the certificate type.
1. On the Identity Certificates tab page, click Import.
2. On the Import Identity Certificate page, set related parameters, and click
Submit.

NO TE
Identity certificates include the Certificate library type and Independent public and
private keys type. The certificate type varies depending on the service requirements.
– Certificate library: The public key, private key, and certificate chain are in the
same keystore file. When importing the certificate, you only need to import one
file.
– Independent public and private keys: The public key and private key are separate
files.
Table 8-71 Parameters for the identity certificate of the certificate library type
Parameter Description
Certificate alias Customized certificate name, which
is used to classify or identify the
imported certificate.
Certificate format Certificate file format.
Certificate file Certificate to be imported.
Certificate password File password set when the
certificate of the certificate library
type is generated.
Table 8-72 Parameters for the identity certificate of the independent public
and private keys type
Parameter Description
Certificate alias Customized certificate name, which
is used to classify or identify the
imported certificate.
Public key file Public key file to be imported.
Private key file Private key file to be imported.
Private key password Password set when the private key
file is generated.
Certificate chain Trust certificate chain corresponding
to the identity certificate.
3. On the Trust Certificates tab page, click Import. On the Import Trust
Certificate page, set related parameters, and click Submit.
4. On the Certificate Revocation Lists tab page, click Import. On the Import
Certificate Revocation List page, set related parameters, and click Submit.

NO TE
– By default, Enable CRL check is enabled on the Certificate Management >
Settings > General Configurations page. If Enable CRL check is disabled, you do
not need to import the CRL.
– The total number of identity certificates, trust certificates, and CRLs imported for a
service cannot exceed 200.
Step 5 In the High Risk dialog box, read the information carefully and confirm whether
to import the certificate.
● If so, select I understand the risk and want to continue, click OK, and go to
Step 6.
● If not, click Cancel.
NO TE
When an identity certificate is imported, the message displayed contains the
parameter comparison between the old and new certificates.
Step 6 In the dialog box that is displayed, click OK.
----End
8.4.11.6 Updating Certificates for Communication Between NetEco and
Third-Party Systems Online (the NetEco)
You can quickly apply for a certificate using an existing certificate template, or
apply for certificate by manually configuring certificate information. You can then
apply the obtained certificate to each service. For a service that has an identity
certificate, you can quickly update its identity certificate using wizard-based
update. During routine maintenance, periodically update the certificates to
prevent security risks caused by private key leakage. To prevent system function
unavailability due to certificate expiration, periodically check the certificate validity
in certificate management or check for alarms for expiring and expired certificates.
If the private key is leaked or a certificate has expired, update the certificate
immediately. If a certificate is about to expire, update the certificate in time.
Prerequisites
● The interconnection with the CA server has been configured.
● You have the Query Certificate Application Task and Manage Certificate
Application Task permissions.
● You have obtained the information about the certificate, such as the
certificate format, common name (CN), country or region (C), province or
state (ST), city (L), organization (O), department (OU), email (E), validity
period, key algorithm, key length, subject alternative name (SAN), certificate
type, key usage, and extended key usage.
Context
● The time difference between NetEco and the CA server must be less than 5
minutes. Otherwise, the certificate application fails.
● The validity period of a certificate applied from the CA is jointly determined

application template supported by the CA. Generally, the validity period does
not exceed the maximum validity period supported by the CA and the validity
period of the application template supported by the CA. The actual validity
period depends on the interconnected CA.
● Table 8-73 describes the certificate formats, key algorithms, key lengths,
certificate types, key usages, and extended key usages supported by certificate
management.
● When you update a certificate (identity certificate or trust certificate), the
corresponding services will load the certificate. During the loading, the
corresponding functions may be unavailable for a short period of time.
Therefore, you are advised to perform this operation in off-peak hours.
● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to "Checking Validity of
the NetEco Certificates".
Table 8-73 Certificate information
Parameter Description
Certificate Format of the certificate.
format
● PEM
● PKCS12
Key Key algorithm of the certificate.
algorithm
● RSA
● ECDSA
Key length ● Length of the RSA certificate key:
– 8192
– 6144
– 4096
– 3072
– 2048
NOTE
RSA whose length is shorter than 2,048 bits is insecure. You are
advised to use the certificate encrypted by RSA whose length is
3,072 bits or longer.
● Length of the ECDSA certificate key:
– 521
– 384
– 256
Certificate Type of the certificate.
type
● End Entity: Select this type if you do not need to use the
key corresponding to the certificate to issue certificates.
● CA: Select this type when you need to use the
corresponding key to issue certificates.

Parameter Description
Key usage Usage of the certificate key.
● Digital signature: Select this usage if the certificate
requires identity authentication and data integrity
authentication.
● Non-repudiation: Select this usage if the certificate
requires authentication on the digital signature of the
public key to prevent the signing entity from denying its
requests.
● Key encipherment: Select this usage if the certificate
requires an encryption protocol.
● Data encipherment: Select this usage if the certificate
encrypts its application data.
● Key agreement: Select this usage if the public key is used
by the sender and receiver to encrypt data during
communication through plaintext agreement.
● Certificate signing: Select this usage if the public key
authenticates the certificate signature. It can only be used
as a proxy certificate.
● CRL signing: Select this usage if the public key of the
certificate is used to verify the signature on the revocation
information.
● Encipher only: Select this usage if Key agreement is
selected. The public key is used only for encrypting data
during the key agreement.
● Decipher only: Select this usage if Key agreement is
selected. The public key is used only for deciphering data
during the key agreement.
Extended Extended usage of the certificate key.
key usage
● Server authentication: TLS WWW server authentication.
Select this usage when Digital signature, Key
encipherment, or Key agreement in Key usage is
selected.
● Client authentication: TLS WWW client authentication.
Select this usage when Digital signature or Key
agreement in Key usage is selected.
● Email protection: Email protection. Select this usage when
the Digital signature, Non-repudiation, Key
encipherment, or Key agreement in Key usage is
selected.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.

requirements.
NO TE
● Online Application: You can quickly apply for a certificate using an existing certificate
template, or apply for a certificate (identity certificate or trust certificate) by manually
configuring certificate information.
● Wizard-based Update: You can quickly update the identity certificates for the services
that already have an identity certificate.

Mode Procedure
Online 1. Click Online Application.
applicatio
2. Select an online certificate update mode based on site
n
requirements.
– Updating using an existing template
a. (Optional) Create a certificate application template. For
details, see 8.4.8.4 (Optional) Managing Certificate
Templates (the NetEco).
b. Select a template from the Template drop-down list.
c. Select a CA interconnection configuration from the CA
Server drop-down list.
d. Set parameters as prompted based on site requirements.
NOTE
After a certificate application template is selected, you can change
the values of some parameters as required. For example, if the
validity period set in the certificate application template is 5 years,
but the required validity period of an online certificate application
task is 10 years, you can set the validity period to 10 years for the
task.
– Updating without a template
Set the parameters of the online certificate application task
as prompted based on site requirements.
3. Click OK.
4. In the list of online certificate update tasks, check Task Status
of the created task.
– Pending: The online certificate application task is applying
for a new certificate. Refresh the page and check the task
status.
– Successful: The online certificate application task is created
successfully.
– Failed: The online certificate application task fails to be
created. Rectify the fault based on the failure causes. Click
in the Operation column. In the dialog box that is displayed,
click Details and rectify the fault based on the details. If the
problem persists, contact technical support.
5. To apply the obtained certificate to services, perform Step 3.5.a
to Step 3.5.c.
a. Click in the Operation column of the row that contains
the certificate.
b. Select the services to which you want to apply the certificate.
c. Click OK.
d. In the High Risk dialog box, read the information carefully
and confirm whether to apply certificate to services.

Mode Procedure
▪
If so, select I understand the risk and want to continue,
click OK, and go to Step 3.5.e.
▪
If not, click Cancel.
e. Check whether the certificate is successfully applied to the
corresponding services.
If the message "Certificates applied to services successfully."
is displayed, the certificate is successfully applied to the
service. Click OK. Otherwise, handle the problem according to
the information in the dialog box.
NOTE
When a certificate is applied to services, the identity certificate and
certificate chain of the certificate are applied to the services.

Mode Procedure
Wizard- 1. Click Wizard-based Update.
based
2. Enter the task name and click OK.
update
3. Select the services whose certificate need to be updated and
click Next.
NOTE
In the service list, the current identity certificates of the services whose
Source is empty are not obtained through online application.
4. (Optional) Set the certificate validity period as required. The
default value is 50 years.
5. (Optional) Select services as required and click Configure CA
Server. In the dialog box that is displayed, configure the CA
server interconnected with the certificates of the selected
services.
6. (Optional) Click Modify in the Operation column of the row
that contains the corresponding service. On the page that is
displayed, set certificate parameters as required and click OK.
7. Click Finish.
8. In the High Risk dialog box, read the information carefully and
confirm whether to update the certificates of the services.
– If so, select I understand the risk and want to continue,
and click OK.
– If not, click Cancel.
9. In the list of online certificate update tasks, check Task Status
of the created task.
– Pending: The wizard-based certificate update task is applying
for a new certificate. Refresh the page and check the task
status.
– Successful: The certificates of all services are successfully
updated.
– Partially successful: The certificates of some services are
successfully updated.
– Failed: The certificates of all services fail to be updated.
NOTE
If the task status is Partially successful or Failed, click in the
Operation column of row that contains the corresponding task. In the
dialog box that is displayed, click Details and rectify the fault based on
the information. If the fault persists, contact technical support.
----End
Related Tasks
● Deleting an online certificate update task
Click in the Operation column of the row that contains the desired online

NO TE
If the certificate applied for in a task has been applied to a service, the task cannot be
deleted.
● Restoring a historical certificate
If a certificate fails to be updated, is updated to an incorrect certificate, or is
deleted by mistake, you can perform the following operations to restore the
certificate:
a. In the navigation pane, choose Service Certificate Management.
b. On the Services page, click the service with a certificate to be restored.
c. Click the Identity Certificates tab.
d. Click Historical Certificates.
e. On the Historical Certificates page, click in the Operation column of
the row that contains the desired certificate.
8.4.11.7 Updating Certificates for Communication Between NetEco and
Third-Party Systems Using CSR Management (the NetEco)
You can use the CSR management function to create a CSR, export the CSR file,
and submit the CSR to the CA to apply for a public key and certificate chain. After
applying for the public key and certificate chain, you can use the CSR
management function to bind the public key and certificate chain to the CSR and
private key in the database to form a complete identity certificate. After the
binding, the certificate can be quickly applied to services. During routine
maintenance, periodically update the certificates to prevent security risks caused
by certificate expiration and private key leakage. To prevent system function
unavailability due to certificate expiration, periodically check the certificate validity
in certificate management or check for alarms for expiring and expired certificates.
If the private key is leaked or a certificate has expired, update the certificate
immediately. If a certificate is about to expire, update the certificate in time.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the information about the CSR to be created, such as the
common name (CN), country or region (C), province or state (ST), city (L),
organization (O), department (OU), email (E), key algorithm, key length,
subject alternative name, certificate type, key usage, and extended key usage.
● Before updating a certificate, you have applied for and obtained the public
key and certificate chain from the CA.
Context
● Table 8-75 describes the key algorithms, key lengths, certificate types, key
usages, and extended key usages supported by certificate management.
● When you update a certificate (identity certificate), the corresponding services
will load the certificate. During the loading, the corresponding functions may
be unavailable for a short period of time. Therefore, you are advised to
perform this operation in off-peak hours.
● You can check the validity of a certificate by referring to "Checking Validity of

Parameter Description
Key Key algorithm of the certificate.
algorithm
● RSA
● ECDSA
Key length ● Length of the RSA certificate key:
– 8192
– 6144
– 4096
– 3072
– 2048
NOTE
RSA whose length is shorter than 2,048 bits is insecure. You are
advised to use the certificate encrypted by RSA whose length is
3,072 bits or longer.
● Length of the ECDSA certificate key:
– 521
– 384
– 256
Certificate Type of the certificate.
type
● End Entity: Select this type if you do not need to use the
key corresponding to the certificate to issue certificates.
● CA: Select this type when you need to use the
corresponding key to issue certificates.

Parameter Description
Key usage Usage of the certificate key.
● Digital signature: Select this usage if the certificate
requires identity authentication and data integrity
authentication.
● Non-repudiation: Select this usage if the certificate
requires authentication on the digital signature of the
public key to prevent the signing entity from denying its
requests.
● Key encipherment: Select this usage if the certificate
requires an encryption protocol.
● Data encipherment: Select this usage if the certificate
encrypts its application data.
● Key agreement: Select this usage if the public key is used
by the sender and receiver to encrypt data during
communication through plaintext agreement.
● Certificate signing: Select this usage if the public key
authenticates the certificate signature. It can only be used
as a proxy certificate.
● CRL signing: Select this usage if the public key of the
certificate is used to verify the signature on the revocation
information.
● Encipher only: Select this usage if Key agreement is
selected. The public key is used only for encrypting data
during the key agreement.
● Decipher only: Select this usage if Key agreement is
selected. The public key is used only for deciphering data
during the key agreement.
Extended Extended usage of the certificate key.
key usage
● Server authentication: TLS WWW server authentication.
Select this usage when Digital signature, Key
encipherment, or Key agreement in Key usage is
selected.
● Client authentication: TLS WWW client authentication.
Select this usage when Digital signature or Key
agreement in Key usage is selected.
● Email protection: Email protection. Select this usage when
the Digital signature, Non-repudiation, Key
encipherment, or Key agreement in Key usage is
selected.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.

1. Click Create CSR.
2. Select a CSR creation mode based on site requirements.
– Creating a CSR by selecting an existing template
i. (Optional) Create a certificate application template. For details, see
8.4.8.4 (Optional) Managing Certificate Templates (the NetEco).
ii. Select a template from the Template drop-down list.
iii. Set parameters as prompted based on site requirements.
NO TE
After using an existing certificate application template, you can also modify
some parameters as required.
– Creating a CSR directly
Set parameters as prompted based on site requirements.
3. Click OK.
Step 4 Export the CSR file and apply for a public key and certificate chain from the CA.
1. Click in the Operation column of the row that contains the CSR to export
the CSR as a file. The CSR file is in .csr format.
NO TE
In the CSR management list, you can view the status of the created CSRs.
– Not exported: CSRs in the Not exported state can be modified, exported, and
deleted.
– To be bound: CSRs in the To be bound state can be modified, exported, bound,
and deleted.
– Bound: CSRs in the Bound state can be deleted and the corresponding certificate
can be applied to services.
2. Submit the CSR file to the CA to apply for a public key and certificate chain.
Step 5 Bind the obtained public key and certificate chain to the CSR and private key in
the database.
1. Click in the Operation column of the row that contains the desired CSR.
2. Enter certificate information and import the public key and certificate chain.
3. Click Submit.
4. In the dialog box, read the information carefully and confirm whether to bind
the certificate.
– If so, click OK. Go to Step 6.
– If not, click Cancel.
Step 6 Apply the certificate bound with the CSR to services.
1. Click in the Operation column of the row that contains the desired CSR.
2. Select the services to which you want to apply the certificate.
3. Click OK.
4. In the High Risk dialog box, read the information carefully and confirm

go to Step 6.5.
– If not, click Cancel.
5. Check whether the certificate is successfully applied to the corresponding
services.
If the message "Certificates applied to services successfully." is displayed, the
certificate is successfully applied to the service. Click OK. Otherwise, handle
the problem according to the information in the dialog box.
NO TE
When a certificate is applied to services, the identity certificate and certificate chain of
the certificate are applied to the services.
----End
Related Tasks
● Modifying a CSR
To modify the information about an existing CSR, click in the Operation
column of the CSR on the CSR Management page.
NO TE
A CSR that has been bound to a certificate cannot be modified.
● Deleting a CSR
On the CSR Management page, click in the Operation column of the
desired CSR to delete it.
NO TE
CSRs corresponding to certificates that have been applied to services cannot be
deleted. To delete such a CSR, delete the certificate of the corresponding services first.
8.4.11.8 Updating the SMS Server Certificate for Notifications (the NetEco)
In a scenario where you need to send notifications by SMS message and the SMS
server requires secure connections, the SMS server certificate and CRL are
configured. After the certificate is configured, periodically check whether there are
notifications service certificate alarms related to the SMS server. If there is an
alarm indicating that the SMS server certificate is about to expire, update the
certificate in time and delete the certificate that is about to expire. If there is an
alarm indicating that the SMS server certificate has expired, update the certificate
immediately and delete the certificate that has expired. If the trusted CA has
updated the CRL, update the SMS server certificate and CRL in a timely manner.
Prerequisites
● You have the SMS Settings permission.
● You have the Query Certificates, Import Certificates, and Delete
Certificates permissions.
● SnbCertMgmt has been deployed, and the certificate management function is

communicates with the SMS server. For details, see 9.4.1.1 Checking the
Connectivity of the SMS Server.
Context
● To send notifications to relevant personnel, you need to enter their personal
data, such as mobile numbers and email addresses. You are obligated to take
considerable measures, in compliance with the laws of the countries or
regions concerned and the user privacy policies of your company, to ensure
that users' personal data is fully protected.
● For security purposes, personal data such as mobile numbers and email
addresses are anonymized on the GUI and encrypted during transmission.
● For data security purposes, the notifications service supports certificates
signed using the SHA256withRSA, SHA384withRSA, or SHA512withRSA
algorithm.
● You can check for notifications service certificate alarms related to the SMS
server by referring to 8.4.13 Checking Certificate Validity.
Procedure
Step 1 Obtain the SMS server certificate and CRL according to Table 8-76.

Scenario Operation
Obtaining the 1. Obtain the latest SMS server certificate from the SMS
certificate server and save it to your local PC.
2. Press win+R to open the Run dialog box. Enter CMD
and click OK.
3. Run the following command to go to the keytool
directory:
cd /d keytool directory
NOTE
keytool is a JRE command. You need to download the JRE
installation package of the OpenJDK 8 (LTS) version from the
official website (https://adoptopenjdk.net/releases.html),
decompress the package, and install it. Generally, keytool is
located in the JRE installation path\bin directory.
4. Run the following command to convert the certificate
format and encrypt the keystore:
keytool -importcert -file path for saving the original
certificate\name of the original certificate -keystore
path for saving the converted certificate\name of the
converted certificate
Enter keystore Password:
Reenter New Password:
The certificate is converted to a .keystore file. Remember
the certificate password, which will be used when you
import the certificate.
NOTE
You need to set the keystore password. For security purposes, it
is recommended that the keystore password meet the following
complexity requirements:
– Contain 8 to 32 characters.
– Not contain the username or the reverse of the username.
– Contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter,
and one digit.
– Contain at least one of special characters !"#$%&'()*
+,-./:;<=>?@[\]^`{_|}~ and spaces.
Obtaining the CRL Obtain the latest CRL from the certificate authority (CA)
that issues the SMS server certificate and save it to the
local PC.
Step 2 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.
Step 3 In the navigation pane, choose Service Certificate Management.
Step 4 On the Services page, click the service with certificates to be imported.
Step 5 Configure certificate information based on the certificate type.
1. On the Trust Certificates tab page, click Import. On the Import Trust

Certificate Revocation List page, set related parameters, and click Submit.
NO TE
– By default, Enable CRL check is enabled on the Certificate Management >
Settings > General Configurations page. If Enable CRL check is disabled, you do
not need to import the CRL.
– The total number of identity certificates, trust certificates, and CRLs imported for a
service cannot exceed 200.
Step 6 In the High Risk dialog box, read the information carefully and confirm whether
to import the certificate.
● If so, select I understand the risk and want to continue, click OK, and go to
Step 7.
● If not, click Cancel.
NO TE
When an identity certificate is imported, the message displayed contains the
parameter comparison between the old and new certificates.
Step 7 In the dialog box that is displayed, click OK.
----End
8.4.11.9 Updating the Email Server Certificate for Notifications (the NetEco)
In a scenario where you need to send notifications by email and the SMTP server
requires secure connections, the email server certificate and CRL are configured.
After the certificate is configured, periodically check whether there are
notifications service certificate alarms related to the email server. If there is an
alarm indicating that the email server certificate is about to expire, update the
certificate in time and delete the certificate that is about to expire. If there is an
alarm indicating that the email server certificate has expired, update the
certificate immediately and delete the certificate that has expired. If the trusted
CA has updated the CRL, update the email server certificate and CRL in a timely
manner.
Prerequisites
● You have the Email Server Settings permission.
● You have the Query Certificates, Import Certificates, and Delete
Certificates permissions.
● SnbCertMgmt has been deployed, and the certificate management function is
available.
● The interconnected email server supports Simple Mail Transfer Protocol
(SMTP) and the SMTP port is available.
– In common connection mode, the port 25 is used.
– In STARTTLS connection mode, the port 587 is used.
– In SSL or TLS connection mode, the port 465 is used.
● The node where the campmessagingservice process resides properly
communicates with the email server. For details, see 9.4.3.1 Checking the

● For data security purpose, you are advised to use the default TLS version
when configuring the email server.
● To send notifications to relevant personnel, you need to enter their personal
data, such as mobile numbers and email addresses. You are obligated to take
considerable measures, in compliance with the laws of the countries or
regions concerned and the user privacy policies of your company, to ensure
that users' personal data is fully protected.
● For security purposes, personal data such as mobile numbers and email
addresses are anonymized on the GUI and encrypted during transmission.
● For data security purposes, the notifications service supports certificates
signed using the SHA256withRSA, SHA384withRSA, or SHA512withRSA
algorithm.
● You can check for notifications service certificate alarms related to the email
server by referring to 8.4.13 Checking Certificate Validity.
Procedure
Step 1 Obtain the email server certificate and CRL according to Table 8-77.

Scenario Operation
Converting the 1. Obtain the SSL/TLS certificate (that is, the trust
certificate format certificate with the file name extension .cer) of the
email server from the organization that issues the email
server certificate, and save the certificate to your local
PC.
2. Press win+R to open the Run dialog box. Enter CMD
and click OK.
3. Run the following command to go to the keytool
directory:
cd /d keytool directory
NOTE
keytool is a JRE command. You need to download the JRE
installation package of the OpenJDK 8 (LTS) version from the
official website (https://adoptopenjdk.net/releases.html),
decompress the package, and install it. Generally, keytool is
located in the JRE installation path\bin directory.
4. Run the following command to convert the certificate
format and encrypt the keystore:
keytool -importcert -file path for saving the original
certificate\name of the original certificate -keystore
path for saving the converted certificate\name of the
converted certificate
Enter keystore Password:
Reenter New Password:
The certificate is converted to a .keystore file. Remember
the certificate password, which will be used when you
import the certificate.
NOTE
The user-defined keystore password must contain 8 to 32
characters. For security purposes, it is recommended that the
keystore password meet the following complexity requirements:
– Not contain the username or the reverse of the username.
– Contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter,
and one digit.
– Contain at least one of special characters !"#$%&'()*
+,-./:;<=>?@[\]^`{_|}~ and spaces.
Obtaining the CRL Obtain the latest CRL from the CA that issues the email
server certificate and save it to the local PC.
Step 2 Choose System > About > Certificate Management from the main menu.
Step 3 In the navigation pane, choose Service Certificate Management.
Step 4 On the Services page, click the service with certificates to be imported.

1. On the Trust Certificates tab page, click Import. On the Import Trust

Certificate Revocation List page, set related parameters, and click Submit.
NO TE
– By default, Enable CRL check is enabled on the Certificate Management >

not need to import the CRL.
– The total number of identity certificates, trust certificates, and CRLs imported for a
service cannot exceed 200.
Step 6 In the High Risk dialog box, read the information carefully and confirm whether
to import the certificate.
● If so, select I understand the risk and want to continue, click OK, and go to
Step 7.
● If not, click Cancel.

8.4.12 Updating the Certificate Issued by NEPL
The certificate issued by NEPL in earlier versions is about to expire and needs to
be replaced.
Prerequisites
● All devices connected to the current NetEco support verification of certificates
issued by Huawei Network Energy Product CA.
● You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossuser users of the
management node.
Procedure
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the management node as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
For details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the ossuser user:
su ossuser
Password: password for the ossuser user
Step 3 Back up the configuration file and certificate file.
CA UTION
Each service updates all NEPL certificates at a time. Back up the configuration file
before the update, so that the certificate can be restored.

MqttBrokerService-South2-RSA NetEcoMqttBrokerService
Step 5 Run the following command to view the update result:
cd/opt/oss/log/NetEco/Microservice name
cat upgrade_nepl_certs.log
If the system update log contains the following information, the update is
successful.
certificate upgrade handle start ...
certificate upgrade handle success.

1. On the main menu of PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Operation and
Maintenance Management > Panoramic Monitor.
2. In the navigation pane, choose Node Monitoring.
3. In the upper left corner of the page, select NetEco.
4. On the Service tab page, restart the corresponding microservice as prompted.
----End

[Certificate file name] [-storepass password]

[Certificate file name] -alias [Alias name]

press Enter, confirm the new password, and press Enter.

[Certificate file name]
Enter the old password as prompted, press Enter, enter the new password,
press Enter, confirm the new password, and press Enter.

c. In the upper left corner of the page, select NetEco.

prompted.
8.4.13 Checking Certificate Validity
During routine maintenance, you are advised to periodically check the certificate
validity to prevent system function unavailability due to certificate expiration and
ensure system security. You can view the certificate validity in certificate
management or check for alarms for expiring and expired certificates on the
Current Alarms page.
8.4.13.1 Checking Validity of the PowerEcho Certificates
During routine maintenance, you are advised to periodically check the certificate
validity to prevent system function unavailability due to certificate expiration and
ensure system security. You can view the certificate validity in certificate
management or check for alarms for expiring and expired certificates on the
Current Alarms page.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho as the system administrator user. For
details, see 1.2 Logging In to the PowerEcho.
● You have logged in to the NetEco as the system administrator user. For
details, see 1.1 Logging In to the NetEco.

depending on certificates. For details, see Table 8-83.

8.5 Managing Logs
This section describes how to manage logs, including log forwarding settings, log
dump settings, log specifications for log forwarding, log forwarding packet
reference, and log reference.
8.5.1 Configuring Log Forwarding (the PowerEcho)
During the routine operation and maintenance, a large number of logs are
generated. To ensure sufficient space for storing new logs, the historical logs are
cleared with the generation of new logs. The log forwarding function allows the
system to automatically forward security logs, operation logs, and system logs to

● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● You have imported the syslog server trust certificate if TLS is selected as the
transfer protocol. For details, see 8.4.11.1 Updating the PowerEcho
Certificates for Communication Between NetEco and Third-Party Systems
by Uploading Certificate Files.
● You have obtained the IP addresses, port numbers, transfer protocols, and
syslog levels of the active and standby syslog servers to be interconnected.
Context
Figure 8-7 shows the principles of log forwarding settings.
Figure 8-7 Principles of log forwarding settings
The log forwarding settings feature converts logs in the log management
database and log forwarding database into syslog packets and forwards them to
the syslog server.
● If UDP is used, the PowerEcho forwards syslog packets only to the active
syslog server. Therefore, no standby syslog server is required.
● If TLS or TCP is used, the system attempts to connect to the active syslog
server in each period. If the connection fails, it attempts to connect to the
standby syslog server. The following cases may occur:
– If the PowerEcho is successfully connected to the active syslog server, logs
are forwarded only to the active server.
– If the PowerEcho fails to connect to the active syslog server, it attempts
to connect to the standby syslog server. If the connection is successful,
the PowerEcho forwards logs only to the standby syslog server. The alarm

will not forward logs but it reports the alarm "Alarm of the Failure to
Connect the Master and Standby Syslog Servers".
NO TE
● For security purposes, the system uses TLSv1.3 or later by default.
● To ensure successful communication between the system and syslog servers, clear the
alarms in a timely manner.
● For details about the syslog packet structure, see 8.5.5 Log Forwarding Packets.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Log Management > Log Forwarding
Settings from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Forwarding Server.
Step 3 On the Forwarding Server page, click Create.
Step 4 Set syslog server parameters. For details about some of the parameters, see Table
8-86.
Table 8-86 Syslog server parameters
Parameter Description
Active server IP address IP address of the active syslog server that
receives logs forwarded by the PowerEcho.
Active server port Port number of the active syslog server that
receives forwarded logs.
The value range is 1 to 65535.
Standby server IP address (Optional) IP address of the standby server.
The standby server is a backup of the active
syslog server.
Standby server port The value range is 1 to 65535.

Parameter Description
Transfer protocol TLS, UDP, and TCP are supported.
● UDP cannot ensure transfer reliability.
Connection Status in the forwarding server
list indicates whether logs can be
successfully sent out as syslog packets. It
does not mean that packets can successfully
arrive at syslog servers.
● TLS is recommended, because it is more
secure than UDP and TCP. If TLS is selected,
the log forwarding function encrypts logs
before forwarding them to a syslog server.
● If TCP or TLS is selected, the log forwarding
function sends syslog packets to the active
server first. If the sending fails, the log
forwarding function sends syslog packets to
the standby server. After the active server is
restored, logs are sent to the active server. If
UDP is selected, syslog packets are sent only
to the active server. If TLS is selected, the
trust certificate of the log forwarding server
must be imported.
NOTE
For security purposes, the system uses TLSv1.3 by
default.
Syslog protocol The headers of the forwarded log packets vary
with syslog protocol. The following uses
Timestamp (log operation time) as an
example:
● RFC 5424: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX,
for example,
2020-03-10T17:49:33.000+09:00
● RFC 3164: MM dd HH:mm:ss, for example,
Aug 9 10:47:47
Syslog level Select all levels of logs of different types to be
forwarded.
Required string Only the logs that contain the string are
forwarded.
● Regular expressions are not supported.
● Wildcard characters are not supported.
Syslog server time zone You can set this parameter based on the time
zone where the syslog server is located.
After the setting, the time in the forwarded
syslog packets is automatically converted to
the time of the time zone where the syslog
server is located.

Parameter Description
Enable DST When DST is used in the region where the
syslog server is located, you can enable the
DST.
After the setting, when the time zone where
the syslog server is located is in the DST
period, the forwarded syslog packets contain
DST identifiers.
Encoding format You can set the encoding format of forwarded
syslog packets as required. UTF-8 and GBK are
supported. The default encoding format is
UTF-8.
After this parameter is set, the syslog packets
are forwarded in the selected encoding format.
Step 5 Click OK.
On the Forwarding Server page, check the syslog server information, and check
the connection between the system and the syslog server.
● If Connection Status is Normal, the system is successfully interconnected
with the syslog server.
● If Connection Status is Unknown, it is uncertain whether the syslog server
has been successfully interconnected. In this situation, click Refresh later to
check the latest status.
● If Connection Status is Disconnected, the system fails to interconnect with
the syslog server. In this situation, check the parameter settings. If the
parameter settings are correct, rectify the fault by referring to the alarm
"Alarm of the Failure to Connect the Master and Standby Syslog Servers".
Step 6 Optional: Set the number of times and interval for checking the connectivity of
the syslog server as required.
1. In the navigation pane, choose Forwarding Rule.
2. On the Forwarding Rule page, enable Forwarding Server Connectivity
Check.
3. Set the number of times and interval for checking the connectivity of the
syslog server as required.

NO TE
– This function takes effect only when both the active and standby syslog servers are
configured. By default, this function is disabled.
– If this function is enabled and the active and standby syslog servers are configured,
the system periodically checks whether the connectivity of the active syslog server
is normal at the configured interval. If the connectivity of the active syslog server is
normal, logs are forwarded to the active syslog server. If the number of check
times reaches the specified value and the connectivity of the active syslog server is
still abnormal, the system then checks the connectivity of the standby syslog server
at the configured interval. If the connectivity of the standby syslog server is
normal, the system forwards logs to the standby syslog server and reports the
alarm "Alarm of the Switchover to the Standby Syslog Server." If the number of
check times reaches the specified value and the connectivity of the standby syslog
server is still abnormal, logs are not forwarded. In this case, the alarm "Alarm of
the Failure to Connect the Master and Standby Syslog Servers" is reported.
----End
8.5.2 Configuring Log Forwarding Rules (the PowerEcho)
The log forwarding rule defines the levels of different types of logs to be
forwarded.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the management plane. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to
the PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Log Management > Log Forwarding
Settings from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Forwarding Rule.
Step 3 On the Forwarding Rule page, click the desired levels for different types of logs.
Step 4 Click Apply.
Then the system forwards logs based on the log forwarding rule and Syslog level
on the Forwarding Server page.
----End
8.5.3 Setting Log Dump Parameters (the PowerEcho)
Security logs, system logs, and operation logs are stored in the database after
being generated. To prevent the database space from being exhausted, the system
automatically dumps the logs that meet specified conditions to the hard disk as
files and deletes the log files that meet specified conditions from the hard disk. If
the conditions for dumping logs and deleting log files from the hard disk do not
meet your requirements, you can modify the conditions as required by following

You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossadm users of the
management node.
Context
The default conditions for dumping logs and deleting log files from the hard disk
are as follows:
● Conditions for dumping logs: The number of logs in the database exceeds 1
million, the size of the logs in the database exceeds 80% of the capacity, or
the logs have been stored in the database for more than 90 days. To prevent
excessive logs from exhausting the database space, the system checks the
logs every hour, saves logs meeting the conditions to a .csv or .zip file, and
automatically deletes logs that have been successfully dumped from the
database. The log files in .csv or .zip format are stored in the /opt/share/oss/
manager/MCCommonService/dump/timestamp directory on the hard disk. If
the PowerEcho is deployed in single-server mode, and the management node
and product node are the same node, replace MCCommonService with
UniEPLiteService.
● Conditions for deleting log files from the hard disk: The size of the log files is
greater than 1024 MB (default value), the log files are stored for more than
45 days (default value), or the total number of log files exceeds 1000 (default
value). To prevent excessive log files from exhausting the disk space, the
system checks log files every hour and deletes log files meeting the conditions
from the hard disk.
Procedure
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the management node, as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
NO TE
If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, that is, there are multiple management
nodes, perform operations on Management0 or Management1. For details about how to
obtain the IP address of a node, see 12.5 Querying the Management IP Address of a
Node.
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: Password for the ossadm user
Step 3 Run the following command to set the log dump parameters:
> cd /opt/oss/manager/apps/MCCommonService/bin
NO TE
If the PowerEcho is deployed in single-server mode, and the management node and
product node are the same node, replace MCCommonService with UniEPLiteService. For
details about how to check that the management node and product node are the same
node, see 12.11 Checking the Deployment Mode of Nodes.
● Querying the settings of dump parameters

successfully queried:
The configuration information is as follows:
Dump file compression status: Not Compressed
Dump file generation status: Not Generated
Dump file type: CSV
Log retention days: 90
Dump task status: Deactivated
Log file retention days: 45
Total number of files in the dump directory: 255
Total file size in the dump directory (MB): 1024
Maximum data records stored in the database table: 1000000
Log space usage (%): 80
The script is executed successfully.
● Setting dump parameters
> bash auditTool.sh -s Percentage of the database space occupied by logs -p
Number of days for storing logs in the database -f Number of days for storing
dump files -a Whether to enable log dump -g Whether to generate dump
files during log dump -c Whether to compress the dump files -n Total number
of files in the dump directory -S Total size of files in the dump directory
For details about the parameters in the command, see Table 8-87.

to 3000.

directory

to 6144.
If the following information is displayed, the log dump parameters are
successfully set:
The script is executed successfully.
----End
8.5.4 Setting Log Dump (the NetEco)
Security logs, system logs, and operation logs are stored in the database after
being generated. To prevent the database space from being exhausted, the system
automatically dumps the logs that meet specified conditions to the hard disk as
files. To prevent the disk space from being exhausted, the system automatically
deletes the log files that meet specified conditions from the hard disk. You can
specify the conditions for dumping logs and deleting log files based on site
requirements.
Prerequisites
You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossuser users for logging in
to the node where SMLogLic resides.
Context
● Conditions for dumping logs: The number of logs in the database exceeds
million, the size of the logs in the database exceeds 80% of the capacity, or
the number of days for storing the logs exceeds 1000 days. To prevent
excessive log files from exhausting the database space, the system checks logs
every day and saves logs that meet the requirements to the hard disk of a
server. Then the dumped logs are automatically deleted from the database.
● Conditions for deleting log files: The size of the log files is greater than 6144
MB, the log files are stored for more than 365 days, or the total number of
log files exceeds 3000. To prevent excessive log files from exhausting the disk
space, the system checks log files every day and deletes log files meeting the

NO TE
The values in the preceding conditions for dumping logs and deleting log files are
default values.
Procedure
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the node where SMLogLic resides, as the sopuser user in
SSH mode. For details about how to obtain the IP address of the node where a
service resides, see 12.3 Querying the Management IP Address of the Node
Where a Process or Service Resides
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the ossuser user:
su - ossuser
Password: password for the ossuser user
Step 3 Run the following commands to set the log dump parameters:
cd /opt/oss/NetEco/apps/SMLogLicService/bin
● To query the settings of the log dump parameters, run the following
command:
./auditTool.sh -cmd list
If information similar to the following is displayed, the log dump parameter
settings are successfully queried:
The configuration information is as follows:
Dump file compression status: Compressed
Dump file generation status: Generated
Dump file type: CSV
Log retention days: 1000
Dump task status: Activated
Log file retention days: 365
Total number of files in the dump directory: 3000
Total file size in the dump directory (MB): 6144
Maximum data records stored in the database table: 0000
Log space usage (%): 80.00
The script is executed successfully.
● To set log dump parameters, run the following command:
./auditTool.sh -s Percentage of the database space occupied by logs -p
Number of days for storing logs in the database -f Number of days for storing
log files -a Whether to enable log dump -g Whether to generate dump files
during log dump -c Whether to compress the dump files -n Total number of
files in the dump directory -S Total size of files in the dump directory
If information similar to the following is displayed, the log dump parameters
are successfully set:
The script is executed successfully.

Operation Result
The system checks logs every day, saves the logs that meet the requirements as a
CSV or ZIP file, and saves the file to the /opt/share/oss/NetEco/XXXService/
dump/<timestamp directory>/<timestamp directory> directory on the hard disk of
the server. The value of XXXService can be SMLogLicService or

You can refer to this section to understand the structure of forwarded log packets
when necessary, for example, when you need to interconnect the system with a
third-party log management platform.
Packet Structures of Forwarded Logs
A forwarded log packet consists of the following fields.
Field Meaning Description
PRI Priority The priority is represented by a number of 1 to 3 digits,
and is enclosed by < and >. PRI is a value calculated
based on the facility number (Facility) and log severity
(Severity) as follows: PRI = Facility * 8 + Severity
Timestamp Log The format varies with the protocol for forwarding the
operatio log:
n time
● TLS: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX, for example,
2020-03-10T17:49:33.000+09:00
● TCP/UDP: MM dd HH:mm:ss, for example, Aug 9
10:47:47
NOTE
Whether the server time zone is included in forwarded
logs depends on your log forwarding server settings. If the
time zone is included, the log operation time is in the
format of MM dd HH:mm:ss XXX, for example, Aug 9
10:47:47 +08:00.
HostName Hostnam ● For the NetEco: IP address of the node where
e SMLogLic resides.
● For the PowerEcho: IP address of the management
node
Tag Tag By default, a hyphen (-) is displayed for this field.
Msg Log body Content of the forwarded log.
The content of the Msg field varies with the log type. The fields of different log
types are described in the following table.
Log Type Msg Field Example
Operation log Log Type OperationLog
Separator %%
SN Log serial number

testuser.

8.5.6.2 Security-related Logs
During service running, security logs, system logs, and operation logs are recorded
into the database. Users who have permission to view logs can view security-
related logs on the NetEco web client.

Definition
Operation logs record all security-irrelevant operations on the NetEco client, for
example, acknowledging alarms and clearing alarms.

8.5.6.2.2 System Logs
Definition
System logs record tasks that affect the running status of NetEco on the NetEco
server. These tasks are triggered by the NetEco client (for example, starting or
executing a scheduled task) or by the NetEco server (for example, starting or
stopping the NetEco service).

identify the abnormal user logins.

26862:M 18 Sep 22:48:37.981 * 10.67.178.153:34407 operation:config get master-read-only

9
System Configurations
System configurations commission the network configurations, time zone, time,
and user password of a product in a centralized manner.
9.1 Software Management
This chapter describes the operations related to product software, including
software package management, third-party patch management, and product
software installation, upgrade, capacity expansion, and uninstallation.
9.1.1 Managing Software Packages
You can upload software packages required for installation and upgrade, or
management of third-party patches, and delete unnecessary software packages if
the disk space is insufficient.
Prerequisites
● The communication between your PC and the PowerEcho node is normal.
● To upload a software package:
– You have obtained the software package and corresponding signature file
to be uploaded.
– You have obtained the password for the sopuser and ossadm user on the
PowerEcho node.
● To delete a software package:
The software package to be deleted is not in the running state.
Precautions
● The software package to be uploaded must meet the following requirements.
Otherwise, the upload fails.
– The software package name can contain a maximum of 128 characters,

file at the same time. The signature file can be in .asc, .cms, or .crl
format.
– If the software package is in .tar format, you do not need to upload the
signature file at the same time because the software package contains
the corresponding signature file.
● If the software package size is less than 6 GB, the signature file size is less
than 2 MB, and the network bandwidth between your PC and the PowerEcho
node is greater than 100 Mbit/s, you can upload software packages using the
software management function on the PowerEcho. If the preceding conditions
are not met, upload the software packages in resumable transfer mode.
● During the scanning for software packages, do not restart any management
node. Otherwise, the scanning may fail.
● If the scanning fails, the uploaded software packages will be automatically
deleted to free up the disk space.

Task Operation
(Recommended) Upload the 1. Use PuTTY to log in to the PowerEcho node
software package to the as the sopuser user in SSH mode. For
PowerEcho in resumable details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server
transfer mode. Using PuTTY.
NOTE
If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode,
perform operations on Management0. For details
about how to obtain the IP address of a node,
see 12.5 Querying the Management IP Address
of a Node
2. Run the following command to switch to
the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
3. Run the following command to check the
available space in the /opt directory:
> df -h /opt
Check the value of Avail in the command
output.
● If the value is greater than or equal to
three times the size of the software
packages, go to 4.
● If the value is less than three times the
size of the software packages, refer to
operations provided in this section to
delete unnecessary software packages. If
the disk space is still less than three
times the size of the software packages,
contact technical support to clear the
disk space.
4. Use FileZilla to upload the software
package and signature file to the default
temporary directory /opt/oss/
manager/var/tmp on the PowerEcho node
as the ossadm user. For details, see 13.2
Transferring Files Using FileZilla.
5. Log in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2
Logging In to the PowerEcho.
6. On the PowerEcho, choose Product >
Software Management > Manage
Software Packages from the main menu.
7. Click Scan. In the Information dialog box,
click OK.

Task Operation
NOTE
The system automatically checks the integrity of
the software package. The result can be viewed
in the detailed information about the scanning
task on the Task List page. If the scanning task
fails, rectify the fault as prompted, and then
upload the software package and execute the
scanning task again.
8. After the scanning task is executed
successfully, view the uploaded software
package on the Manage Software
Packages tab page.

Task Operation
Use the software 1. Use PuTTY to log in to the PowerEcho node
management function of the as the sopuser user in SSH mode. For
PowerEcho to upload the details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server
software package. Using PuTTY.
NOTE
If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode,
perform operations on Management0. For details
about how to obtain the IP address of a node,
see 12.5 Querying the Management IP Address
of a Node
2. Run the following command to switch to
the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
3. Run the following command to check the
available space in the /opt directory:
> df -h /opt
Check the value of Avail in the command
output.
● If the value is greater than or equal to
three times the size of the software
packages, go to 4.
● If the value is less than three times the
size of the software packages, refer to
operations provided in this section to
delete unnecessary software packages. If
the disk space is still less than three
times the size of the software packages,
contact technical support to clear the
disk space.
4. Run the following command to check the
available space in the /opt/share directory:
> df -h /opt/share
Check the value of Avail in the command
output.
● If the value is greater than the size of the
uploaded software package, go to 5.
● If the value is less than the size, upload
the software package in resumable
transfer mode.
5. Log in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2
Logging In to the PowerEcho.
6. On the PowerEcho, choose Product >
Software Management > Manage
Software Packages from the main menu.
7. On the Manage Software Packages page,
click Upload to upload the software

Task Operation
NOTE
During the upload, the system automatically
checks the integrity of the installation package. If
the upload fails, rectify the fault as prompted
and then upload the software package again.
Delete a software package. 1. Log in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2
Logging In to the PowerEcho.
2. On the PowerEcho, choose Product >
Software Management > Manage
Software Packages from the main menu.
On the Manage Software Packages page,
delete the software package as prompted.
9.1.2 Upgrading Product Software
After the product software upgrade package is uploaded to the PowerEcho, you
can upgrade the product.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● During the upgrade, the system backs up the product data. Ensure that
backup parameters have been configured. For details, see 10.4 Configuring
Backup Parameters.
● The product software upgrade package has been uploaded. For details, see
9.1.1 Managing Software Packages.
● The services and databases on all nodes of the product to be upgraded are
running properly. For details, see 5.1.1 Monitoring Products.
Precautions
● During the upgrade, services of the product to be upgraded are automatically
stopped. Therefore, exercise caution when performing this operation. You are
advised to perform this operation in off-peak hours.
● After the residual data is cleared, rollback to the source version is not
supported. Exercise caution when performing this operation.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Product > Software Management > Deploy Product
Software from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Deploy Product Software page, click the product to be upgraded. The
product details page is displayed.
Step 3 In the upper right corner, click Upgrade, and choose Upgrade Wizard from the
drop-down menu. Perform operations as prompted. For details, see the upgrade

NO TE
When you upgrade or roll back the Product (including the upgrade or rollback failure),
clicking Upgrade Wizard on the PowerEcho displays a message indicating that the upgrade
wizard fails to be started. You can log in to https://client IP address of the
PowerEcho:31050 to access the upgrade wizard page.
Step 4 (Optional) If you confirm that the rollback to the source version is not required,
clear residual data after the upgrade to free up the disk space.
In the upper right corner of the page for the product deployment details, click
More and choose Clear Residual Data from the drop-down menu. Clear
unnecessary deployment paths and databases as prompted.
NO TICE
After the data is cleared, the rollback to the source version is not supported.
Perform this operation only when you confirm that the rollback is not required.
Step 5 After the upgrade, historical backup files have become invalid. Back up the
following data:
● The application and data of the PowerEcho. For details, see 10.6.1 Manually
Backing Up the Application and Data of the PowerEcho
● The product applications. For details, see 10.5.3 Backing Up Product
Applications.
● The database applications. For details, see 10.5.4 Backing Up Database
Applications.
● The product data. For details, see 10.5.2 Backing Up Product Data.
----End
9.1.3 Uninstalling Product Software
You can uninstall the product software that has been installed. This operation will
uninstall both the NetEco and PowerEcho.
Prerequisites
You have obtained the password for the sopuser user and root user of the NetEco
server.
Procedure
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the management node as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
For details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.
NO TE
If PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, log in to any management node and perform the
uninstallation operation.
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the root user:

Step 3 Run the following command to uninstall the NetEco:
# cd /opt/NetEcoTools/
# screen -S "uninstall.sh" bash uninstall.sh
When the following information is displayed, type y or Y and press Enter:
Are you sure to continue? [y/n]
NO TE
To cancel the uninstallation, enter n or N.
If the following information is displayed, NetEco is uninstalled successfully:
Otherwise, contact Technical support engineers.
Uninstall... done
NO TE
If PuTTY is disconnected during the uninstallation, log in to the NetEco server again and
switch back to the task session window displayed when PuTTY is disconnected. For details,
see 12.23 How Do I Switch Back to the Task Window After PuTTY Is Disconnected?
Step 4 After the uninstallation is complete, the historical backup files of the applications
and data of the management plane will become invalid. You need to back up the
applications and data of the management plane. For details, see 10.6.1 Manually
Backing Up the Application and Data of the PowerEcho.
----End
9.1.4 Modifying Configuration Parameters of Product
Software
You can use this function to modify the configurations of installed product
software.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Precautions
● Parameters on this page are installation and deployment procedure
parameters and have been configured during installation. Do not change the
parameters unless detailed modification scenarios and procedures are
described in the documentation.
● After the modification, historical backup files of the application and data of
the PowerEcho will become invalid. Therefore, the backup files before the
modification cannot be used for restoration. Back up data in a timely manner
after the modification.

Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Product > Software Management > Deploy Product
Software from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Deploy Product Software page, click the product with configurations to
be modified. The product details page is displayed.
Step 3 In the upper right corner, click More and choose Modify Configurations from the
drop-down menu, and perform operations as prompted.
NO TE
Exercise caution when performing this operation. Incorrect parameters may cause
configuration failures or even system unavailability.
Step 4 After the modification, historical backup files of the application and data of the
PowerEcho have become invalid. Back up the application and data of the
PowerEcho. For details, see 10.6.1 Manually Backing Up the Application and
Data of the PowerEcho.
----End
9.1.5 Adding Product Features
You can incrementally install product features.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The product to which you need to add features has been installed.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Product > Software Management > Deploy Product
Software from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Deploy Product Software page, click the product with features to be
installed. The product details page is displayed.
Step 3 In the upper right corner of the page, click More and choose Add Features from
the drop-down menu.
Step 4 In the Features area, select features to be installed, and perform operations as
prompted.
NO TE
After a feature is selected, new configuration items may be displayed in the Configurations
area. Ensure that the values of the configuration items are correct.
Step 5 After the add, historical backup files have become invalid. Back up the following
data:
● The application and data of the PowerEcho. For details, see 10.6.1 Manually
Backing Up the Application and Data of the PowerEcho.
● The product applications. For details, see 10.5.3 Backing Up Product

Applications.
● The product data. For details, see 10.5.2 Backing Up Product Data.
----End
9.1.6 Deleting Product Features
You can Delete unnecessary features of a product to save system resources.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Product > Software Management > Deploy Product
Software from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Deploy Product Software page, click the product with features to be
deleted. The product details page is displayed.
Step 3 In the upper right corner of the page, click More and choose Delete Features
from the drop-down menu.
Step 4 On the Delete Features page, click on the left of the product with features to
be uninstalled. Select the features to be uninstalled, and perform operations as
prompted.
Step 5 After the delete, historical backup files have become invalid. Back up the following
data:
● The application and data of the PowerEcho. For details, see 10.6.1 Manually
Backing Up the Application and Data of the PowerEcho.
● The product applications. For details, see 10.5.3 Backing Up Product
Applications.
● The database applications. For details, see 10.5.4 Backing Up Database
Applications.
● The product data. For details, see 10.5.2 Backing Up Product Data.
----End
9.1.7 Managing Third-Party Software Patches
After a third-party patch package is uploaded to the PowerEcho, you can install
the patch.
Context
The following types of patches are supported: EulerOS.

● The third-party patch package has been uploaded. For details, see 9.1.1
Managing Software Packages.
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Precautions
OS patch management and upgrade. During the upgrade, the OS is restarted and
cannot be logged in and services are interrupted. You are advised to perform the
operations in off-peak hours.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Product > Software Management > Manage Third-
Party Software Patches from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Manage Third-Party Software Patches page, click the tile corresponding
to the product to be upgraded and perform operations as prompted.
----End
9.2 Collecting Equipment Serial Numbers
Before applying for a product, collect the equipment serial numbers (ESNs) of the
product.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Information Collection > Collect ESN
Information from the main menu.
Step 2 In the Select Product and Collect ESN Information area on the Collect ESN
Information page, select a product type and click Collect on the right.
Step 3 In the displayed dialog box, click Yes. In the displayed dialog box, click OK.
Step 4 After the task of Collecting ESN Information is complete. In the ESN
Information list, click the .zip package of the collected ESN to download the file
to your PC.
----End
9.3 Display Format Settings on the PowerEcho
The PowerEcho allows you to set the display format of the time, number, date,

9.3.1 Date and Time Zone Display Format
The PowerEcho allows you to set the display format of the date and time zone on
the web client. After the setting takes effect, the date on the PowerEcho is
displayed in the configured format.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Precautions
This setting takes effect only for the current user who logs in to the PowerEcho
web client.

Step 2 On the Set Date and Timezone Display Format page, perform operations as
prompted.
----End

9.4 Configuring and Commissioning Notifications (the
NetEco)
The notifications service allows you to send messages remotely. Before the
notifications service is used to send messages, ensure that the system can
communicate with a short message service (SMS) server, SMS modem, or email
server.
9.4.1 Commissioning the SMS Server
When you need to receive alarm and event notifications by SMS message, the
SMS server connectivity must be normal and the SMS server parameters are
correctly set so that SMS messages can be sent properly.
9.4.1.1 Checking the Connectivity of the SMS Server
Before the SMS server is used to send alarm and event notifications by SMS
message, you need to ensure that the nodes where the campmessagingservice
process resides properly communicate with the SMS server.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the password for the sopuser user of the nodes where the
campmessagingservice process resides.
● You have obtained the IP address and port number of the SMS server from

Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the node where the campmessagingservice process resides,
as the sopuser user in SSH mode. For details about how to obtain the IP address
of the node where a process resides, see "Querying the Management IP Address of
the Node Where a Process or Service Resides" in Administrator Guide.
Step 2 Run the following command to check the connectivity between the node where
the campmessagingservice process resides and the SMS server port:
ssh -v -p port number of the SMS server IP address of the SMS server
If the following information is displayed, the connection is normal. Otherwise, the
connection fails. In this case, contact the administrator.
debug1: Connection established.
Step 3 If the NetEco is deployed in cluster mode, perform the preceding operations on all
the nodes where the campmessagingservice process resides.
----End
9.4.1.2 Commissioning SMS Server Notifications
When you need to send notifications by SMS message, you need to set the SMS
server parameters and verify the settings to ensure that the SMS messages can be
sent properly.
Prerequisites
● You have the SMS Settings permission.
● You have obtained the following information from the SMS server provider:
– Type of the communication protocol used between the notifications
service and SMS server and related protocol parameters.
– Domain name or IP address, encoding protocol, port number, and
username and password of the SMS server.
– Sender mobile number.
● A DNS server has been configured and can resolve the domain name, if the
SMS server address is a domain name.
● The node where the campmessagingservice process resides properly
communicates with the SMS server. For details, see 9.4.1.1 Checking the
Connectivity of the SMS Server.
Context
● If both an SMS server and SMS modem are configured, the SMS modem is
preferentially used to send SMS messages. If the SMS messages fail to be
sent, the SMS server is used. Sending SMS messages using an SMS modem is
slower than using an SMS server. It is recommended O&M personnel use the
SMS server.
● To send notifications to relevant personnel, you need to enter their personal
data, such as mobile numbers and email addresses. You are obligated to take

that users' personal data is fully protected.
● For security purposes, personal data such as mobile numbers and email
addresses are anonymized on the GUI and encrypted during transmission.
● If the SMS server requires secure connections and provides the corresponding
certificate, you need to configure the SMS server certificate and CRL in
notifications. After the certificate is configured, periodically check whether
there are notifications service certificate alarms related to the SMS server. If
there is an alarm indicating that the SMS server certificate is about to expire,
update the certificate in time and delete the certificate that is about to expire.
If there is an alarm indicating that the SMS server certificate has expired,
update the certificate immediately and delete the certificate that has expired.
If the trusted CA has updated the CRL, update the SMS server certificate and
CRL in a timely manner.
For data security purposes, the notifications service supports certificates
signed using the SHA256withRSA, SHA384withRSA, or SHA512withRSA
algorithm.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > Notifications from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose SMS Settings > Protocol. Check whether the
parameter settings are consistent with those used by the SMS server.
● If so, go to Step 3.
● If not, set the parameters to make them consistent with those used by the
SMS server, and click Apply.
Step 3 Perform the following operations to set SMS server parameters:
1. In the navigation pane, choose SMS Settings > SMS Server.
2. Set the domain name or IP address and port number of the SMS server, and
the sender number, as shown in Table 9-1.
Table 9-1 Parameters on the SMS server settings page
Parameter Description
SMS server domain SMS server domain name or IP address obtained
name/IP address from the SMS server provider.
SMS protocol Encoding protocol used by the SMS server to
interconnect with the SMS server. SMPP 3.3, SMP
3.4, CMPP 2.x, CMPP 3.x, SGIP, SMGP, and CNGP are
supported.
SMS server port Port number for interconnection with the SMS
server. For details, see NetEco Communication

timestamp, and message subject, which are used to
identify the message source and provide basic
message details. Separate the prefix from the
message body with square brackets ([]), for
example, [123].

timestamp, and message subject, which are used to
identify the message source and provide basic
message details. Separate the suffix from the
message body with square brackets ([]), for
example, [123].

– If this parameter is enabled, an SMS notification
will be sent after the test is successful.
– If this parameter is disabled, the configuration is
saved as a draft and no SMS notification will be
sent.

Parameter Description
Allow long SMS – Yes:
messages Each SMS message contains a maximum of 134
characters by default. Messages that exceed the
maximum length are segmented into multiple
messages before sending to the SMS server. Once
received, the segments are combined by header
and sent to the receiver as one complete SMS

that the recipient can receive the complete message.

to the notifications service after the message is
