know whether the message is successfully sent.
4. Click Test to check whether the system is properly interconnected with the
SMS server.
– If "Test succeeded" is displayed, they are properly connected. Click Apply.
– If the test fails, rectify the fault based on the error information or contact
technical support.
----End

Before the SMS modem is used to send alarms and events, ensure that the SMS
modem is successfully connected to a physical machine and the SMS modem
parameters are correctly set so that SMS messages can be sent properly. If both
the SMS modem and SMS server are configured, the SMS modem is preferentially
used to send SMS messages. If the SMS messages fail to be sent, the SMS server is
used. Sending SMS messages using an SMS modem is slower than using an SMS
server. You are advised to use the SMS server.
9.4.2.1 Preparing for Commissioning
Environment Requirements
If an SMS modem is used to send SMS messages, NetEco must be deployed on the
physical machine. If the SMS modem is connected to the physical machine using a
serial cable, the physical machine must have an RJ45 serial port. If NetEco is
deployed on a VM, use an SMS server to send SMS messages.
Preparing the SMS Modem
● Complete the commissioning of the SMS modem. For details, see the
instructions delivered with the SMS modem.
● Insert an SIM card into the SMS modem, and record the network system of
the SIM card and the baud rate of the SMS modem.
● The SMS modem and SIM card must meet the following requirements:
– SMS modem: You are advised to use the SMS modems of the E-TEK GSM
modem TD-8412 (in China) and E-TEK GSM modem TD-8422 (outside
China) models, and the SMS modems that support the outdoor IoT 4G
module.
– SIM card (standard): Ensure that the SIM card is not in arrears, is
reachable, and can send and receive SMS messages. Check methods: 1.
Insert the SIM card into a mobile phone, and make a call. 2. Contact the
SIM card carrier. In addition, you need to inform the carrier of the usage
of the SIM card to prevent the carrier from processing the SMS messages
you sent as junk messages.
– If multiple SMS modems are deployed, ensure that the network systems,
physical serial ports, and baud rates of the SMS modems are the same. It
is recommended that the SIM cards of the SMS modems are from the
same carrier.
Preparing Serial Cables
If an SMS modem that supports the outdoor IoT 4G module is used, no serial
cable is required.
If an SMS modem of the E-TEK GSM modem TD-8412 or E-TEK GSM modem
TD-8422 model is used, prepare a serial cable with an RS232 male connector at
one end and an RJ45 connector at the other end, as shown in Figure 9-1. You can
purchase the serial cable or make the serial cable by yourself. Purchasing a

● Purchase a serial cable whose model is Signal cable, V2R5C10 serial cable, 3
m, DB9M, CC2P0.48B(S), MP8-VI and whose BOM number is 04071652.
● Make a serial cable as follows:
a. Prepare the RS232 male connector, RJ45 connector, and multimeter.
Figure 9-2 shows the pointer numbers of the RS232 male connector and
RJ45 connector of the serial cable.
b. Connect one end of the multimeter to pointer 2 of the RS232 male
connector, and connect the other end of the multimeter to the eight
wires of the network cable in sequence. Connect the wire that is short-
circuited to pointer 2. Connect the wire to pointer 3 of the RJ45
connector.
c. Use the same method to connect pointers 3 and 5 of the RS232 male
connector to pointers 6 and 4 of the RJ45 connector. Other wires do not
need to be connected.

9.4.2.2 Commissioning the SMS Modem Using the Physical Machine
To send alarm and event notifications by SMS message using the SMS modem, the
SMS modem must be properly connected to the physical machine. When the SMS
modem is deployed for the first time or the SIM card on the SMS modem is
replaced, you need to commission the SMS modem to ensure that SMS messages
can be successfully sent. The network systems of the SMS modem include GSM,
CDMA, LTE, and FDD LTE. The connection and commissioning methods of these
SMS modems are similar.
Prerequisites
● The deployment environment, SMS modem, and serial cable are ready. For
details, see 9.4.2.1 Preparing for Commissioning.
● You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and root users of the nodes
where the campmessagingservice process resides.
● Before using a SIM card, you must disable the PIN check function of the SIM
card. Otherwise, the SIM card cannot be used. You can insert the SIM card
into a mobile phone and use the PIN setting function of the mobile phone to
disable the PIN check function. The setting method varies according to the
mobile phone model. For details, see the operation guide delivered with the
mobile phone.
Context
Hot swap of SIM cards is not supported on the SMS modem. To replace a SIM card
or remove a SIM card and insert it again, you need to power off and then power
on the SMS modem. Otherwise, the SMS modem cannot identify the SIM card and
SMS messages failed to be sent. If multiple SMS modems are deployed and some
SIM cards need to be replaced, you are advised to replace them with ones from
the same carrier.

modem from and then connect it to the USB port of the physical machine.
● For the SMS modem of the E-TEK GSM modem TD-8412 or E-TEK GSM
modem TD-8422 model, remove and reconnect the power cable of the SMS
modem.
Procedure
Step 1 Connect to the SMS modem.
● If an SMS modem that supports the outdoor IoT 4G module is used, directly
connect the SMS modem to the USB port of the physical machine.
● If an SMS modem of the E-TEK GSM modem TD-8412 or E-TEK GSM modem
TD-8422 model is used, connect the RS232 male connector of the serial cable
to the SMS modem and the RJ45 connector at the other end to the serial port
of the physical machine where the campmessagingservice process resides,
record the name of the serial port.
Step 2 Connect the power cable to the SMS modem and power on the SMS modem.
Step 3 Check the status of the indicator on the SMS modem and ensure that the SMS
modem works properly.
Table 9-3 describes the status of the indicator on the SMS modem and the
corresponding handling policies.
Table 9-3 Description of indicator status
Indicator Working Status Handling Policies

Step 4 If the SMS modem that supports the outdoor IoT 4G module is used, select the
node where the SMS modem is deployed on the NetEco. Otherwise, the serial port
number of the SMS modem cannot be queried in subsequent operations.
1. Choose System > System Settings > Notifications from the main menu.
2. In the navigation pane, choose SMS Modem Settings.
3. On the SMS Modem Settings page, set Settings take effect to Yes.
4. Select the node where the SMS modem is deployed.
Step 5 Use PuTTY to log in to the node where the campmessagingservice process resides,
as the sopuser user in SSH mode. For details about how to obtain the IP address
of the node where a process resides, see "Querying the Management IP Address of
the Node Where a Process or Service Resides" in Administrator Guide.

su - root

9.4.2.3 Commissioning the SMS Modem Using Notifications
The notifications service provides the function of setting an SMS modem. When
you need to send notifications by SMS message, you need to set the SMS modem
parameters and verify the settings to ensure that the SMS messages can be sent
properly.
Prerequisites
● The SMS modem has been connected to the serial port of the physical
machine and has been commissioned. For details, see 9.4.2.1 Preparing for
Commissioning and 9.4.2.2 Commissioning the SMS Modem Using the
Physical Machine.
● You have obtained the network system of the SIM card in the SMS modem
from the carrier.
– 2G: GSM
– 3G: CDMA
– 4G: LTE or FDD LTE, which needs to be confirmed with the carrier.
● You have obtained the baud rate of the SMS modem from the instructions
delivered with the SMS modem.
– The baud rate of the SMS modem of the E-TEK GSM modem TD-8412 or
E-TEK GSM modem TD-8422 model is 115200.
– The baud rate of the SMS modem that supports the outdoor IoT 4G
module is 9600.
● You have obtained the serial port of the physical machine connected to the

port of the physical machine is ttyUSB_4G.
– For the SMS modem of the E-TEK GSM modem TD-8412 and E-TEK GSM
modem TD-8422 models, the serial port of the physical machine is COM1
or COM2.
Context
● To send notifications to relevant personnel, you need to enter their personal
data, such as mobile numbers and email addresses. You are obligated to take
considerable measures, in compliance with the laws of the countries or
regions concerned and the user privacy policies of your company, to ensure
that users' personal data is fully protected.
● For security purposes, personal data such as mobile numbers and email
addresses are anonymized on the GUI and encrypted during transmission.
● The serial port connected to the SMS modem is a secure hardware-level
connection.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > Notifications from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose SMS Modem Settings.
Step 3 On the SMS Modem Settings page, set Settings take effect to Yes.
Step 4 Set the SMS modem deployment node, network system, serial port used by the
SMS modem to connect to the physical machine, and baud rate of the SMS
modem, as shown in Table 9-6.
Table 9-6 Parameters on the SMS Modem Settings page
Parameter Description
SMS modem ● If the campmessagingservice process is deployed on
node multiple nodes and the SMS modem is deployed on each
node, you can select default. The system randomly
allocates an SMS modem node.
● If the campmessagingservice process is deployed on only
one node, you can select default or the corresponding SMS
modem node.
NOTE
The SMS modem node is the physical machine node to which the
SMS modem connects. In a single-node deployment scenario, if the
SMS modem parameters have been configured before an upgrade
of CAMPMessagingService, you do not need to set the SMS modem
node after the upgrade. In a multi-node deployment scenario, you
need to reconfigure the SMS modem node for an upgrade of
CAMPMessagingService.

Parameter Description
Network ● Set this parameter to the network system of the SIM card in
system of SMS the SMS modem obtained from the carrier.
modem
– 2G: GSM
– 3G: CDMA
– 4G: LTE or FDD LTE, which needs to be confirmed with
the carrier.
SMS modem ● Set this parameter to the serial port of the physical machine
serial port connected to the SMS modem.
– For the SMS modem that supports the outdoor IoT 4G
module, the serial port of the physical machine is
ttyUSB_4G.
– For the SMS modem of the E-TEK GSM modem TD-8412
and E-TEK GSM modem TD-8422 models, the serial port
of the physical machine is COM1 or COM2.
SMS modem ● Set this parameter to the baud rate of the SMS modem
bound rate obtained from the instructions delivered with the SMS
modem.
– The baud rate of the SMS modem of the E-TEK GSM
modem TD-8412 or E-TEK GSM modem TD-8422 model
is 115200.
– The baud rate of the SMS modem that supports the
outdoor IoT 4G module is 9600.
NOTE
If the physical machine has multiple serial ports and the name of the
serial port connected to the SMS modem cannot be determined, select
the serial ports one by one for the test. If the test is successful, the
serial port passed the test is the one to which the SMS modem
connects.
Recipient Enter a mobile phone number used to check whether SMS
mobile messages can be received during a test.
number

Parameter Description
Allow long ● Yes:
SMS messages Each SMS message contains a maximum of 134 characters
by default. Messages that exceed the maximum length are
segmented into multiple messages before sending to the
SMS server. Once received, the segments are combined by
header and sent to the receiver as one complete SMS
message.
● No:
Each SMS message contains a maximum of 140 characters
by default. Messages that exceed the maximum length are
segmented into multiple messages before sending to the
SMS server and sent to the receiver as multiple messages.
NOTE
● To send a long message, set this parameter to Yes to ensure that
the recipient can receive a complete message.
● You can configure the maximum length of an SMS message as
required. For the configuration method, see "Changing the
Maximum Length of Each SMS Message" in Administrator Guide.
Settings take Determine whether to enable this parameter after testing the
effect connectivity of the SMS modem. For details, see 9.4.2.1
Preparing for Commissioning and 9.4.2.2 Commissioning the
SMS Modem Using the Physical Machine.
● If this parameter is enabled, an SMS notification will be sent
after the test is successful.
● If this parameter is disabled, the configuration is saved as a
draft and no SMS notification will be sent.
Step 5 Enter the mobile number for receiving notifications. The mobile number can be in
the following format:
Mobile number with country/region code (for example, 0040+mobile number and
+40+mobile number)
Step 6 Click Test to check whether the system is properly interconnected with the SMS
modem.
● If "Test succeeded" is displayed, they are properly interconnected. Click Apply.
If the test SMS message is received, the SMS modem is successfully
connected.
● If "Connectivity failed." is displayed, thy are not properly interconnected. In
this case, go to Step 7.
Step 7 Ensure that the required serial port of the physical machine is idle, and the
network status and signal strength of the SMS modem are normal.

su - root

9.4.3 Commissioning the Email Server
When you need to receive alarm and event notifications by email, the email server
connectivity must be normal and the email server parameters are correctly set so
that emails can be sent properly.
9.4.3.1 Checking the Connectivity of the Email Server
Before the email server is used to send alarm and event notifications by email, you
need to ensure that the nodes where the campmessagingservice process resides
properly communicate with the email server.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the password for the sopuser user of the nodes where the
campmessagingservice process resides.
● You have obtained the domain name/IP address and port number of the
email server from the email server provider.
Procedure
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the node where the campmessagingservice process resides,
as the sopuser user in SSH mode. For details about how to obtain the IP address
of the node where a process resides, see "Querying the Management IP Address of
the Node Where a Process or Service Resides" in Administrator Guide.
Step 2 Run the following command to check the connectivity between the node where
the campmessagingservice process resides and the email server port:
ssh -v -p Port number of the email server Domain name/IP address of the email
server
For example, if the port number of the email server is 587 and the email server IP
address (10.10.10.1) is used for connection, the command is ssh -v -p 587
10.10.10.1.
If the following information is displayed, the connection is normal. Otherwise, the
connection fails. In this case, contact the administrator.
debug1: Connection established.
Step 3 If the NetEco is deployed in cluster mode, perform the preceding operations on all
the nodes where the campmessagingservice process resides.
----End

When you need to send notifications by email, you need to set the email
notification parameters and verify the settings to ensure that emails can be sent
properly.
Prerequisites
● You have the Email Server Settings permission.
● The interconnected email server supports Simple Mail Transfer Protocol
(SMTP) and the SMTP port is available.
– In common connection mode, the port 25 is recommended.
– In STARTTLS connection mode, the port 587 is recommended.
– In SSL or TLS connection mode, the port 465 is recommended.
● You have obtained the server information from the SMTP server administrator,
such as the domain name/IP address, port number, whether identity
authentication is required, username, and user password.
● A DNS server has been configured and can resolve the domain name, if the
email server address is a domain name.
● A valid email address has been registered.
● The node where the campmessagingservice process resides properly
communicates with the email server. For details, see 9.4.3.1 Checking the
Connectivity of the Email Server.
Context
● For security purposes, you are advised to retain the default value SSL/TLS for
Security protocol when configuring the email server.
● To send notifications to relevant personnel, you need to enter their personal
data, such as mobile numbers and email addresses. You are obligated to take
considerable measures, in compliance with the laws of the countries or
regions concerned and the user privacy policies of your company, to ensure
that users' personal data is fully protected.
● For security purposes, personal data such as mobile numbers and email
addresses are anonymized on the GUI and encrypted during transmission.
● If the SMTP server requires secure connections and provides the
corresponding certificate, you need to configure the email server certificate
and CRL in notifications. After the certificate is configured, periodically check
whether there are notifications service certificate alarms related to the email
server. If there is an alarm indicating that the email server certificate is about
to expire, update the certificate in time and delete the certificate that is about
to expire. If there is an alarm indicating that the email server certificate has
expired, update the certificate immediately and delete the certificate that has
expired. If the trusted CA has updated the CRL, update the email server
certificate and CRL in a timely manner.
For data security purposes, the notifications service supports certificates
signed using the SHA256withRSA, SHA384withRSA, or SHA512withRSA

Step 1 Choose System > System Settings > Notifications from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Email Server Settings. Set the domain name or IP
address of the SMTP server, and the sender email address, character encoding, and
server port, as shown in Figure 9-3 and Table 9-9.
Figure 9-3 Email Server Settings page
Table 9-9 Parameters on the Email Server Settings page
Parameter Description
SMTP server Domain name or IP address obtained from the email server
domain name/IP provider.
address
Sender email Email address allocated by the email server.
address
NOTE
The email address must be registered on the peer SMTP server and
must be complete. You are advised not to use a personal email
address.
Charset Character encoding used for communication with the SMTP
server.

Parameter Description
Security protocol If SMTP server certificate authentication (Applies when
an email server certificate for SMTP server is already
installed. TLS is recommended.) is selected, select a secure
connection protocol and configure the certificate and CRL.
For details, see 8.4.11.9 Updating the Email Server
Certificate for Notifications (the NetEco).
For security purposes, SSL/TLS is recommended.
Server port Port number of the SMTP server. For details, see NetEco
Communication Matrix.
Require identity If the SMTP email server does not require identity
authentication authentication, do not select this option. Otherwise, emails
for the SMTP will fail to be sent.
server
NOTE
If the SMTP server requires identity authentication, obtain the
username and password from the SMTP server administrator.
● The username must be the same as that contained in the value
of Sender email address. You are advised not to use a personal
username.
● If no authorization code is available for logging in to the SMTP
email server, set the password to the password of Sender email
address. Otherwise, set the password to the authorization code
for logging in to the SMTP email server.
Efficient sending ● Set it to Yes for the email server that can send multiple
emails through a single connection to improve the email
sending efficiency.
● Set it to No for the email server that can send only one
email through a single connection. Otherwise, some
emails may fail to be sent.
Settings take Determine whether to enable this parameter after testing
effect the connectivity of the email server. For details, see 9.4.3.1
Checking the Connectivity of the Email Server.
● If this parameter is enabled, an email notification will be
sent after the test is successful.
● If this parameter is disabled, the configuration is saved as
a draft and no email notification will be sent.
Step 3 Click Test to check whether the system is properly connected to the email server.
● If the button is dimmed, check whether all mandatory items are set or
configured.
● If "Test succeeded" is displayed, they are properly connected. Click Apply.
● If the test fails, rectify the fault based on the error information or contact
technical support.
----End

You can specify whether to enable the SSL or TLS protocol for an email server. If
the SSL or TLS protocol is enabled, you can configure the protocol version based
on the security protocol supported by the email server and encrypt the SSL/TLS
certificate of the email server to ensure data transmission security.
9.4.4.1 Converting the SSL/TLS Certificate Format of the Email Server
If the email server requires secure connection and provides the corresponding
certificate, you need to import the SSL/TLS certificate of the email server to the
notifications service. Before importing the certificate, you need to convert the
certificate format to ensure that the certificate can be successfully imported.
NO TE
To update the email server certificate, you can import the certificate or click Reset to
import the email server certificate.
Prerequisites
keytool is a Java runtime environment (JRE) command. Ensure that this command
is executable.
Procedure
Step 1 Obtain the SSL/TLS certificate (that is, the trust certificate with the file name
extension .cer) of the email server from the organization that issues the email
server certificate, and save the certificate to your local PC.
Step 2 Press win+R to open the Run dialog box. Enter CMD and click OK.
Step 3 Run the following command to go to the keytool directory:
cd /d keytool directory
NO TE
keytool is a JRE command. You need to download the JRE installation package of the
OpenJDK 8 (LTS) version from the official website (https://adoptopenjdk.net/
releases.html), decompress the package, and install it. Generally, keytool is located in the
JRE installation path\bin directory.
Step 4 Run the following command to convert the certificate format and encrypt the
keystore:
keytool -importcert -file path for saving the original certificate\name of the
original certificate -keystore path for saving the converted certificate\name of the
converted certificate
Enter keystore Password:
Reenter New Password:
The certificate is converted to a .keystore file. Remember the certificate password,

NO TE
You need to set the keystore password. For security purposes, it is recommended that the
keystore password meet the following complexity requirements:
● Contain 8 to 32 characters.
● Not contain the username or its reverse.
● Contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, and one digit.
● Contain at least one of special characters !"#$%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?@[\]^`{_|}~ and spaces.
----End
9.4.4.2 Changing the Email Protocol Version
By default, the NetEco server uses TLSv1.3 to communicate with the email server.
If the interconnected email server uses SSL or TLS of another version, you need to
change the SSL or TLS version of the NetEco server to be consistent with the
protocol and version of the interconnected email server, ensuring that the
notification function can be used properly.
Prerequisites
The node where the campmessagingservice process resides can communicate with
the email server, if an SMTP-based email server is used. You are advised to run the
ssh command, for example, ssh -v -p Port number of the email server Domain
name/IP address of the email server on the node where the
campmessagingservice process resides to check the network connectivity.
Context
● The notifications service supports SMTP-based email servers and transmission
channel encryption using SSL or TLS. The interconnected email servers must
support SMTP.
● TLSv1.2 and TLSv1.3 are more secure than SSLv3, TLSv1.0, and TLSv1.1.
Therefore, you are advised to change the protocol version to TLSv1.2 or later.
● A DNS server has been configured and can resolve the domain name, if the
email server address is a domain name.
Procedure
Change the email protocol version on the GUI. For details, see 8.1.2 Checking
Service Configurations.
9.4.4.3 Changing the Maximum Length of SMS Messages
The maximum length of SMS messages is related to the encoding format set on
the SMS server. When an SMS message fails to be sent, the SMS server displays
the message "Message length is invalid". You need to change the length of SMS
messages according to the encoding format set on the SMS server.
Prerequisites
You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser, ossuser, and ossadm users of

● US ASCII is a 7-bit code. When the SMS server uses this encoding format to
send SMS messages, the maximum length of an SMS message is 160
characters.
● LATIN 1 is an 8-bit code. When the SMS server uses this encoding format to
send SMS messages, the maximum length of an SMS message is 140
characters.
Procedure
Step 1 Create the smsconfig.properties configuration file on your local PC and change
the value of maxAsciiLength to 140 or 160. The file content format is as follows:
SMGP-heartbeat-interval=30
SMGP-reconnect-interval=5
SMGP-heartbeat-noresponseout=5
SMGP-transaction-timeout=10
SMGP-version=20
SMGP-debug=false
CMPP-heartbeat-interval=5
CMPP-reconnect-interval=5
CMPP-heartbeat-noresponseout=3
CMPP-read-timeout=15
CMPP-transaction-timeout=10
CMPP-version=48
CMPP-debug=false
SMPP-heartbeat-interval=30
SMPP-reconnect-interval=5
SMPP-heartbeat-noresponseout=3
SMPP-transaction-timeout=10
SMPP-read-timeout=150
SMPP-debug=false
SGIP-transaction-timeout=10
SGIP-reconnect-interval=0.2
SGIP-read-timeout=120
SGIP-version=1
SGIP-debug=false
contentMaxLength=4000
recipientListMaxLen=2000
maxAsciiLength=140
maxUcs2Length=70
CNGP-heartbeat-interval=3
CNGP-reconnect-interval=5
CNGP-heartbeat-noresponseout=3
CNGP-read-timeout=10
CNGP-transaction-timeout=10
CNGP-version=0
CNGP-debug=false
Step 2 Use FileZilla to log in to the node where the campmessagingservice process
resides and upload the smsconfig.properties file to the directory, as the sopuser
user in SFTP mode. For details, see 13.2 Transferring Files Using FileZilla.
Step 3 Use PuTTY to log in to the node where the campmessagingservice process resides,
as the sopuser user in SSH mode. For details about how to obtain the IP address
of the node where a process resides, see 12.3 Querying the Management IP

smsconfig.properties file:
chmod 640 /smsconfig.properties
Step 5 Run the following command to switch to the ossuser user:
su - ossuser
Password: password for the ossuser user
Step 6 Run the following command to copy the smsconfig.properties file to the specified
directory:
cp -P /smsconfig.properties /opt/oss/product name/apps/
CAMPMessagingService/etc/config/sms/smsconfig.properties
Step 7 Run the following commands to change the permission for the
smsconfig.properties file:
cd /opt/oss/product name/apps/CAMPMessagingService/etc/config/sms
chmod 600 smsconfig.properties
Step 8 Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
Step 9 Run the following commands to restart CAMPMessagingService:
. /opt/oss/manager/bin/engr_profile.sh
ipmc_adm -cmd restartapp -app CAMPMessagingService
If information similar to the following is displayed, CAMPMessagingService is
restarted successfully. Otherwise, contact technical support.
Starting process campmessagingservice-1-0 ... success
Step 10 Run the following commands to switch back to the sopuser user:
exit
exit
Step 11 Run the following command to delete files in the directory:
rm -rf /smsconfig.properties
----End
9.4.4.4 Setting the Owner and Permissions of the Serial Port File on the
Server Connected to the SMS Modem
When the SMS modem is used to send SMS messages, ensure that the owner and
permissions of the serial port file on the server connected to the SMS modem are

You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and root users of the node
where the campmessagingservice process resides.
Procedure
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the node where the campmessagingservice process resides,
as the sopuser user in SSH mode. For details about how to obtain the IP address
of the node where a process resides, see 12.3 Querying the Management IP
Address of the Node Where a Process or Service Resides.
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the root user:
su - root
Password: password for the root user
Step 3 Run the following command to check the serial port number of the SMS modem,
and record it:
dmesg | grep tty
[ 0.000511] console [tty0] enabled
[ 4.034951] 00:01: ttyS0 at MMIO 0x3f00002f8 (irq = 15, base_baud = 115200) is a 16550A
[ 10.832195] console [ttyS0] enabled
ttyS0 indicates the activated serial port.
Step 4 Run the following command to check the status of the serial-getty@ttyS* service:
NO TE
ttyS* indicates the serial port connected to the SMS modem, which is recorded in Step 3,
for example, ttyS0.
systemctl status serial-getty@ttyS*
Active: active (running) since Fri 2019-09-27 19:51:02 CST; 1 day 18h ago
If the service is in the running state, run the following command to stop the
serial-getty@ttyS* service. Otherwise, go to Step 5.
1. Run the following command to stop the serial-getty@ttyS* service:
systemctl mask serial-getty@ttyS*.service
2. Run the following command to restart the OS:
shutdown -r
NO TICE
Running this command will restart the OS. Exercise caution when performing
this operation.
Step 5 Run the following command to check whether the owner and permissions of the
serial port file on the server connected to the SMS modem are ossuser:ossgroup:
ls -l /dev/ttyS*

● If the file owner and permission are incorrect, run the following command to
change the owner and permission to ossuser:ossgroup, and perform this step
again to check the file owner and permission:
chown -h ossuser:ossgroup /dev/ttyS*
Step 6 Run the following command to switch back to the sopuser user:
exit
----End
9.4.4.5 Configuring the Function of Sending Notifications to the Internal IP
Address
To prevent server-side request forgery (SSRF) attacks, the SMS server and third-
party server in notifications are not allowed to send requests to the NetEco server
by default. When the SMS server or third-party server is deployed on the NetEco
server, you need to configure the internal IP address of the NetEco server to send
messages to the SMS server or third-party server.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossuser users of the
node where the campmessagingservice process resides.
● The SMS server or third-party server is deployed on the NetEco server.
Procedure
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the node where the campmessagingservice process resides,
as the sopuser user in SSH mode. For details about how to obtain the IP address
of the node where a process resides, see 12.3 Querying the Management IP
Address of the Node Where a Process or Service Resides.
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the ossuser user:
su - ossuser
Password: Password for the ossuser user
Step 3 Run the following commands to create the forbiddenClusterIp file in the service
directory:
cd /opt/oss/product name/apps/CAMPMessagingService/
touch forbiddenClusterIp
Step 4 Run the following commands to switch back to the sopuser user:
exit
----End

9.5 Configuring Network Information
On the PowerEcho, you can configure network information for each node. Ensure
that nodes can communicate with each other properly.
9.5.1 Modifying Hostnames
To identify the meaning and function of a node on the network, set a unique
hostname for each node.
Prerequisites
● The hostname has been planned and meets the hostname naming rules.
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Precautions
If a management node is faulty, restore the node to the normal state first.
Otherwise, the host name fails to be changed. For details, see Table 9-10.
Table 9-10 Troubleshooting the management node faults
No. Check Item Check Method Troubleshooting Method
1 Network Contact the Contact the administrator to
connection administrator to rectify the network fault.
check whether the
network connection
is normal.
2 Running Contact the Contact the administrator to
status of VMs administrator to rectify the fault of the VMs or
or physical check whether the physical machines.
machines VMs or physical
machines are
abnormal, for
example, whether
the VMs or physical
machines are
powered-off or
deleted.
3 Running Log in to the ● If the login page is displayed,
status of PowerEcho. the fault is rectified
PowerEcho
● If the login page is not
displayed or no response is
returned, restore PowerEcho.
For details, see 10.8 Restoring
the PowerEcho.

The hostname must meet the following requirements:
● The hostname must be unique on the network.
● The hostname contains 2 to 63 characters, and can only contain letters, digits,
and hyphens (-).
● The hostname cannot contain double hyphens (--).
● The hostname cannot contain spaces.
● The hostname must start with a letter, and cannot end with a hyphen (-).
● The hostname is case-sensitive.
● The hostname cannot be localhost or localhost.localdomain, regardless of
the letter case.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Network Configuration > Modify
Hostname from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Modify Hostname page, perform the operations by referring to Table
9-11.
Table 9-11 Modifying hostnames
Task Operation

configuration. modified hostname file to the system to modify the
hostnames of nodes in batches.
Click Import Configuration and perform operations as
prompted.
NOTE
● The file to be imported must be in .xlsx or .csv format. The size
of the file cannot exceed 2 MB.
● The requirements for the file name are as follows:
1. The file name can contain only letters, digits, hyphens (-),
and underscores (_).
2. The file name can contain a maximum of 60 characters.
Configure Directly change the hostnames of nodes in sequence on
hostnames on the the Direct Configuration page.
web client.
Click Direct Configuration and perform operations as
prompted.
----End
9.5.2 Configuring Network Interfaces
If the network interface configuration changes (for example, an equipment room

and maintain the network interface on the PowerEcho.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The network interface to be added to the PowerEcho.
Precautions
● When a network interface is deleted, its IP address is deleted, and the
network interface is removed from the PowerEcho. However, the network
interface will not be removed from the VM.
● If the product services are not in the Running state, after a network interface
is added or deleted, the product services will be automatically started. In the
Warning dialog box, if you deselect Automatically start the product
services after the configuration, the product services will not be
automatically started, and you need to manually start them. For details, see
3.3 Starting Product Services.
NO TE
If a network interface without IP address is added or deleted, the system does not
need to start product services and this Warning dialog box will not be displayed.
● If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode and some of Management0,
Management1, and Management2 are faulty, restore the faulty nodes first.
Otherwise, the network interface fails to be configured.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Network Configuration > Configure
NIC from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Configure NIC page, perform operations as prompted.
NO TE
● If you want to perform other configuration operations that need to restart product
services after configuring the network interfaces, do not select Automatically start the
product services after the configuration in the Warning dialog box. In this case, after
the configuration, the product services will not be automatically started, preventing the
product services from being restarted for several times. After the configuration,
manually start all product services. For details, see 3.3 Starting Product Services.
● If a network interface without IP address is added or deleted, the system does not need
to start product services and this Warning dialog box will not be displayed.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
After a network interface is configured, all historical backup files have become
invalid. Manually back up the application and data of the PowerEcho, the
database applications, product applications, and product data. For details, see
10.6.1 Manually Backing Up the Application and Data of the PowerEcho and

If IP address conflict occurs on the network or the overall network plan changes
(for example, an equipment room is relocated or a subnet mask is changed),
change the IP address, subnet mask, and usage accordingly.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The databases of the product to be modified are running properly. For details,
see 5.1.4 Monitoring Databases.
● If the IP address to be changed is the client login IP address of the PowerEcho,
add the route corresponding to the new IP address to the PowerEcho to
ensure that the PowerEcho can be accessed after the IP address is changed.
For details, see 9.5.4 Configuring Routes.
● In the cluster scenario, if you want to change the IP addresses in different
network segments, change the IP addresses on all nodes.
Precautions
● After you change IP address of a product node, the product services will be
automatically restarted by default. In the Warning dialog box, if you deselect
Automatically start the product services after configuring the IP
addresses, the product services will not be automatically started, and you
need to manually start them after the configuration. For details, see 3.3
Starting Product Services.
● After you change IP address of a database node, the databases and the
product services will be automatically restarted by default. In the Warning
dialog box, if you deselect Automatically start the product services after
configuring the IP addresses, the product services will not be automatically
started, and you need to manually start them after the configuration. For
details, see 3.3 Starting Product Services.
● If you select Automatically start the product services after IP address is
configured in the Warning dialog box after changing the IP address of a
product node, the product services will be automatically restarted.
● After the IP address of the management node is modified, the service status
of the service plane is not affected.
● After you change the IP address of the management node, the databases and
the PowerEcho service will be automatically restarted.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Network Configuration > Configure
IP Address from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Configure IP Address page, refer to Table 9-12 to perform the operations.

Task Operation
Import the Configure the IP address information in the exported
configuration. network interface information file, and then import the
modified network interface information file to the system to
change the IP addresses of the network interfaces in
batches.
Click Import Configuration and perform operations as
prompted.
NOTE
● The file to be imported must be in .xlsx or .csv format. The size
of the file cannot exceed 2 MB.
● The requirements for the file name are as follows:
– The file name can contain only letters, digits, hyphens (-),
and underscores (_).
– The file name can contain a maximum of 60 characters.

addresses. on the GUI.

If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, select the node to be

Add IP addresses. Add the IP addresses of network interfaces in sequence on
the GUI.
Click Add IP Address and perform operations as prompted.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
● If the new and old IP addresses belong to different network segments, delete
the routes corresponding to the old IP addresses from the PowerEcho. For
details, see 9.5.4 Configuring Routes.
● After an IP address is configured, all historical backup files have become
invalid. Manually back up the application and data of the PowerEcho, the
database applications, product applications, and product data. For details, see
10.6.1 Manually Backing Up the Application and Data of the PowerEcho
and 10.5 Backing Up Products.
● Changing the IP address of the backup server will cause failure to save files to
the backup server. Update the backup parameters accordingly. For details, see
10.4 Configuring Backup Parameters.
9.5.4 Configuring Routes
If the network configuration changes (for example, an equipment room is

IP address, the system communicates with the destination IP address using the
default route.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the destination network, subnet mask, and gateway of the
route.
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Precautions
● If the gateway address of the route and the IP address of corresponding
network interface are in different network segments, change the
corresponding IP address to make the route take effect. For details, see 9.5.3
Configuring IP Addresses.
● Do not delete the route used for connecting to the PowerEcho when deleting
a route of the PowerEcho node. Otherwise, you cannot log in to the
PowerEcho.
● If there are two routes with the same destination address, you need to
manually delete the unnecessary route on the Configure Route page.
Otherwise, the network connection to the destination address is abnormal.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance> Network Configuration > Configure
Route from the main menu.
Step 2 In the Select node and query route area, select the nodes with routes to be
configured.
Step 3 Click Query.
Step 4 In the Configure Route area, perform operations according to Table 9-13.

Task Operation
Modifying In the query result, if the network port name is displayed as -,
routes the route is the default route. You can only modify the default
route.
IP addresses of the same protocol (IPv4 or IPv6) have only one
default route.
1. Select and modify a route.
– To modify the default route, select the corresponding
records whose NIC Name is -, and configure Gateway/
Next Hop.
– To modify a non-default route, select the desired network
interface from the NIC Name drop-down list based on
the planning information, and configure Destination
Network, Subnet Mask/Prefix Length, and Gateway/
Next Hop.
2. Click Apply and perform operations as prompted.
The related task is created successfully. Click Task List to
view the task execution status. If the task execution fails,
rectify the fault based on the task details.
Adding routes If no default route is available or new routes need to be added,
perform the following operations:
● Adding a route
1. Click Add.
2. Select nodes. Select nodes with route to be added.
3. Add a route.
a. To add a default route, select Default Route and
configure Gateway/Next Hop.
b. To add a non-default route, select the desired network
interface from the NIC Name drop-down list, and
configure Destination Network, Subnet Mask/Prefix
Length, and Gateway/Next Hop.
4. Click OK and perform operations as prompted.
5. The related task is created successfully. Click Task List to
view the task execution status. If the task execution fails,
rectify the fault based on the task details.
● Adding multiple routes
1. Click Batch Export to obtain the route information.
2. Configure routes in the exported Excel template and save
it.
3. Click Batch Add, import the configured route file, and
perform operations as prompted.

Task Operation
Deleting If routes are no longer necessary, delete them to save
routes resources.
1. Select the desired routes and delete them.
– To delete default routes, select the desired routes, and
click Delete.
– To delete non-default routes, select one or more desired
routes and click Delete.
2. In the Warning dialog box, click OK.
The related task is created successfully. Click Task List to
view the task execution status. If the task execution fails,
rectify the fault based on the task details.
NOTE
Do not delete the route used for connecting to PowerEcho when
deleting a route of PowerEcho node. Otherwise, you cannot log in to
PowerEcho.
Handling If Status of a route is Temporary, the route is a temporary
temporary route. Temporary routes will be cleared after the OS is restarted
routes or the OS network service is restarted. Determine whether to
retain the temporary routes.
● If yes, convert the temporary routes to normal ones.
1. Select one or more temporary routes.
2. Click Apply to create a task and perform operations as
prompted. After the task execution is successful, the
temporary routes are converted to normal routes.
If the task execution fails, locate and rectify the fault
based on the task details.
● If no, delete the temporary routes.
1. Select one or more temporary routes.
2. Click Delete and perform operations as prompted.
The related task is created successfully. Click Task List to
view the task execution status. If the task execution fails,
locate and rectify the fault based on the task details.
----End
9.5.5 Configuring Floating IP Addresses
If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, you can configure floating IP
addresses for multiple nodes to prevent the system from failing to provide services
due to node faults. The PowerEcho allows you to configure floating IP addresses
for specified nodes of a product, and modify or delete the configured floating IP
addresses.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the

see 5.1.4 Monitoring Databases.
● The nodes for which the floating IP address is to be configured must be of the
same type. For details, see node types in 5.1.4 Monitoring Databases.
Precautions
● After you change the floating IP address, the product services will be
automatically restarted by default. In the Warning dialog box, if you deselect
Automatically start the product services after the floating IP address
configuration, the product services will not be automatically started, and you
need to manually start them after the configuration. For details, see 3.3
Starting Product Services.
● After the floating IP address of the management node is configured, the
service status of the service plane is not affected.
● After the floating IP address of the product node is configured, if you select
Automatically start the product services after the floating IP address
configuration in the Warning dialog box, the system automatically restarts
the product services.
● Modifying the floating IP addresses for interconnection will cause
interconnection exceptions between systems. After modification, you need to
reconfigure the interconnection between systems.
● If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode and Management0,
Management1, or Management2 is faulty, restore the faulty node first.
Otherwise, the floating IP address cannot be configured. For details, see 10.8
Restoring the PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance > Network Configuration > Configure Floating IP Address
from the main menu of the PowerEcho.
Step 2 On the Configure Floating IP Address page, refer to Table 9-14 to perform the
operations.
Table 9-14 Configuring floating IP addresses
Task Operation
Adding a floating On the Configure Floating IP Address page, click Add and
IP address perform operations as prompted.
Adding multiple 1. On the Configure Floating IP Address page, click Export
floating IP Template to obtain a template.
addresses
2. Configure floating IP addresses in the exported Excel
template and save the template.
3. Click Batch Add to import the configured floating IP
address file.

NO TE
● If you want to perform other configuration operations that need to restart product
services after configuring the floating IP addresses, do not select Automatically start
the product services after the floating IP address configuration in the Warning
dialog box. In this case, after the configuration, the product services will not be
automatically started, preventing the product services from being restarted for several
times. After the operation is complete, manually start all product services. For details,
see 3.3 Starting Product Services.
● If HyperHAMgr has been deployed, pay attention to the following when configuring the
floating IP address:
– If the node with floating IP address to be configured needs to be managed by
HyperHAMgr, add HAAccess to the NIC usage of the floating IP address.
– To add or change the floating IP address, add HAAccess to the NIC usage of the
floating IP address.
– If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, the initially installed management
node has two floating IP addresses whose NIC usage contains ACCESSIP and
ACCESSIP2. The floating IP address whose NIC usage contains ACCESSIP2 cannot
be deleted from the PowerEcho.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
After the floating IP address is configured, back up the applications and data, the
database applications, product applications, and product data of the PowerEcho.
For details, see 10.6.1 Manually Backing Up the Application and Data of the
PowerEcho and 10.5 Backing Up Products.

9.6 Configuring Time
If the time zone or time are different among nodes, you can configure the time
zone or time of each node on the PowerEcho.
9.6.1 Changing the Time Zone and Time
If the current time and time zone of a node are inconsistent with the local time
and time zone, an error may occur during data processing or service processing
accuracy may be reduced when you perform operations such as backup and
restoration and operation log recording. Therefore, you need to change the time
and time zone.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the local time zone and time of the node. You can visit
https://www.timeanddate.com to query the local standard time zone and
time of the node.
● Ensure that the time zone has been configured for all nodes. If the time zone
is displayed for a node in the Time Zone column on the Configure Time
Zone and Time page, the time zone has been configured for the node.
Otherwise, contact technical support.
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Context
● DST is short for daylight saving time, and is one hour ahead compared with
the standard time.
● The DST is associated with the time zone. When you set the time zone

configured time zone.
Precautions
● To prevent security risks, change the node time to a time point within the
validity period of the IR and ER certificates, and ensure that the new node
time is at least 24 hours later than the date from which the IR certificate
becomes valid. Do not change the node time in CLI mode. Otherwise,
unknown errors may occur. You can check the validity period of the server.cer
certificate in the certificate storage path.
– The IR certificates are stored in the /opt/oss/manager/etc/ssl/internal
directory.
– The ER certificates are stored in the /opt/oss/manager/etc/ssl/er
directory.
● Before the time zone and time are changed, the PowerEcho automatically
stops the product services and product databases.
● Client sessions may be automatically logged out if you change the time zone
and time.
● If an NTP server has been configured, the node time will be synchronized with
the NTP server and cannot be changed on the GUI. You are advised to keep
the time zone of nodes consistent with that of the NTP server. If no NTP
server is configured, you can change the node time on the GUI.
● If no NTP server is configured, you can change the node time on the GUI.
● If the time is adjusted sharply (by more than an hour), periodic tasks and the
data synchronization between nodes in a cluster scenario of products may be
affected. The tasks and data synchronization can be executed properly after a
period of time.
● If any PowerEcho node is faulty, restore the node first. Otherwise, the time
zone or time cannot be configured.
● If the modified time is earlier than the current time, causing the alarm
clearance time to be earlier than the alarm reporting time, the alarm cannot
be automatically cleared and therefore needs to be manually cleared.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Time Management > Configure Time
Zone and Time from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Configure Time Zone and Time page, perform the operations by referring
to Table 9-15.

Task Operation
Change the time zone When the time and time zone on the product node
and time. and the management node are inconsistent with the
local time and time zone, you need to change them
accordingly.
Click Modify and perform operations as prompted.
NOTE
● After the NTP server is configured, the date and time of
product nodes and the management node will be
automatically synchronized and cannot be modified.
● After you change the time zone or time, in the Warning
dialog box, if you have deselected Automatically start
the product databases and product services after the
modification, the product databases and product
services are not automatically started after the change,
and you need to manually start them. For details, see
3.2 Starting Product Databases and 3.3 Starting
Product Services.
Forcibly synchronize the Forcibly synchronize the time zone and time if one of
time zone and time. the following occurs:
● The time of the product node is inconsistent with
that of the management node.
● The time of the management node is inconsistent
with the NTP server time.
● New product information is added.
● An NTP server is added.
Click Forcibly Synchronize and perform operations
as prompted.
NOTE
After you forcibly synchronize the time zone and time, in
the Warning dialog box, if you have deselected
Automatically start the product databases and product
services after the forcible synchronization, the product
databases and product services are not automatically
started, and you need to manually start them. For details,
see 3.2 Starting Product Databases and 3.3 Starting
Product Services.
----End
9.6.2 Configuring NTP Servers
To ensure time consistency and accuracy among system nodes, configure an
external clock source that uses the standard NTP protocol as the NTP server. If the
IP address of the NTP server changes, modify the corresponding configurations.

● Ensure that the IP address of the NTP server to be added communicates with
the northbound IP address of the management node and the NTP server
status is normal.
NO TE
The northbound IP address refers to the IP address of the network port whose usage
includes the northbound IP address. You can view the network port usage on the
Configure IP Address page.
● You have obtained the password for the sopuser and root user on the
management node.
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● You have obtained the IP address and encryption mode of the NTP server, and
the port 123 has been enabled. If the encryption mode is NTPv4
Authentication, the digest type, key index, and key of the server are also
required.
● If the UTC time difference between the node and the NTP server is too large
(larger than 999 seconds), the node time cannot be synchronized with the
NTP server time and an unknown error may occur. To prevent this exception,
change the UTC time of the node to be consistent with the NTP server. You
are advised to keep the time zone of the node consistent with that of the NTP
server. For details, see 9.6.1 Changing the Time Zone and Time.
Context
NTP-based time synchronization operations include identifier authentication and
information integrity authentication. This ensures time information integrity and
protects servers from being attacked during time synchronization.
● A maximum of 10 NTP servers can be added on the PowerEcho. Only one
active NTP server can be configured and the active NTP server is mandatory.
● After the active NTP server is configured, the the management node will first
synchronize time from the NTP server, time on all product nodes will be
synchronized from the the management node.
● If the active NTP server fails, the PowerEcho will select an available NTP
server from the standby NTP servers within 15 minutes. The The management
node then synchronizes time with the selected NTP server. If multiple NTP
servers configured on the PowerEcho become invalid, the the management
node cannot synchronize time from the NTP servers, and the product nodes
will no longer synchronize time from the the management node.
● If you do not configure an NTP server, the the management node functions as
the clock source by default.
● If the management node synchronizes time with the NTP server slowly and
the time is inconsistent, the product node cannot automatically synchronize
time with the management node. The product node synchronizes time with
the management node only after the time of the management node is
consistent with that of the NTP server.

NO TE
If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, the preceding management node indicates
Management0 and Management1.
Precautions
● Do not set a Windows-based server as the NTP server. Otherwise, the time
may fail to be synchronized.
● The IP address of the the management node or the IP address of any product
node cannot be set to the IP address of an NTP server. Otherwise, the time of
product nodes may be incorrect.
● If multiple NTP servers are configured, ensure that the time is consistent
between the NTP servers. Otherwise, NTP services are abnormal.
● Do not set a VM as the NTP server. This prevents frequent time changes
caused by the interaction between the VM time and host time.
● Do not set the clock source in a circular manner. For example, do not set A as
the clock source of B, B as the clock source of C, and C as the clock source of
A.
● If any management node is faulty, restore the node first. Otherwise, the NTP
server cannot be configured.
Procedure
Step 1 Check whether the connection between the management node and the NTP
server is normal.
1. Use PuTTY to log in to the management node as user sopuser in SSH mode.
NO TE
If PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, that is, there are multiple management
nodes, perform the following operations on either the Management0 or
Management1 node.
2. Check whether the NTP server can communicate properly.
> su - root
Password: password for the root user
# ntpdate -q NTP server address
If the command output contains the following information, the stratum value
is 1 to 15, the offset value is not 0, and the delay value is not 0, the NTP
server can communicate properly. Otherwise, contact technical support.
server NTP server address, stratum 6, offset -0.000010, delay 0.02599
3. Run the following command to exit the root user:
# exit
Step 2 On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Time Management > Configure NTP
from the main menu.
Step 3 On the Configure NTP page, perform operations based on Table 9-16.

Task Operation
Adding NTP servers Click Add, and perform the operations
based on Table 9-17.
Reconfiguring NTP servers If an alarm of abnormal NTP service is
generated for NetEco and the possible
cause indicates that the time
synchronization relationship between
nodes is abnormal, reconfigure the
NTP server.
Click Reconfigure and perform
operations as prompted.
Table 9-17 NTP parameters
Parameter Description
NTP Server IP Address IP address of the NTP server.
Encryption Mode Set this parameter to NTPv4
Authentication or NTPv4. For security
purposes, O&M personnel of the NTP
server is advised to configure the NTP
server with more secure NTPv4
authentication mode.
● NTPv4 Authentication:
Authentication is required.
● NTPv4: Authentication is not
required.
Calculation Digest Digest algorithm type of the time
synchronization packet.
This parameter is mandatory if
Encryption Mode is set to NTPv4
Authentication. The digest algorithm
type must be consistent with that on
the NTP server.
NOTE
Setting Calculation Digest to MD5 may
pose security risks. Exercise caution when
setting this parameter. For security
purposes, you are advised to select the
more secure SHA256 protocol.

Parameter Description
Key Index Parameter used to quickly search for
the key and digest algorithm type for
authentication during the
communication with the NTP server.
This parameter is mandatory if
Encryption Mode is set to NTPv4
Authentication. The key index must
be consistent with that on the NTP
server.
NOTE
The key index is an integer from 1 to
65534, excluding 10000. By default, the
index of the PowerEcho is 10000, so the
key index cannot be 10000.
Key An important part used to generate a
digest for authentication during the
communication with the NTP server.
This parameter is mandatory if
Encryption Mode is set to NTPv4
Authentication. The key must be
consistent with that on the NTP server.
Role The active or standby status of the
NTP server.
Time Sync Status Time synchronization status between
the NTP server and the PowerEcho.
Step 4 In the NTP server list, check that the NTP server has been successfully added.
● If the time synchronization status of the NTP server is Synchronizing, the
time between the PowerEcho and the NTP server is being synchronized. Wait
for 5 to 17 minutes. After the synchronization between the PowerEcho and
the NTP server is complete, check the synchronization status of the NTP
server, and perform the following operations based on the time
synchronization status of the NTP server. For details, see ALM-51023 NTP
service abnormal.
● If the time synchronization status of the NTP server is Normal, the time
between the PowerEcho and the NTP server is successfully synchronized. Go
to Step 5.
Step 5 Forcibly synchronize the time and time zone on the PowerEcho to the product
nodes.
1. On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Time Management > Configure
Time Zone and Time from the main menu.
2. On the Configure Time Zone and Time page, click Forcibly Synchronize.

NO TE
– After the NTP server is configured, if the UTC time difference between the node
and the NTP server is greater than the threshold (999s by default), the
synchronization is abnormal. Perform forcible synchronization. After you click
Forcibly Synchronize, the management node and product nodes gradually adjust
the time until it is consistent with the NTP server time. This prevents functions with
high requirements on time accuracy, for example, the backup and restore function,
from being affected by sudden time change.
– If you want to perform other configuration operations that need to restart product
services or product databases after forcibly synchronizing the time zone and time,
do not select Automatically start the product databases and product services
after the forcible synchronization in the Warning dialog box. In this case, after
the forcible synchronization, product databases and product services will not be
automatically started, preventing the product services or product databases from
being restarted for several times. After the operation is complete, manually start all
product services and product databases. For details, see 3.1 Starting Product
Services and Databases.
Step 6 After an NTP server is added, modified, deleted, or reconfigured, the historical
backup data becomes invalid. You need to back up the following data:
● Adding, modifying, or deleting an NTP server: For details about the
applications and data of the management plane, see 10.6.1 Manually
Backing Up the Application and Data of the PowerEcho.
● Reconfiguring an NTP server: For details about the applications and data of
the management plane, see 10.6.1 Manually Backing Up the Application
and Data of the PowerEcho.
----End
Follow-up Procedure
After configuring the NTP servers, back up the following data:
Applications and data of PowerEcho. For details, see 10.6.1 Manually Backing Up
the Application and Data of the PowerEcho.
9.7 Configuring Server Alarm Receiving Parameters
After configuring server hardware alarm parameters, you can use the PowerEcho
to report server alarms to the NetEco so that the NetEco can monitor server
hardware alarms. If the cluster system is used, perform the operations in this
section on each of the each server.
Prerequisites
● The iBMC IP address and PowerEcho IP address belong to the same network
segment, and the network communication is normal.
● The communication between the PC and the iBMC remote management
system is normal.
● You have obtained the iBMC version information. For details, log in to the

Alarms generated before server alarm parameters are configured will be
automatically synchronized to the NetEco 24 hours after the configuration is
complete.
Procedure
1. Log in to the iBMC remote management system of the server and view the
iBMC version on the home page.
a. In the address box of a browser, enter https://IP address of the iBMC
maintenance network port, and press Enter. The iBMC remote
management system login page is displayed.
b. Enter the initial user name and password of the iBMC and click Log In.
On the page that is displayed, view the iBMC version information.
2. View and configure iBMC parameters.
– If the iBMC version is 3.XX, perform the following operations:
i. Check the SNMP versions supported by iBMC.
1) On the main menu, click Configuration. In the navigation pane,
choose System.
2) In the SNMP Versions area, view and record related parameters,
as shown in Figure 9-4.
Figure 9-4 SNMP Versions
ii. Set trap notification parameters.
1) On the main menu, click Alarms & SEL. In the navigation pane,

by referring to Table 9-18. To set the Current Status, Trap
Server Address, and Trap Port parameters in the Trap Server
and Message Format area, click for any row where Trap
Server Address is empty, set related parameters, and click Save.
NO TE
Use the default values for the parameters that are not listed in the
following table.

– If the iBMC version is 5.XX, 3.XX.XX.XX, or later, perform the following

Parameter Description
IP Address Indicates the iBMC IP address.
Server Type Indicates the type of the server for which an alarm is
triggered.
Username Indicates the SNMP user name set on the server.
NOTE
The SNMP user must be the same as the SNMPv3 user set
on the iBMC.
Authentication Indicates the authentication protocol set on the
Protocol server. The PowerEcho supports HMAC-SHA1, HMAC-
SHA2-256, and HMAC-SHA2-384 authentication
protocols. If the authentication algorithm is SHA,
SHA1, or HMAC-SHA on the server, set this
parameter to HMAC-SHA1 on the PowerEcho.
NOTE
HMAC-SHA1 is an insecure protocol. To ensure compatibility
with special requirements in some scenarios, you can set the
authentication protocol to HMAC-SHA1, which poses
security risks. Exercise caution when performing this
operation.
Authentication Indicates the authentication password set on the
Password server.
NOTE
That is, the password of the SNMPv3 user set on the iBMC.
Encryption Indicates the encryption protocol set on the server.
Protocol The PowerEcho supports AES128 and AES256. If the
encryption algorithm is set to AES on the server, set
this parameter to AES128 on the PowerEcho. If the
encryption algorithm is set to AES256 on the server,
set this parameter to AES256 on the PowerEcho.
Other encryption algorithms are not supported.
Encryption Indicates the encryption password set on the server.
Password
NOTE
That is, the password of the SNMPv3 user set on the iBMC.
The default value is the same as the user login password.
Device Name (Optional) Custom device name for identifying the
device in the reported server alarms.

9.8 Alarm Dump
Context
To prevent alarm data from fatiguing the database, the system processes events,
masked alarms, and historical alarms every 2 minutes according to the following
rules:
● If the database space usage reaches 80%, the system dumps data to files in
order of occurrence time and data table types (event, masked alarm, and
historical alarm).
● Dump files will be retained for a maximum of 180 days.
● If dump files exceed 1024 MB or there are more than 1000 dump files, the
earliest ones will be deleted.
Configuration Guide
Currently, dump supports only the default configuration file dumpconfig.json
in /opt/oss/envs/Product-FMWebsite/21.40.563/etc/dump. (The version and
directory levels are subject to the actual situation.)
"maxDatabaseSize": 71680,
"whenDumpPercent": 80,
"dumpPeriod": true,
"reservedDay": 90,
"saveFile": true,
"period": 2,
"periodUnit": "MIN",
"startTime": "01:00",
"fileSavePath": "alarmdump",
"fileTotalSize": 1024,
"fileSaveDay": 180,
"isCompress":1,
"fileType":"csv",
"keepFileNum":1000,
"siExportType":1
Parameter Description
dumpPeriod: specifies whether to enable the time-based dump function. The
default value is true.
fileSaveDay: specifies the maximum number of days for saving dump files in the
dump directory. The default value is 180, and the value range is 3 to 365.
fileSavePath: specifies the default path for saving dump files. The default value is
alarmdump (the name can be changed).
fileTotalSize: specifies the maximum size of files that can be saved in the dump
directory. The default value is 1024 MB, and the value range is 200 to 6144 MB.
fileType: specifies the dump file type. Currently, only CSV is supported.
isCompress: specifies whether to compress files. 0: no compression; 1: zip; 2: gzip;

The default value is 1000. The value range is 200 to 3000.
maxDatabaseSize: specifies the maximum database capacity. The default value is
71680, and the value range is 5120 to 230400.
Period: specifies the dump period. The default value is 2.
periodUnit: specifies the unit of the dump period. The default value is MIN. The
value range of Period is 2 to 527040 for MIN, 1 to 8784 for HOUR, and 1 to 366
for DAY.
reservedDay: specifies the maximum number of days for which dump files can be
retained. The default value is 90, and the value range is 1 to 180. This parameter
does not take effect currently.
saveFile: specifies whether to save the file. The default value is true. Retain the
default value.
siExportType: specifies the file dump type. This parameter is provided to maintain
consistency with the iMAP. 1: automatic; 2: manual. This parameter is not used.
startTime: specifies the execution time. The default value is 01:00.
whenDumpPercent: specifies the dump threshold. The default value is 80. The
value range is 50 to 90.
If dump is based on the database capacity, data that occupies at least 20% of the
current database usage is dumped.
A maximum of 5000 records can be saved in each file.
Procedure
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the node where the FM service is deployed, as user sopuser
in SSH mode. For details about how to obtain the IP address of the node where a
service resides, see 12.3 Querying the Management IP Address of the Node
Where a Process or Service Resides
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the ossuser user:
$ su - ossuser
Password: password for the ossuser user
Step 3 Set alarm dump parameters.
1. Run the following command to access the directory of the configuration file:
$ cd /opt/oss/envs/Product-FMWebsite/21.40.563/etc/dump
NO TE
The version and directory levels are subject to the actual situation.
2. Run the following command to open the dumpconfig.json configuration file
using the vi editor:
$ vi dumpconfig.json
The information similar to the following is displayed:
"maxDatabaseSize": 71680,

"reservedDay": 90,
"saveFile": true,
"period": 2,
"periodUnit": "MIN",
"startTime": "01:00",
"fileSavePath": "alarmdump",
"fileTotalSize": 1024,
"fileSaveDay": 180,
"isCompress":1,
"fileType":"csv",
"keepFileNum":1000,
"siExportType":1
3. Press i to enter the insertion mode.
4. Modify the alarm dump configuration file based on the information provided
in Parameter Description.
5. Press Esc to return to the vi command line mode.
6. Enter :wq, save the modification, and exit the vi editor.
7. Restart the FM service. For details, see 4.2 Stopping Product Services and 3.3
Starting Product Services.
----End

10
Backup and Restore
Backup and restore can improve system reliability by reducing the data loss of the
PowerEcho or the NetEco caused by misoperations or faults.
10.1 Overview
To improve the reliability of the PowerEcho and the NetEco, periodically back up
the application and product data. If the PowerEcho or the NetEco is faulty, you
can restore the PowerEcho or the NetEco to the state before backup by using the
backup data.
Concepts
Table 10-1 lists the concepts in backup and restore. This helps you understand the
function.
Table 10-1 Common concepts
Concept Description
Application Data that does not change in real time during
the system running, including product
applications (such as files in the /opt/oss/
NetEco directory) and database applications
(such as files in the /opt/redis directory and
the /opt/zenith directory).
Product data Data and configuration files that change in
real time during system running, such as files
in the /opt/neteco directory.
Scheduled backup The system automatically backs up all data to
the backup server at the scheduled time. Data
can be backed up once or periodically.
Manual backup Manually back up data of a time point to the
backup server.

After the backup server is configured, you can back up the data of products and
applications. The backup data is transferred to the backup server through a
transfer protocol, for example, Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP). You can back
up any type of data independently.
If the data of the PowerEcho or the NetEco is missing or damaged, you can
restore the corresponding data. Before restoring a type of data, ensure that its
lower-layer data is normal. For example, before restoring the product data, ensure
that the application data and OS data are normal.
Advantages
● Flexibility and ease of use
Function Description
Multi-dimensional Data backup of instances, nodes, and products is
backup supported.
Multi-dimensional Data restoration of instances, nodes, and products is
restoration supported.
Scheduled backup Periodic scheduled data backup of products and the
PowerEcho is supported. Manual operations are not
required.
Easy operations On the web client of the PowerEcho, the backup
and restore operations are easy with guidance
provided.
● Security and reliability
Function Description
Secure transfer of Data is transferred over SFTP, and security measures
backup data are provided to ensure data security.
Real-time task status The details of backup and restore tasks is displayed
display in real time in the task list.
● High performance
Function Description
Concurrent backup Backup tasks of different products can be executed
at the same time.
Time-specific Data can be restored to a specific time point.
restoration

The application and data of a product and the PowerEcho can be backed up and
restored. Before restoring upper-layer data, ensure that its lower-layer data is
normal. For example, before restoring the product data, ensure that the
application and OS are normal. Before restoring the application, ensure that the
OS is normal. When you restore the database application or product application,
the two types of restoration are independent from each other.
Configuration Requirements
Table 10-2 Configuration requirements
Item Description
Database type The GaussDB and Redis databases are
supported.
OS EulerOS is supported.
Disk space Calculate the backup space based on site
requirements of each product and the
PowerEcho in different network scales.
Total backup space = Sum of backup space for
all products + Backup space for the PowerEcho
Backup space for a product = Backup space for
the product data + Backup space for the
product application + Backup space for the
database applications + Backup space for the
product OS
Backup space for the PowerEcho = Backup
space for the application and data of the
PowerEcho + Backup space for the OS of the
PowerEcho
Data transfer protocol The data can be transferred over Secure File
Transfer Protocol (SFTP).
Backup Contents
Table 10-3 Backup Contents
Backup Item Backup Contents
Product Data Database data, and /opt/neteco
Product Applications /opt/oss/NetEco
Database Applications /opt/zenith(The service dynamic data file is
not included)

Backup File Storage Threshold
Backup file storage threshold indicates the maximum number of latest backup
files that can be stored. If the number of backup files exceeds the storage
threshold, the earliest backup files are automatically deleted until the number of
backup files is equal to the storage threshold.
Table 10-5 lists the default number of latest backup files that can be stored.
Table 10-5 Backup file storage thresholds
Type Number of Description
Backup Files

and must be an integer from 1 to 100.
For details, see 10.4 Configuring
Backup Parameters.
Product application 2 The storage threshold is not

Type Number of Description
Backup Files
Database application 2 The storage threshold is not
configurable.
PowerEcho 3 The storage threshold is configurable
and must be integer from 1 to 10. For
details, see 10.4 Configuring Backup
Parameters.
