10.3 Backup Server Requirements
The backup server stores backup data. You need to prepare a backup server that
meets certain requirements. A third-party server or the management node can be
used as the backup server.
Table 10-11 lists the requirements for the backup server.
● If the management node is used as the backup server:
– If the disk space usage is greater than the configured upper limit of the
backup server usage, the PowerEcho automatically clears the backup files
of product data, application data in sequence to ensure that the disk
space usage is less than the lower limit of the backup server usage.
– If the management node is damaged, the backup data stored on the
management node may also be damaged, which hinders the data

– You are advised to periodically clear the product data and application
data in sequence. Retain at least one latest backup file for each data
type.
– NAT cannot be configured between the third-party backup server and
PowerEcho.
Table 10-11 Backup server requirements
Item Requirement
SFTP requirements ● The server supports the SFTP protocol. You have
obtained the user name and password of the
backup server that uses SFTP to transfer files.
● The user who logs in to the backup server using
SFTP has read and write permissions to the SFTP
shared directory.
● The settings of the SFTP connection parameters
MaxSessions, MaxStartups, and MaxAuthTries
meet the connection requirements. It is
recommended that the parameters be set as
follows:
MaxSessions: 500
MaxStartups: 2000
MaxAuthTries: 6
● The client and server support only secure key
exchange algorithms, for example, diffie-
hellman-group-exchange-sha256, diffie-
hellman-group14-sha256, diffie-hellman-
group15-sha512, diffie-hellman-group16-
sha512, diffie-hellman-group17-sha512, diffie-
hellman-group18-sha512, ecdh-sha2-nistp256,
ecdh-sha2-nistp384, and ecdh-sha2-nistp521.
Port The default port number of the backup server is 22,
and the port number range is 1 to 65535.
Username ● The username cannot be empty.
● The username contains a maximum of 32
characters.
● The username cannot contain spaces, newline
characters, carriage return characters, tab
characters, form feeds, or special characters
<>&"',;$()`|@

Item Requirement
Password To ensure the security of backup server users, you
are advised to periodically change the password for
the backup server users. The password must meet
the following requirements:
● The password must contain 1 to 64 characters. It
is recommended that the password contain 8 to
64 characters. Plan a password based on the
server type.
● Contain at least two of the following: lowercase
letters, uppercase letters, digits, and special
characters ~@#%=^*-_+[{}]:./?!
● Not be the same as the username or the reverse
of the username.
● Ensure that the correct password is obtained.
The user may be locked if an incorrect password
is entered. Exercise caution.
Private key password To ensure the security of backup server users, you
are advised to periodically change the private key
password for the backup server users. It is
recommended that the private key password meet
the following rules:
● The passphrase must contain 1 to 64 characters.
It is recommended that the password contain 8
to 64 characters. Plan a password based on the
server type.
● Contain at least two of the following: lowercase
letters, uppercase letters, special characters ~@#
%=^*-_+[{}]:./?!, and digits.
● Be different from the user name or user name in
its inverted order.
● Ensure that the correct private key password is
obtained. Your account may be locked if an
incorrect password is entered. Enter the
password with caution.
NOTE
The private key password of the third-party backup server
must meet the following requirements:
● The passphrase can contain 1 to 64 characters.
● The password cannot start with an exclamation mark
(!) and can contain only the following characters:
lowercase letters, uppercase letters, digits, and special
characters ~@#%=^*-_+[{}]:./?!
The password set based on the preceding rules is weak
and may pose security risks. Exercise caution when setting
the password.

Item Requirement
Key file To ensure the security of backup server users, you
are advised to periodically change the key files.
The key file of the backup server must meet the
following requirements:
● The key file cannot exceed 4 KB, and only one
file can be uploaded.
● The requirements for the file name are as
follows:
– The file name can contain only letters, digits,
hyphens (-), and underscores (_).
– The file name can contain a maximum of 60
characters.
– The file name extension can be .key, .pem, or
no extension.
Backup path ● The path must be a relative path under the
default SFTP user directory and cannot start
with a slash (/). It can contain only letters, digits
from 0 to 9, hyphens (-), or underlines (_).
● The path can contain a maximum of 60
characters.
Connectivity Data can be transferred between the backup server
and all nodes over SFTP.
Back up server IP address. If PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, use the
fixed IP address instead of the floating IP address
of the backup server to configure the backup
server.
Disk space 600 GB or above
NOTE
You are advised to periodically clear the disk space.
Otherwise, the backup and restoration may take a long
time.
Bandwidth Recommended: 1.5 Gbit/s
Minimum: 1 Gbit/s
10.4 Configuring Backup Parameters
Backup files are backed up to the corresponding backup server according to the
initial backup policy. Configure backup server parameters and backup file storage
policies before backing up data on the PowerEcho.

● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● For the SFTP transfer mode, you have obtained the IP address and port
number of the backup server. If the backup server uses password
authentication, you need to obtain the username and password of a user who
can transfer files using SFTP. If the backup server uses key authentication, you
need to obtain the username, passphrase, and key files (that is, private key
files in .key or .pem format, or without file name extension) for transferring
files using SFTP. For details about how to obtain the key files, see 12.22
Obtaining the Public and Private Key Files of a Backup Server.
Context
A maximum of 10 backup servers can be added on the PowerEcho. If multiple
backup servers are configured, the same backup files are saved on all the backup
servers. If a backup server is faulty, other backup servers can still provide backup
files for data restoration.
Precautions
If the parameters of the backup server are changed, update the parameters in the
Backup Server area and back up the application and data of the PowerEcho
again. Otherwise, the PowerEcho cannot be backed up or restored. For details, see
10.6.1 Manually Backing Up the Application and Data of the PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Backup and Restore > Configuration > Configure
Backup Parameters from the main menu.
Step 2 Configure the backup parameters.
1. Configure the backup server parameters.
a. In the Backup Server area, click Add Backup Server.
b. Select password or key authentication as planned, and set the
parameters. For details, see Table 10-11.
c. Click and select the planned backup path. Alternatively, click Create in
the Select Backup Path dialog box to create a backup path and perform
operations as prompted. The backup path must be a subdirectory in the
default directory of the SFTP user.

NO TE
▪
If PowerEcho detects that the SFTP fingerprint has not been authenticated on
a node, perform the following operations as required:
○ (Recommended) Click Yes in the Confirm dialog box, and PowerEcho
automatically authenticates the SFTP fingerprint on the node.
○ Click No in the Confirm dialog box. Log in to the node and manually
authenticate the SFTP fingerprint. For details, see 13.15 Manually
Authenticating SFTP Fingerprint of a Backup Server.
▪
After the OS of the backup server is reinstalled, click to verify the
connectivity of the backup server to ensure that the backup server is
available.
▪
When the management node or a product node is used as the backup server,
if the SSH trust relationship has been configured between all nodes and the
backup server, the identity information is invalid when password
authentication is used for the backup server.
d. Click OK.
2. Perform the following operations to modify the storage thresholds for the
product data backup files as required.
In the Storage Strategy for the Product Data Backup Files area, perform
the operations.
a. Select the products for which you want to configure the backup file
storage threshold.
b. Click Modify Product Threshold.
c. In the dialog box that is displayed, configure the number of backup files
and click Save.
3. Perform the following operations to modify the storage thresholds for the
PowerEcho backup files as required.
a. In the Storage Strategy for the PowerEcho Backup Files area, configure
the number of stored backup packages.
b. Click Save.
----End
10.5 Backing Up Products
After backup parameters are configured, periodically back up product data to
ensure the reliability. If the product is abnormal due to misoperations or other
situations, you can use the backup data to restore it.
10.5.1 Backing Up Product on a Scheduled Basis
When a product is running properly, you can create scheduled tasks for backing up
its application, and data, so that the backup data is periodically saved to the
backup server. If a product becomes abnormal due to misoperations or other

● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The backup parameters have been configured. For details on how to configure
the parameters, see 10.4 Configuring Backup Parameters.
● When backing up the product data, ensure that the database instances on all
nodes are in the Running state. For details, see 5.1.4 Monitoring Databases.
● When backing up the product data, ensure that the product deployment
status of the product is Installed. For details, see 12.17 Querying the
Deployment Status of a Product
● The time is consistent among nodes. Otherwise, the scheduled task for
backing up the product data fails. For details, see 9.6.1 Changing the Time
Zone and Time.
Context
● When the product information is imported on the PowerEcho for the first
time, the PowerEcho creates a default scheduled task for backing up the
product data. After the product is installed and backup parameters are
configured, the task automatically backs up the product data at 01:00:00
every day. When the number of products, nodes or service instances is
changed due to upgrade or capacity expansion, the modified product data is
automatically backed up by the scheduled backup task after the upgrade or
capacity expansion is complete.
● The backup files of the application and product data are stored on the backup
server. For details about the backup file path, see Table 10-12. Do not delete
the backup files in this directory. Otherwise, the product cannot be restored.
Table 10-12 Backup file path
Data Path
Database application /root directory of the backup server user/
path specified in the backup parameters/
product name/static/timestamp/node
name/DB
Product application /root directory of the backup server user/
path specified in the backup parameters/
product name/static/timestamp/node
name/APP
Product data /root directory of the backup server user/
path specified in the backup parameters/
product name/dynamic
Precautions
● You are advised to stagger the start time of each scheduled backup task, for
example, at an interval of two hours. For details about the execution

tasks and device data synchronization, stagger the execution period of the
scheduled backup tasks and that of device data synchronization or service
provisioning. You are advised to stagger the report aggregation time at 03:00
a.m.
● For periodic scheduled backup, you are advised to set the backup interval to
24 hours. A long interval is not recommended, because data backup at long
intervals may result in data loss during data restoration. Specify a backup
interval as required.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Backup and Restore > Configuration > Configure
Scheduled Backup Task from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Configure Scheduled Backup Task page, Click Create.
Step 3 Perform operations as required to manually create a scheduled backup task.
● Select Product Data and create a scheduled task for backing up the product
data.
● Select Product Application and create a scheduled task for backing up the
product application.
● Select Database Application and create a scheduled task for backing up the
database application.
Step 4 In the Backup Object area, select the backup object. Refer to Table 10-13 to
perform the operations.
Table 10-13 Backing up product on a scheduled basis
Task Operation
Perform one-time backup at a Select One-Time and perform operations as
specified time point. prompted.
Perform periodic backup Select Periodic and perform operations as
within a specified period. prompted.
NO TE
● After a scheduled backup task is created successfully, you can enable or disable the task
in the Enabled State column.
● If you need to execute a task with higher priority, you can click in the Operation
column of the task whose progress is less than 90% in the task list to forcibly stop the
scheduled backup task.
----End
10.5.2 Backing Up Product Data
Before a configuration file that affects product functions is modified and before

backup data if the product is abnormal due to misoperations or other situations.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The backup parameters have been configured. For details, see 10.4
Configuring Backup Parameters.
● The database instances on all nodes are in the Running state. For details, see
5.1.4 Monitoring Databases.
● The deployment status of the product is Installed. For details, see 12.17
Querying the Deployment Status of a Product
● The time among nodes is consistent. Otherwise, the scheduled task for
backing up the product data fails. For details, see 9.6.1 Changing the Time
Zone and Time.
Context
The backup files of the product data are stored in the /root directory of the
backup server/path specified in the backup parameters/product name/dynamic/
directory on the backup server. Do not delete the backup files in this directory.
Otherwise, no backup file can be used for restoring product data, causing
restoration failures.
Precautions
● To ensure product data accuracy during backup, do not manually back up
data during service provisioning.
● If you need to execute a task with higher priority, you can forcibly stop the
tasks for backing up product data.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Backup and Restore > Data Backup > Back Up
Product Data
Step 2 On the Back Up Product Data page, select the product and perform operations as
prompted.
NO TE
If you need to execute a task with higher priority, you can click in the Operation
column of the task whose progress is less than 90% in the task list to forcibly stop the
product data backup task.
----End
10.5.3 Backing Up Product Applications
After the initial installation of the product application or before and after the
upgrade of the product application, you can back up the product application in

backup file to restore the product application to the state before the backup.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The backup parameters have been configured. For details on how to configure
the parameters, see 10.4 Configuring Backup Parameters.
Context
The backup files of the product applications are stored in the /root directory of the
backup server user/path specified in the backup parameters/product name/static/
timestamp/node name/APP directory on the backup server. Do not delete the
backup files in this directory. Otherwise, no backup file can be used for restoring
product applications, causing restoration failures.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Backup and Restore > Data Backup > Back Up
Product Application from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Back Up Product Application page, perform operations as prompted.
NO TE
If you need to execute a task with higher priority, you can click in the Operation
column of the task whose progress is less than 90% in the task list to forcibly stop the
product application backup task.
----End
10.5.4 Backing Up Database Applications
After the initial installation of the database application or before and after the
upgrade of the database application, you can back up the database application,
that is, the database data that does not change in real time when the system runs,
on the PowerEcho. If the database is not running properly because a file in the
database application is damaged or lost, but the OS of the node is running
properly, you can use the backup file to restore the database application to the
state before the backup.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The backup parameters have been configured. For details on how to configure
the parameters, see 10.4 Configuring Backup Parameters.
Context
The backup files of the database applications are stored in the /root directory of

backup files in this directory. Otherwise, no backup file can be used for restoring
database applications, causing restoration failures.
Precautions
If the management node and the product node are the same node and use the
same database software, the database applications cannot be backed up using
this function. In this case, back up the database applications by backing up the
PowerEcho. For details, see 10.6.1 Manually Backing Up the Application and
Data of the PowerEcho. Refer to 12.13 Checking Whether Management Nodes
and Product Nodes Use the Same Database Software to determine whether you
can back up database applications by backing up the PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Backup and Restore > Data Backup > Back Up
Database Application from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Back Up Database Application page, perform operations as prompted.
NO TE
If you need to execute a task with higher priority, you can click in the Operation
column of the task whose progress is less than 90% in the task list to forcibly stop the
database application backup task.
----End
10.6 Backing Up the PowerEcho
Periodically back up the application and data of the PowerEcho and OS of the
management node on the PowerEcho. If the PowerEcho is abnormal due to
exceptions or misoperations, you can use the backup files to restore the OS,
application, and data of the PowerEcho.
10.6.1 Manually Backing Up the Application and Data of the
PowerEcho
After initial installation of the PowerEcho, before and after an upgrade or patch
installation, or before major service adjustment, you are advised to manually back
up the PowerEcho. If the service is abnormal or the database is abnormal, you can
use the backup package to restore the PowerEcho to the state before the backup.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The backup parameters have been configured. For details on how to configure
the parameters, see 10.4 Configuring Backup Parameters.
● The database and service instances on the management node are in the

The backup files of the PowerEcho are stored in the /root directory of the backup
server/path specified in the backup parameters/management/management/
timestamp/node name directory on the backup server. Do not delete the backup
files in this directory. Otherwise, no backup file can be used for restoring the
PowerEcho, causing restoration failures.
Precautions
● Ensure that no database-related operations are being performed, such as
modifying IP addresses and routes. Otherwise, the backup data will be
incomplete.
● O&M data includes indicator data, data of exceptions and events, and data of
resource objects (nodes, services, and databases). Call chain data records the
calling relationships between services. The preceding two types of data are
not backed up during the backup of the PowerEcho data due to the time
sensitivity and volume of the data. However, monitoring rule settings of the
O&M data will be backed up. The PowerEcho periodically collects O&M data,
and the O&M data and call chain data that are not backed up do not affect
the monitoring of the PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Backup and Restore > Data Backup > Back Up the
Management Plane from the main menu.
Step 2 On this page, perform operations as prompted.
NO TE
If you need to execute a task with higher priority, you can click in the Operation
column of the task whose progress is less than 90% in the task list to forcibly stop the task
for backing up applications and data of the PowerEcho.
Step 3 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Task List from the main menu and view the
execution status of the task for backing up the PowerEcho.
● If Task Status is Execution Succeeded, the PowerEcho is backed up
successfully.
● If Task Status is Partially Succeeded, the backup file fails to be uploaded to
some of the configured backup servers. Contact technical support.
● If Task Status is Execution Failed, the PowerEcho fails to be backed up.
Contact technical support.
----End
10.6.2 Backing Up the PowerEcho Applications and Data on a
Scheduled Basis
In routine maintenance, you can create scheduled backup tasks for backing up the
application and data of the PowerEcho on a scheduled basis. If the PowerEcho is
unreachable due to service or database exceptions, you can use the backup
package to restore it. In this manner, no manual operation is required, reducing

● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The backup parameters have been configured. For details, see 10.4
Configuring Backup Parameters.
● The database and service instances on the PowerEcho node are in the
Running state. For details, see 5.1.4 Monitoring Databases.
Context
The backup files of the PowerEcho are stored in the /root directory of the backup
server user/path specified in the backup parameters/management/management/
timestamp/node name directory on the backup server. Do not delete the backup
files in this directory. Otherwise, the PowerEcho cannot be restored.
Precautions
● To prevent high resource usage caused by conflicts between scheduled backup
tasks and device data synchronization, stagger the execution period of the
scheduled backup tasks and that of device data synchronization or service
provisioning.
● You are advised to stagger the start time of each scheduled backup task, for
example, at an interval of two hours. For details about the execution
sequence of backup tasks, see 10.2.1 Backup Scenarios and Policies.
● For periodic scheduled backup, you are advised to set the backup interval to
24 hours. A long interval is not recommended, because data backup at long
intervals may result in data loss during data restoration. Specify a backup
interval as required.
● O&M data includes indicator data, data of exceptions and events, and data of
resource objects (nodes, services, and databases). Call chain data records the
calling relationships between services. The preceding two types of data are
not backed up during the backup of the PowerEcho data due to the time
sensitivity and volume of the data. However, monitoring rule settings of the
O&M data will be backed up. The PowerEcho periodically collects O&M data,
and the O&M data and call chain data that are not backed up do not affect
the monitoring of the PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Backup and Restore > Configuration > Configure
Scheduled Backup Task from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Configure Scheduled Backup Task page, Click Create.
Step 3 Select PowerEcho and refer to Table 10-14 to perform operations.

Task Operation
Perform one-time backup at a Select One-Time and perform operations as
specified time point. prompted.
Perform periodic backup Select Periodic and perform operations as
within a specified period. prompted.
NO TE
● After a scheduled backup task is created successfully, you can enable or disable the task
in the Enabled State column.
● If you need to execute a task with higher priority, you can click in the Operation
column of the task whose progress is less than 90% in the task list to forcibly stop the
scheduled task for backing up applications and data of the PowerEcho.
----End
10.7 Restoring Products
If a product is abnormal due to misoperations or other situations, you can use the
backup data to restore the product OS, database applications, product application,
and product data.
NO TICE
● The prerequisites and procedure for restoring the OS, product applications, and
data of a product node are complex. Read them carefully before performing
related restoration operations.
10.7.1 Restoring Database Applications
If the database is abnormal due to damages but the OS of the product node is still
running properly, restore the database application.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● There are backup files of the database application to be restored.
Precautions
● If the management node and the product node are the same node and use
the same database software, the database applications cannot be restored
using this function .
● Before the database application is restored, the PowerEcho will automatically

stopped automatically, choose Product > System Monitoring to manually
stop them. For details, see 4.1 Stopping Product Services and Databases .
● If the backup files on the backup server are manually deleted, or the files are
not displayed in the Backup File column, click Synchronize to synchronize
the information about the backup files to the PowerEcho.
● The system automatically verifies the integrity of backup files. Only
successfully verified files can be used for restoration.
● When you perform operations in this section to restore the OS, do not restore
the product data or start the services.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Backup and Restore > Data Restoration > Restore
Database Application.
Step 2 On the Restore Database Application page, select the backup server where the
files to be restored reside. If multiple backup servers are available, select the IP
address of the desired backup server from the Backup Server drop-down list.
Otherwise, skip this step.
Step 3 Select an object to be restored and select the target file in the Backup File
column. Restore the data based on Table 10-15.
Table 10-15 Restoring database applications
Task Operation
Restore database applications Select the product and perform operations as
of a product. prompted.
Step 4 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Task List from the main menu and view the
execution status of the task for restoring the database applications.
● If Task Status is Execution Succeeded, the database applications are restored
successfully.
● If Task Status is Execution Failed, the database applications fail to be
restored. Contact technical support.
Step 5 Restore the product data. For details, see 10.7.3 Restoring Product Data.
Step 6 Start the restored services. For details, see 3.3 Starting Product Services.
----End
10.7.2 Restoring Product Applications
If the product applications are abnormal due to damaged files of the product
applications or configuration files, but the OS of the product node is still running
properly, the product applications need to be restored.

● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The backup files of the product application to be restored exist on the backup
server.
Precautions
● Before the product application is restored, the PowerEcho automatically stops
services of the product to be restored.
● If the backup files on the backup server are manually deleted, or the files are
not displayed in the Backup File column, click Synchronize to update the
information about the backup files to the PowerEcho. Ensure that the
information about the backup server, including IP address, username,
password, and backup path, configured on the Configure Backup Parameters
page on the PowerEcho is consistent with the backup server where the
desired backup files reside.
● The system automatically verifies the integrity of backup files. Only
successfully verified files can be used for restoration.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Backup and Restore > Data Restoration > Restore
Product Application from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Restore Product Application page, select the backup server where the
backup files used for the restoration reside. If multiple backup servers are
available, select the IP address of the backup server from the Backup Server drop-
down list. Otherwise, skip this step.
Step 3 Select an object to be restored and select the target file in the Backup File
column. Restore the data based on Table 10-16.
Table 10-16 Restoring product applications
Task Operation
Restoring the application of a Select the product and perform operations as
product prompted.
Step 4 Restore the product data. For details, see 10.7.3 Restoring Product Data.
Step 5 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Task List from the main menu and view the
task execution status.
● If Task Status is Execution Succeeded, the product application is restored
successfully.
● If Task Status is Execution Failed, the product application fails to be
restored. Contact technical support.

If the product cannot be used when database instances are running properly but
product data is abnormal, you can restore the product data based on the
restoration scenario.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The backup files of the product data to be restored exist on the backup server.
Context
If multiple backup servers are configured, the same backup data is stored on all
the backup servers. The backup servers work in redundancy mode. During data
restoration, all the backup servers can provide backup data. By default, the system
randomly selects data from one of the backup servers. You can also specify a
backup server to provide backup data.
Precautions
● Before the product data is restored, the PowerEcho automatically stops the
services of the product to be restored.
● If the backup files on the backup server are manually deleted, or the files are
not displayed in the Backup File column, click Synchronize to update the
information about the backup files to the PowerEcho. Ensure that the
information about the backup server, including IP address, username,
password, and backup path, configured on the Configure Backup Parameters
page on the PowerEcho is consistent with the backup server where the
desired backup files reside.
● The system automatically verifies the integrity of backup files. Only files that
pass the verification can be used for restoration.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Backup and Restore > Data Restoration > Restore
Product Data from the main menu.
Step 2 On the page that is displayed, if the Backup server drop-down list is displayed
and there are multiple backup servers available, select the backup server where
the backup file for restoration resides. Otherwise, skip this step.
Step 3 Select an object to be restored and select the target file in the Backup File
column. Restore the data based on Table 10-17.

NO TE
● In the dialog box displayed when you create a product data restoration task, set the
product services to be automatically or manually started after the restoration is
complete. For details about how to start product services, see 3.3 Starting Product
Services.
● If the system uses the protection password for the data integrity verification key and the
protection password has changed, you need to use the backup files generated after the
protection password change to restore product data. Otherwise, the restoration task
fails.
Table 10-17 Restoring product data
Task Operation
Restoring the data of a Select the product and perform operations as
product prompted.
Step 4 On the PowerEcho, choose System > Task List from the main menu and view the
task execution status.
● If Task Status is Execution Succeeded, the product data is restored
successfully.
● If Task Status is Partially Succeeded, some service instances fail to be
restored. Contact technical support.
● If Task Status is Execution Failed, the product data fails to be restored.
Contact technical support.
----End
10.8 Restoring the PowerEcho
If the PowerEcho is unreachable due to service or database exceptions, perform
the operations provided in this section to restore the PowerEcho application,
database application, and product data.
Prerequisites
● The backup files exist on the backup server. Otherwise, the PowerEcho cannot
be restored.
● The OS of the management node is running properly .
● You have obtained the port number, IP address, username, and password of
the backup server, and the network communication between the backup
server and the management node to be restored is normal.
● The time is consistent among all the nodes. Otherwise, you still cannot log in
to the PowerEcho after restoring the application and data of the PowerEcho.
● You have obtained the fixed IP address and passwords for the ossadm and
sopuser users of the management node to be restored.
● You have obtained the protection password if the protection password for the

● If the protection password for the data integrity verification key is used and
then changed, you need to use the backup files generated after the protection
password change for restoration. Otherwise, the restoration task fails.
● If the upgrade of the PowerEcho needs to be rolled back, do not use the
backup files of the PowerEcho of an earlier version to restore the application
and data of the management plane.
● O&M data and call chain data are no longer backed up during the backup of
the PowerEcho. Therefore, such data is cleared after the application and data
of the PowerEcho are restored, which does not affect the PowerEcho
functions.
Procedure
Step 1 Save the backup file and its signature file (management.tar.gz and
management.tar.gz.sign) on the backup server to the /tmp directory of the
faulty management node.
1. Use PuTTY to log in to the faulty management node as the sopuser user in
SSH mode. For details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.
2. Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
3. Run the following commands to save the required files to the /tmp directory
of the faulty management node:
> cd /tmp
> sftp -P backup server port number backup server username@[backup server
IP address]
When the following information is displayed, enter the password for the
backup server user and press Enter:
Backup server username@backup server IP address's password:backup server user password
Connected to 10.10.10.10.
> get path specified in the backup parameters/management/management/
timestamp/node name/management.*
NO TE
The backup file of the PowerEcho and its signature file are stored in path specified in
the backup parameters/management/management/timestamp/node name, for
example, backup/management/management/20190729002834588/node name.
Information similar to the following is displayed. 100% indicates that the file
transfer is complete.
Fetching ... management.tar.gz.sign ... 100% ...
Run the following command to exit the backup server connected in SFTP
mode:
> exit
Step 2 Use PuTTY to log in to the faulty management node as the sopuser user in SSH
mode. For details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.
Step 3 Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user and copy the third-

Password: password for the ossadm user
$ cp /opt/oss/manager/tools/BKSigntool-tool version-OS_system
type_pkg.tar /tmp
NO TE
The restoration of the PowerEcho takes a long time, so PuTTY may be disconnected during
the restoration due to timeout. Configure PuTTY to prevent it from being disconnected. For
details, see 12.20 How Do I Prevent PuTTY from Being Disconnected upon Timeout?
Step 4 Run the following commands to change the permission on the backup files:
> chmod 640 /tmp/management.tar.gz
> chmod 640 /tmp/management.tar.gz.sign
Step 5 Run the following commands to restore the application and data of the
PowerEcho.
> bash /usr/local/osconfig/os/bin/startup.sh -pkg /tmp/management.tar.gz -
cmd restoreManagement.sh -params -role MASTER
If information similar to the following is displayed, the protection password for the
data integrity verification key is used and you need to enter the protection
password to decrypt the master key:
Please enter the protect password:
● If the following information is displayed, the PowerEcho is successfully
restored, and the database instances and the PowerEcho service are started
successfully.
Management restored successfully.
After the PowerEcho is restored, check whether the product status is normal.
For details, see 5.1.1 Monitoring Products.
– If the product status is normal, the product application does not need to
be restored. If y is entered in Step 5, the product data also needs to be
restored. For details, see 10.7.3 Restoring Product Data. If n is entered in
Step 5 but the instances are abnormal, check the product database
instance status by following the instructions provided in 5.1.4 Monitoring
Databases. Restore the product data. Then, go to Step 6. If the product
data fails to be restored, contact technical support.
– If the product status is abnormal, restore the product application and
data. For details, see 10.7.2 Restoring Product Applications. Then, go to
Step 6.
● If the following information is displayed, the PowerEcho service fails to be
started during the restoration. Contact technical support to check the statuses
of the database instances of the PowerEcho.
ERROR: Start management app service falied.
ERROR: Please check if the dbInstance status is ok, if its not ok, please recovery the dbInstance first,
and then try to start management.
ERROR: Restore management failure.
– If the statuses of the PowerEcho database instances are normal, the
PowerEcho service startup failure is not caused by exceptions in the

restore the databases first. For details, see 3.6 Starting the PowerEcho
Service.
● If information similar to the following is displayed, the PowerEcho backup file
fails to be verified. Contact technical support.
ERROR: Verify /opt/backupManagement/management.tar.gz failed.
ERROR: Restore management failure.
● If the following information is displayed, the task execution fails. Contact
technical support.
ERROR: Restore management failure.
Step 6 Run the following command to exit the ossadm user:
$ exit
Step 7 Run the following commands to delete the files uploaded to the temporary
directory:
$ cd /tmp/
$ rm -rf management.tar.gz
$ rm -rf management.tar.gz.sign
$ rm -rf BKSigntool-tool version-OS_system type_pkg.tar
----End

11
Routine Maintenance
This chapter describes routine security maintenance operations, including
maintenance and check on logs, online users, alarms, and certificate validity,
which helps maintenance personnel to perform routine security maintenance.
11.1 Daily Maintenance
This section describes daily maintenance tasks to ensure the stable system
operation.
11.1.1 Checking Logs (the PowerEcho)
The PowerEcho records three types of O&M logs: security logs, system logs, and
operation logs. You can periodically check whether exceptions are recorded in
these logs to proactively identify potential security risks and running exceptions on
the PowerEcho. You can locate and rectify faults in a timely manner based on the
information recorded in the logs, ensuring proper running and security of the
PowerEcho.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● To query logs in CLI mode, you need to obtain the passwords for the sopuser
and ossadm users of the management node.
Context
Logs are classified into security logs, system logs, and operation logs.
● Security logs record user operations that affect system security, such as
logging in to the system, changing passwords, creating users, and logging out
of the system.
● System logs record system-triggered events, such as system exceptions,
network faults, and attacks against the system. These logs help you analyze

as configuring NTP servers and modifying routes.
Procedure
● Query logs on the web page.
Step 1 Choose one of the following menus as needed:
● To query security logs, choose System > Log Management > Security Logs
from the main menu of the PowerEcho.
● To query system logs, choose System > Log Management > System Logs
from the main menu of the PowerEcho.
● To query operation logs, choose System > Log Management > Operation
Logs from the main menu of the PowerEcho.
Step 2 Click on the left of Filter Criteria and set the following filter criteria in the
displayed area:
● Select Risk from the Level drop-down list.
● Select Failed and Partially successful from the Result drop-down list.
Step 3 Click Filter and check whether any exception or fault information exists in the list.
NO TE
Click Export All or Export Selected to export the logs to your local PC for query and
analysis.
----End
● Query logs in the dump path.
NO TE
Security logs, system logs, and operation logs are stored in the database after being
generated. To prevent logs from exhausting the database space, the system
automatically dumps the logs that meet the conditions to the /opt/share/oss/
manager/MCCommonService/dump/Timestamp/Timestamp directory on the hard
disk as files. If the dump directory does not exist, logs have not been dumped.
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the management node, as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
NO TE
If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, that is, there are multiple management
nodes, log in to Management0 and Management1, respectively and perform the following
operations. For details about how to obtain the IP address of a node, see 12.5 Querying
the Management IP Address of a Node.
If the PowerEcho is deployed in single-server mode, and the management node and
product node are the same node, replace MCCommonService with UniEPLiteService. For
details about how to check that the management node and product node are the same
node, see 12.11 Checking the Deployment Mode of Nodes.
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: Password for the ossadm user
Step 3 Run the following commands to create a temporary directory and copy the log

> cp -P/opt/share/oss/manager/MCCommonService/dump/Timestamp/
Timestamp/Log file /log
NO TE
If the dump directory does not exist, logs have not been dumped.
Step 4 Run the following commands to set the owner group and permissions for the
directory and log file:
> chgrp -R sopgroup /log
> chmod 750 /log
> chmod 640 /log/*
Step 5 Use FileZilla to download the log files in .csv or zip format in the directory on the
node described in Step 1 to a directory on your local PC, as the sopuser user in
SFTP mode. For details, see 13.2 Transferring Files Using FileZilla.
Step 6 Use PuTTY to run the following command as the ossadm user to delete the
temporary directory:
> cd
> rm -rf log
Step 7 Run the following command to switch back to the sopuser user:
> exit
----End
Expected Result
● Security logs, system logs, and operation logs at the Risk level are not
displayed in the list.
● Security logs, system logs, and operation logs with the Failed, Unknown, or
Partially successful operation result are not displayed in the list.
Exception Handling
You can click the value in the Details column of the row that contains the security
log, system log, or operation log at the Risk level or with the Failed or Partially
successful operation result to locate the fault and troubleshoot it.
11.1.2 Checking Logs (the NetEco)
The system records three types of logs: security logs, system logs, and operation
logs. You can periodically check whether exceptions are recorded in these logs to
proactively identify potential security risks and running exceptions on the system.
You can locate and rectify faults in a timely manner based on the information

● You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator. For details, see
1.1 Logging In to the NetEco.
● You have the Query Security Log, Query System Log, and Query Operation
Log permissions.
● You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossuser users for
logging in to the node where SMLogLic resides.
Context
The logs are classified into security logs, system logs, and operation logs.
● Security logs record user operations that affect system security, such as
logging in to the system, changing passwords, creating users, and logging out
of the system.
● System logs record system-triggered events, such as abnormal system
running, network failures, and attacks against the system. These logs help you
analyze the system status and rectify faults.
● Operation logs record user operations that do not affect system security, such
as creating subnets and exporting current alarms.
Procedure
● Query logs on the web page.
Step 1 Choose one of the following menus as needed:
● To query security logs: Choose System > System Management > Log
Management > Security Logs from the main menu.
● To query system logs: Choose System > System Management > Log
Management > System Logs from the main menu.
● To query operation logs: Choose System > System Management > Log
Management > Operation Logs from the main menu.
Step 2 Click next to Filter Criteria, and set the following filter criteria in the displayed
area:
● Select Risk from the Level drop-down list.
● Select Failed and Partially successful from the Result drop-down list.
Step 3 Click Filter and check whether any exception or fault information exists in the list.
NO TE
You can click Export All or Export Selected to export logs to your local PC to facilitate log
viewing and analysis.
----End
● Query logs in the dump path.
Security logs, system logs, and operation logs are stored in the database after
being generated. To ensure sufficient database space, the system
automatically dumps the logs that meet the conditions to the hard disk as

SSH mode. For details about how to obtain the IP address of the node where a
service resides, see 12.3 Querying the Management IP Address of the Node
Where a Process or Service Resides.
NO TE
If the system is deployed in cluster mode, log in to all nodes where the service resides and
perform the subsequent operations on each node.
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the ossuser user:
su - ossuser
Password: password for the ossuser user
Step 3 Run the following commands to create a temporary directory and copy the log
files to the directory:
mkdir /log
cp -P /opt/share/oss/NetEco/SMLogLicService/dump/timestamp/timestamp/log
file /log
NO TE
If the dump directory does not exist, logs have not been dumped.
Step 4 Run the following commands to configure the owner groups and permissions:
chgrp -R sopgroup /log
chmod 750 /log
chmod 640 /log/*
Step 5 Use FileZilla to log in to the node where SMLogLic resides, as the sopuser user in
SFTP mode. Download the .csv or .zip log file from /log to any directory on the
local PC.
Step 6 Use PuTTY to run the following command to switch to the ossuser user:
su - ossuser
Password: password for the ossuser user
Step 7 Run the following commands to delete the temporary directory /log:
cd
rm -rf log
----End
Expected Result
● Security logs, system logs, and operation logs at the Risk level are not
displayed in the list.
● Security logs, system logs, and operation logs with the Failed, Unknown, or

You can click the value in the Details column of the row that contains the security
log, system log, or operation log at the Risk level or with the Failed or Partially
successful operation result to locate the fault and troubleshoot it.
11.1.3 Checking Whether Online Users Are Authorized (the
PowerEcho)
You can check whether the status and operations of online users on the
PowerEcho comply with the plan, ensuring system security.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > Security Management > User Management from the menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Online Users.
Step 3 Refresh the user list to view the latest user status.
Step 4 Click Monitor in the Operation column of the row that contains the user to be
queried, and view operations of this user in the User Operations area.
----End
Expected Result
● The login IP address and login time in the user list comply with the plan.
● The role to which the user is attached complies with the plan.
Exception Handling
● If an online user meets one of the following conditions, click Log Out in the
Operation column of the row that contains this user to forcibly log it out and
then modify the policies and information about it as planned:
– The login time policy does not comply with the plan.
– The client IP address policy does not comply with the plan.
– The role to which the user is attached does not comply with the plan.
● If an online user has performed operations of the Risk level, check whether
these operations comply with the plan. If they do not, modify permissions of
this user.
11.1.4 Checking Whether Online Users Are Authorized (the
NetEco)
You can check whether the status and operations of online users on the NetEco

You have logged in to the NetEco as a security administrator. For details, see 1.1
Logging In to the NetEco.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose System > System Management > User Management from the main
menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Online Users.
Step 3 Refresh the user list.
Step 4 Click Monitor in the Operation column of the row that contains the user to be
queried, and view operations of this user in the User Operations area.
----End
Expected Result
● The login IP address and login time in the user list comply with the plan.
● The role to which the user is attached complies with the plan.
Exception Handling
● If an online user meets one of the following conditions, click Log Out in the
Operation column of the row that contains this user to forcibly log it out and
then modify the policies and information about it as planned:
– The login time policy does not comply with the plan.
– The client IP address policy does not comply with the plan.
– The role to which the user is attached does not comply with the plan.
● If an online user has performed operations of the Risk level, check whether
these operations comply with the plan. If they do not, modify permissions of
this user.
11.2 Weekly Maintenance
This section describes weekly maintenance tasks to ensure the stable system
operation.

product data, product applications, database applications, and OS to ensure that
backup files can be obtained in a timely manner to restore the system when
exceptions occur.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the IP address of the backup server.
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the

Step 1 Check the backup file status of the PowerEcho, product data, product applications,
database applications, and OS. For details, see Table 11-1.

● If the backup files do not exist, go to Step 2.

11.2.2 Checking User Configuration (the PowerEcho)
You can check user configuration on the PowerEcho, including basic user
information, the role to which a user is attached, and user access control policies,
ensuring system security.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.

Step 1 Choose System > Security Management > User Management from the menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Users.
Step 3 On the Users page, click the user to be viewed. The user details page is displayed.
NO TE
To view and export user information in batches, click and choose Export All Users
from the drop-down menu. Alternatively, in the user list, select desired users, and click
and choose Export Selected Users from the drop-down menu.
Step 4 Click the Basic Information, Roles, and Access Policies tabs to check whether the
user configuration is correct.
----End
Expected Result
● Basic user information is correctly configured.
● The user is attached to the planned role. User permissions and managed
objects comply with the plan.
● User access control policies are correct. That is, the login time policy and
client IP address policy comply with the plan.
Exception Handling
If the user information configuration is different from the expected result, modify
the user information based on the latest plan.

You can check user configuration on the NetEco, including basic user information,
the role to which a user is attached, and user access control policies, ensuring
system security.

user configuration is correct.
----End
Expected Result

● The user is attached to the planned role. User permissions and managed
objects comply with the plan.
● User access control policies are correct. That is, the login time policy and
client IP address policy comply with the plan.
Exception Handling
If the user information configuration is different from the expected result, modify
the user information based on the latest plan.
11.2.4 Clearing Residual Data
After a product is upgraded, if the rollback to the source version is not required,
you are advised to clear residual data to free up the disk space.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Precautions
● After the deletion, backup files of the product data, the product database
applications, the PowerEcho applications, and the PowerEcho data will
become invalid. Therefore, the backup files before the deletion cannot be used
for restoration. Back up data in a timely manner after the deletion.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Product > Software Management > Deploy Product
Software from the main menu.
Step 2 On the Deploy Product Software page, click the product with residual data to be
cleared.
Step 3 In the upper right corner of the page, click More and choose Clear Residual Data
from the drop-down menu, and perform operations as prompted.
Step 4 After the residual data is cleared, back up the following data again:
● The application and data of the PowerEcho. For details, see 10.6.1 Manually
Backing Up the Application and Data of the PowerEcho.
● The database applications. For details, see 10.5.4 Backing Up Database
Applications.
● The product data. For details, see 10.5.2 Backing Up Product Data.

11.3 Monthly Maintenance
This section describes monthly maintenance tasks to ensure the stable system
operation.
11.3.1 Checking the Time Zone and Time
You need to periodically check that the time zone and time are correct. Otherwise,
time-related operations, such as backup, restoration, and operation log recording,
may be affected.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● You have obtained the local time zone and time of the node. You can visit
https://www.timeanddate.com to query the local standard time zone and
time of the node.
Procedure
Step 1 On the PowerEcho, choose Maintenance > Time Management > Configure Time
Zone and Time from the main menu.
Step 2 Check whether the current time zone and time of the node are consistent with the
local time zone and time of the node.
● If the time zones and time are consistent, no further action is required.
● If the time zones and time are not consistent, change the time zone and time
of the node. For details, see 9.6.1 Changing the Time Zone and Time.
----End
11.4 Quarterly Maintenance
This section describes quarterly maintenance tasks to ensure the stable system
operation.
11.4.1 Changing Passwords
For security purposes, periodically change user passwords of the PowerEcho.
During routine maintenance, change the passwords at the specified interval.
For details, see 7.1 Password Management.

12
FAQs
This chapter describes common problems and troubleshooting methods during the
use of the system.
12.1 How Do I Clear Disk Space?
Question
If the disk space is insufficient because the system is running for a long time and
the disk space is not cleared, operations such as logging in to the system and
installing software packages will fail. How do I clear the disk space?
Answer
NO TICE
Deleted files cannot be restored. Exercise caution when performing the following
operations.
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the node whose disk space is to be cleared, as the sopuser
user in SSH mode.
NO TE
When clearing the disk space of the backup server:
● Log in to the backup server as a user who has the SSH permission. If there is no such
user, contact the server administrator to grant a user the SSH permission.
● If the management node is used as the backup server, log in to the backup server as
the sopuser user.
Step 2 Run the following command to check the usage of each partition:
> df -h
In the command output, if the usage of a partition exceeds 80%, clear the space.
Filesystem Size Used Avail Use% Mounted on

tmpfs 7.8G 0 7.8G 0% /dev/shm
/dev/xvda1 1003M 50M 903M 6% /boot
/dev/xvda5 1003M 18M 935M 2% /home
/dev/xvda10 5.0G 915M 3.9G 19% /usr
/dev/xvda6 3.0G 176M 2.7G 7% /var
/dev/xvda7 5.0G 3.0G 1.7G 64% /var/log
/dev/xvda8 1003M 18M 935M 2% /var/log/audit
/dev/xvda9 1003M 18M 935M 2% /var/tmp
/dev/mapper/oss_vg-opt_vol 89G 76G 13G 85% /opt
/dev/mapper/oss_vg-optlog_vol 30G 178M 28G 1% /opt/log
Step 3 Run the following command to switch to the root user:
> su - root
Password: password for the root user
Step 4 For example, to clear the /opt directory, run the following commands to go to
the /opt directory and sort the directories in descending order of occupied space
size (unit: MB).
# cd /opt
# du -sm * |sort -rn
The following command output shows that the pub directory occupies the largest
space, that is, about 12.5 GB.
12492 pub
5887 tools
...
1 aquota.user
1 aquota.group
Step 5 Run the following commands to go to the pub directory and sort the directories in
descending order of occupied space size (unit: MB).
# su - ossadm
> cd pub
> du -sm * |sort -rn
The following command output shows that the software directory occupies the
largest space.
12492 software
557 upload
1 manager
1 backup_local
Step 6 Run the following commands to go to the software directory, find unnecessary
files that occupy large space, and record the file names:
> cd software
> du -sm * |sort -rn
Step 7 Run the following command to delete the unnecessary files:

NO TE
● xxx indicates files to be deleted.
● The deleted files cannot be restored. Exercise caution when performing this operation.
● If any command output from Step 5 to Step 7 contains "Permission denied", it indicates
that you do not have permission to perform the operation. In this case, you need to
switch to a user who has the write and execute permissions for the file.
Step 8 Run the following command to exit the root user:
> exit
# exit
Step 9 Repeat the preceding operations to clear the space of directories whose usage
exceeds 80% if any. Otherwise, skip this step.
----End
Suggestions
Check and clear the disk space periodically.
12.2 How Do I Handle the Problem of Certificate Error
or Security Alarm Displayed in the Web Browser
Symptom
● When the Google Chrome is used to log in to NetEco the system displays a
connection error message as shown in Figure 12-1.
● When the Mozilla Firefox is used to log in to NetEco, the system displays a
connection error message as shown in Figure 12-2.
Figure 12-1 A connection error prompted by the Chrome

Procedure
NO TE
Operations on the browser may vary depending on browser versions but are similar to the
examples in the following steps. You are advised to perform the operations based on actual
situations.
● Chrome browser
a. Click Advanced.

● Firefox browser
a. Click Advanced.
b. Click Accept the Risk and Continue.

12.3 Querying the Management IP Address of the Node
Where a Process or Service Resides
You need to obtain the management IP address of the node where the process or
service resides to log in to the node before performing O&M or maintenance
operations on the node.
Context
A service may correspond to one or more service instances. A service instance
contains one or more processes, and the processes may be deployed on the same
or different nodes. The febservice process and FEBS are used as examples to
describe how to query the management IP address of the node where a process or
service resides.
Procedure
Step 1 Access the PowerEcho at https://client IP address of the PowerEcho:31945.
NO TE
If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, that is, there are multiple management
nodes, log in using its floating IP address.
Step 2 On the login page, enter the username and password, and click Log In.
Step 3 Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management > Panoramic

Step 5 In the upper left corner of the Service Monitoring page, select the product or
PowerEcho.
Step 6 Search for a process or service.
● For a process, click the Processes tab, and search for febservice in the search
box. All process instances whose name contains febservice of the current
product are displayed in the table. Go to Step 9.
● For a service, click the Services tab, and search for FEBS in the search box. All
process instances whose name contains FEBS of the current product are
displayed in the table. Go to Step 7.
Step 7 Click the service instance name. The service details page is displayed.
NO TE
If there are multiple service instances, click them one by one.
Step 8 In the lower part of the page, click the Processes tab in the service instance
details area.
Step 9 Click the desired node name. On the top of the page for node details, view the IP
address, which is the management IP address of the node.
Step 10 Log in to the node. For details, see 12.8 Logging In to the OS of a Node
----End

the node name corresponding to the database instance in the database instance
list.

address, which is the management IP address of the node.
Step 8 Log in to the node. For details, see 12.8 Logging In to the OS of a Node
----End

12.7 How Do I Query the Northbound LVS VIP Address
of a Node?
Procedure
Step 1 You have logged in to the management plane. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to
the PowerEcho.
Step 2 Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management > Configure
Global Load Balancing from the main menu.
Step 3 In the VIP address list on the Configure Global Load Balancing page, the VIP
address whose NIC usage contains LVS-er-internal is the northbound LVS VIP
address.
----End
12.8 Logging In to the OS of a Node
There are two methods of logging in to the OS of a node. You can use either of
the two according to the client you use and the node network configurations.
Procedure
The operations of logging in to the OS of a node can be classified in to two types
based on the network connection between the client and the node.
● If the network connection between the client and the target node is normal:
Use PuTTY to log in to the target node as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
● If the network connection between the client and the management node is
normal, but the network connection between the client and the target node is
isolated:
a. Use PuTTY to log in to the management node as the sopuser user in SSH
mode.
NO TE
If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, that is, there are multiple
management nodes, log in using its floating IP address. If you are starting or
stopping HyperHAAgent, or updating the ER certificate, CA certificate, or IR
certificates, you are advised not to use the floating IP address of the
management node to log in. Otherwise, the SSH connection will be disconnected
if the floating IP address becomes abnormal.

c. When information similar to the following is displayed, enter the
password for the sopuser user and press Enter:
sopuser@IP address of the target node's password:
12.9 How Do I Check the Disk Usage?
Question
For the node where the backup server resides, if the available space of /opt is
insufficient, the backup task may fail. How do I check the disk space usage to
prevent the system from being affected by insufficient space?
Answer
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the backup server as the backup server user in SSH mode.
For details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.
NO TE
If the management node is used as the backup server, log in to the backup server as the
sopuser user.
Step 2 Run the following command to check the usage of each partition:
# df -h
The backup directory /opt/backup on the backup server is used as an example. In
the following command output, the /opt partition where the backup directory
resides has only 13 GB free space. Clear the space in a timely manner. For details,
see 12.1 How Do I Clear Disk Space?
Filesystem Size Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/xvda3 17G 2.5G 14G 16% /
devtmpfs 7.8G 152K 7.8G 1% /dev
tmpfs 7.8G 0 7.8G 0% /dev/shm
/dev/xvda1 1003M 50M 903M 6% /boot
/dev/xvda5 1003M 18M 935M 2% /home
/dev/xvda10 5.0G 915M 3.9G 19% /usr
/dev/xvda6 3.0G 176M 2.7G 7% /var
/dev/xvda7 5.0G 3.0G 1.7G 64% /var/log
/dev/xvda8 1003M 18M 935M 2% /var/log/audit
/dev/xvda9 1003M 18M 935M 2% /var/tmp
/dev/mapper/oss_vg-opt_vol 89G 76G 13G 85% /opt
/dev/mapper/oss_vg-optlog_vol 30G 178M 28G 1% /opt/log
----End
12.10 Checking the Deployment Mode of NetEco
You can check the number and names of nodes to determine whether the
PowerEcho or a product is deployed in single-server or cluster mode.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the

Step 1 Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management > Panoramic
Monitoring from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Node Monitoring.
Step 3 In the upper left corner of the Node Monitoring page, select the product or
PowerEcho.
Step 4 View the Node Name column.
● If there is only one node, the PowerEcho or the product is deployed in single-
server mode.
● If there are multiple nodes, the PowerEcho or the product is deployed in
cluster mode.
----End
12.11 Checking the Deployment Mode of Nodes
You can check the IP addresses of the management node and product nodes to
determine whether the management node and a product node are the same node.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 Check and record the IP address of the management node.
1. Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management >
Panoramic Monitoring from the main menu.
2. In the navigation pane, choose Node Monitoring.
3. In the upper left corner of the Node Monitoring page, select PowerEcho.
4. In the Node List area, view and record the IP address of the management
node.
NO TE
If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, check and record the IP addresses of all
management nodes.
Step 2 Check and record the IP address of the product node, and check whether it is the
same as that of the management node.
1. In the upper left corner of the Node Monitoring page, select the product.
2. In the Node List area, check whether the IP address of the product node is
the same as that of the management node recorded in Step 1.
– If yes, the management node and the product node are the same node.
– If no, the management node and the product node are different nodes.

12.12 Checking the Deployment Mode of a Database
Instance
You can check the database information of the PowerEcho or a product to
determine whether the database instance of the PowerEcho or the product is a
single instance or has a master/slave relationship.
Prerequisites
You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
Procedure
Step 1 Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management > Panoramic
Monitoring from the main menu.
Step 2 In the navigation pane, choose Middleware Monitoring.
Step 3 In the upper left corner of the Middleware Monitoring page, select the product
or PowerEcho.
Step 4 Click the Relational Databases tab page, and check the role of the database
instance.
● If Role in the row that contains the database instance is Master, the database
instance is a master instance.
● If Role in the row that contains the database instance is Slave, the database
instance is a slave instance.
● If Role in the row that contains the database instance is --, the database
instance is a single instance.
----End
12.13 Checking Whether Management Nodes and
Product Nodes Use the Same Database Software
You can check the database software used by the management node and a
product node to determine whether the two nodes use the same database
software.
Prerequisites
● You have logged in to the PowerEcho. For details, see 1.2 Logging In to the
PowerEcho.
● The management node and the product node are the same node. For details,
see 12.11 Checking the Deployment Mode of Nodes.

Step 1 Check and record the database software that the management node on the co-
deployed node use.
1. Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management >
Panoramic Monitoring from the main menu.
2. In the navigation pane, choose Middleware Monitoring.
3. In the upper left corner of the Middleware Monitoring page, select
PowerEcho.
4. Click the Relational Databases tab page, and view and record the database
type of the management node in the Database Type column.
Step 2 Check the database software that the product node on the co-deployed node use,
and check whether the product node and the management node use the same
database software.
1. Choose Maintenance > Operation and Maintenance Management >
Panoramic Monitoring from the main menu.
2. In the navigation pane, choose Middleware Monitoring.
3. In the upper left corner of the Middleware Monitoring page, select the
product.
4. Click the Relational Databases tab, and check whether the value in the
Database Type column is the same as that recorded in Step 1.
– If yes, the management node and the product node use the same
database software.
– If no, the management node and the product node use different database
software.
----End
12.14 Performing Security Hardening or Dehardening
for Internal Ports
After the server is installed, deploy a hardware firewall to reduce risks of attacks
on the server to improve security. If the hardware firewall is unavailable, you are
advised to configure the OS firewall to perform security hardening for the internal
ports of the server to ensure the security.
Prerequisites
● You have obtained the passwords for the sopuser and ossadm users of the
management node.
● The firewall functions provided by the OS have been enabled.
● Security hardening files have been imported when you import the planning
data package.
Context
After the security hardening has been performed for the product ports, you can

the security.
Procedure
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the management node as the sopuser user in SSH mode.
NO TE
If the PowerEcho is deployed in cluster mode, that is, there are multiple management
nodes, perform operations on Management0 or Management1. For details about how to
obtain the IP address of a node, see 12.5 Querying the Management IP Address of a
Node
Step 2 Run the following command to switch to the ossadm user:
> su - ossadm
Password: password for the ossadm user
Step 3 Perform security hardening or dehardening for internal ports of the server as
required.
● To perform security hardening for internal ports, run the following commands:
> cd /opt/oss/manager/agent/bin
> bash iptables_adm.sh -cmd setIPTables -product productName
If information similar to the following is displayed, the security hardening for
internal ports of productName is performed successfully:
Product productName setIPTables succeed.
● To perform security dehardening for internal ports, run the following
commands:
> cd /opt/oss/manager/agent/bin
> bash iptables_adm.sh -cmd restoreIPTables -product productName
NO TE
productName indicates the name of the product.
If information similar to the following is displayed, the security dehardening
for internal ports of productName is performed successfully:
Product productName restoreIPTables succeed.
----End
12.15 How Do I Solve the Problem of Slow Response
When Multiple Tab Pages of a Browser Are Opened?
Question
How do I solve the problem of slow response when multiple tab pages of a
browser are opened?

Limited to browser performance, when you open multiple tab pages in the same
browser for the PowerEcho, you may have a problem of slow response.
You are advised to close unnecessary tab pages and ensure no more than 10 tab
pages opened at the same time in a browser.
12.16 Querying the Node Name Corresponding to the
IP Address of the Management Node
If the backup server is normal but the PowerEcho cannot be accessed, you need to
check the name of the node corresponding to the management node.
Prerequisites
You have obtained the IP address, username, and password of the backup server.
Procedure
Step 1 Use PuTTY to log in to the backup server as the backup server user in SSH mode.
For details, see 13.1 Logging In to a Server Using PuTTY.
NO TE
If the management node is used as the backup server, log in to the backup server as the
sopuser user in SSH mode and then switch to the backup server user. If you fail to switch
to the backup server user, switch to the root user, and run the exit command after
completing the operations to exit the root user.
Step 2 Run the following commands to view the node name corresponding to the IP
address of the management node:
> cd /root directory of the backup server/path specified in the backup parameters/
management/management/timestamp
> cat backupNodeNameToIp.txt
If information similar to the following is displayed, you can view the node name
using the IP address of the management node:
{node0=10.18.16.148, node1=10.18.16.144, node2=10.18.16.146}
----End

Software from the main menu.
